nodes.summary: Test helper factory that builds a minimal learning record dict with the given promotion_status for use in parameterized lifecycle tests.

nodes.name: test_learning_record_contract_requires_promotion_metadata
nodes.summary: Asserts that validate_learning_record rejects records missing the promotion_metadata field, verifying the required-field contract.

nodes.name: test_runtime_active_filter_excludes_candidate_and_retired_records
nodes.summary: Verifies filter_runtime_active_records correctly passes DEFAULT/RUNTIME_ACTIVE/PROMOTED records and excludes CANDIDATE/RETIRED/UNKNOWN ones. Also asserts is_default_runtime_active predicate behavior.

nodes.name: test_lesson_recommendations_require_promoted_runtime_status
nodes.summary: End-to-end test verifying that lessons in CANDIDATE status are never recommended even after multiple usages, while RUNTIME_ACTIVE lessons do appear in recommendations. Exercises track_lesson_usage, get_lesson_effectiveness_stats, and get_lesson_recommendation.

nodes.name: autonomous_loop.py
nodes.summary: Main orchestration module for the autonomous improvement loop. Composes TrendDatabase, TriggerEngine, and ResearchPhase; manages ImprovementCycle CRUD in PostgreSQL; runs the research-experiment-validation pipeline in daemon or single-shot mode via CLI.

nodes.name: research_phase.py
nodes.summary: LLM-driven research phase module. Queries local Qwen3 LLM via aiohttp to analyze TrendDatabase anomalies and generate ranked OptimizationHypothesis objects. Stores hypotheses to PostgreSQL.

nodes.name: trend_database.py
nodes.summary: Core metrics data layer. Collects routing and experiment metrics from SQLite DBs, inserts into PostgreSQL timeseries, computes statistical trend analysis, refreshes trend cache, and detects anomalies. Shared dependency of all other modules in this package.

nodes.name: trigger_engine.py
nodes.summary: Event-driven trigger engine that uses the local LLM to classify TrendDatabase anomalies and decide whether to initiate an improvement cycle. Enforces per-type cooldown periods and persists TriggerEvent records to PostgreSQL.

nodes.name: ImprovementCycle
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single autonomous improvement cycle. Tracks cycle type, trigger source, research summary, experiment accept/reject counts, token and dollar costs, timing, and completion status.

nodes.name: AutonomousLoop
nodes.summary: Central orchestrator. Instantiates TrendDatabase, TriggerEngine, and ResearchPhase; persists ImprovementCycle records to PostgreSQL; drives the full research-experiment-validation pipeline; executes PRSI sync subprocess as a pre-flight step.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint supporting --daemon and --once modes. Reads PostgreSQL credentials from SOPS secret path, instantiates AutonomousLoop, runs selected mode, and prints cycle summary.

nodes.name: OptimizationHypothesis
nodes.summary: Dataclass for an LLM-generated optimization hypothesis: hypothesis type, description, evidence, experiment config, estimated impact, risk level, priority score, generating model, and generation timestamp.

nodes.name: ResearchPhase
nodes.summary: Conducts anomaly-driven research using the local LLM. Builds context summaries from TrendDatabase anomalies and trends, prompts the LLM for JSON-encoded hypotheses via call_local_llm, parses/ranks results, and persists them to PostgreSQL via store_hypotheses.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint for standalone research phase execution. Syncs metrics pipeline then runs conduct_research and prints top hypotheses.

nodes.name: MetricSnapshot
nodes.summary: Dataclass for a single metric measurement: timestamp, metric name, value, unit, service, component, and arbitrary metadata dict.

nodes.name: TrendAnalysis
nodes.summary: Dataclass summarizing trend analysis for a metric over a time window: current vs baseline values, percent change, trend direction, volatility score, and sample count.

nodes.name: TrendDatabase
nodes.summary: Core metrics data engine shared by all autonomous-improvement modules. Collects MetricSnapshots from routing and experiment SQLite DBs, bulk-inserts to PostgreSQL via execute_values, computes statistical trends (mean, stddev, change%, volatility), maintains a trend cache table, and detects anomalies using z-score/threshold logic.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint for standalone trend database execution. Syncs the full metrics pipeline and prints detected anomalies.

nodes.name: TriggerEvent
nodes.summary: Dataclass for a trigger event record: id, trigger type, source, severity, metric name, observed/threshold values, context dict, and trigger timestamp.

nodes.name: TriggerEngine
nodes.summary: Evaluates TrendDatabase anomalies by calling the local LLM to assess severity and decide whether to trigger an improvement cycle. Enforces per-anomaly-type cooldown via PostgreSQL, records TriggerEvent on fire, and returns trigger decision with LLM analysis summary.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint for standalone trigger engine execution. Syncs metrics pipeline then calls check_and_trigger, printing whether a cycle was triggered.

nodes.name: consensus_arbiter.py
nodes.summary: Implements ConsensusArbiter for multi-agent result resolution using three strategies: majority vote (embedding-based cosine similarity clustering), best-of-N (score-based selection), and LLM synthesis. Exposes a module-level singleton via init()/get_arbiter().

nodes.name: orchestration_graph_runner.py
nodes.summary: Thin re-export shim that imports and re-exposes the full orchestration_graph_runner implementation from the workflow/ subdirectory. Acts as the public entry point for the module.

nodes.name: test_multi_agent_consensus_l7.py
nodes.summary: Layer-7 tests for ConsensusArbiter covering best-of-N selection, embedding-based majority vote, and singleton lifecycle (init/get_arbiter). Uses mock embedding functions and asyncio.run for async resolution.

nodes.name: workflow/orchestration_graph_runner.py
nodes.summary: Full implementation of the DAG-based workflow orchestration engine. Validates graph structure (cycle detection), computes topological execution waves, executes nodes (agent or consensus type) via the hybrid-coordinator HTTP API, persists run state to JSON, and registers aiohttp HTTP routes for graph run submission, status retrieval, and template listing.

nodes.name: ConsensusArbiter
nodes.summary: Core consensus resolution class. Accepts a list of candidate responses from multiple agents and resolves them to a single best answer using configurable strategies: majority_vote (embedding cosine similarity), best_of_n (confidence scoring), or synthesize (LLM arbitration). Requires an async embedding function at construction time.

nodes.name: ConsensusArbiter.resolve
nodes.summary: Main async dispatch method. Routes to majority_vote for 3+ candidates, synthesize for 2 candidates with sufficient confidence, or best_of_n as the fallback. Returns a normalized consensus result dict.

nodes.name: ConsensusArbiter._synthesize
nodes.summary: LLM-based synthesis strategy. Reads CONSENSUS_SYNTHESIS_PROVIDER env var to select local vs remote LLMClient, formats candidate responses into a synthesis prompt, calls create_message, and falls back to best_of_n on parse failure.

nodes.name: ConsensusArbiter._best_of_n
nodes.summary: Selects the candidate with the highest composite score (confidence + trust_score) using the internal _score helper. Returns a result dict with strategy, winner index, and score.

nodes.name: ConsensusArbiter._majority_vote
nodes.summary: Async embedding-based majority vote. Embeds all candidate texts in parallel via asyncio.gather, computes pairwise cosine similarities with numpy, clusters by mean similarity threshold, and picks the candidate closest to cluster centroid. Falls back to best_of_n if embeddings fail.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Module-level singleton initializer. Creates a ConsensusArbiter with the provided embed_fn and stores it in the module-level _arbiter variable.

nodes.name: get_arbiter
nodes.summary: Returns the module-level ConsensusArbiter singleton, creating a default instance (no embed_fn) if init() has not been called. Safe for late binding of the embedding dependency.

nodes.name: _load_graph_runs
nodes.summary: Loads persisted graph run state from _GRAPH_RUNS_FILE JSON. Returns empty dict if file does not exist.

nodes.name: _save_graph_runs
nodes.summary: Persists graph run state dict to _GRAPH_RUNS_FILE as JSON. Creates parent directories if absent. Logs warning on write failure.

nodes.name: _load_graph_templates
nodes.summary: Loads graph templates from _GRAPH_TEMPLATES_FILE JSON. Returns empty dict if file does not exist.

nodes.name: _validate_graph
nodes.summary: Validates DAG node list: checks for duplicate IDs, missing required fields, invalid dependency references, and cycles (via nested _has_cycle DFS). Returns (valid: bool, error: str).

nodes.name: _topological_order
nodes.summary: Computes Kahn's-style topological execution waves from node dependency lists. Returns a list of sorted node ID sets, each wave containing nodes whose dependencies are all satisfied by prior waves.

nodes.name: _execute_graph_run
nodes.summary: Async executor for a full graph run. Iterates topological waves, launches all nodes in each wave concurrently via asyncio.gather(_execute_node), collects prior_outputs for downstream nodes, persists run state after each wave, and marks the run as completed or failed.

nodes.name: _execute_node
nodes.summary: Async executor for a single DAG node. Handles two node types: 'consensus' (calls ConsensusArbiter.resolve on prior_outputs) and 'agent' (POST to coordinator HTTP API). Builds prompt by injecting dependency summaries into prompt_template. Persists node state at start, completion, and on error.

nodes.name: handle_graph_run_submit
nodes.summary: aiohttp HTTP POST handler for /workflow/graph/run. Accepts inline nodes or template_id reference, validates the graph, generates a run_id, persists initial run record, launches _execute_graph_run as a background asyncio future, and returns the run_id with topology summary.

nodes.name: handle_graph_run_get
nodes.summary: aiohttp HTTP GET handler for /workflow/graph/run/{run_id}. Loads and returns current run state from JSON store, or 404 if not found.

nodes.name: handle_graph_templates
nodes.summary: aiohttp HTTP GET handler for /workflow/graph/templates. Returns a summary list of available graph templates (id, name, description, node count, node IDs).

nodes.name: register_routes
nodes.summary: Registers the three orchestration HTTP routes on the given aiohttp Application: POST /workflow/graph/run, GET /workflow/graph/run/{run_id}, GET /workflow/graph/templates.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Module initializer that sets the module-level _error_payload function used by HTTP handlers for error response formatting.

nodes.name: test_consensus_best_of_n
nodes.summary: Tests that ConsensusArbiter selects the highest-confidence candidate via best_of_n when only 2 candidates are provided (below majority_vote threshold). Verifies winner index and strategy label.

nodes.name: test_consensus_majority_vote
nodes.summary: Tests majority_vote strategy with 3 candidates and a mock embedding function that returns distinguishable vectors. Verifies strategy label and that a winner index is returned.

nodes.name: test_consensus_singleton_accepts_runtime_embedding_dependency
nodes.summary: Tests that the module-level singleton pattern (init → get_arbiter) returns the same ConsensusArbiter instance on repeated calls after initialization.

nodes.name: core/route_aliases.py
nodes.summary: Core route alias resolution module for the hybrid-coordinator. Implements RouteAliasResolver class that maps intent-based route aliases (explore, plan, implementation, reasoning, toolcalling) to switchboard profile names (default, local-tool-calling, etc.) via JSON config. Provides module-level singleton accessor and convenience resolver function.

nodes.name: RouteAliasResolver
nodes.summary: Main resolver class that loads alias-to-profile mappings from a JSON config file (or defaults), validates alias and profile names, resolves aliases case-insensitively, exposes all aliases, checks validity, returns stats, and supports hot-reload. Acts as a cached singleton via get_resolver().

nodes.name: get_resolver
nodes.summary: Module-level factory that returns a cached (module-singleton) RouteAliasResolver instance, constructing it on first call.

nodes.name: resolve_route_alias
nodes.summary: Module-level convenience function that resolves a single alias string to its target profile name using the global singleton resolver.

nodes.name: route_aliases.py
nodes.summary: Single-line re-export shim that imports and re-exposes everything from core/route_aliases.py. Provides a stable top-level package import path for backward compatibility.

nodes.name: tests/test_orchestrate_routing.py
nodes.summary: Test suite for orchestrate-layer routing logic. Covers alias-to-profile resolution for all standard aliases, payload construction for forwarded requests, routing metadata injection into context, and telemetry header stability.

nodes.name: TestOrchestrateCoreLogic
nodes.summary: Tests core alias resolution: explore/plan resolve to default, implementation/reasoning/toolcalling resolve to local-tool-calling, unknown and empty aliases fall back to default, and the convenience function resolve_route_alias works correctly.

nodes.name: TestOrchestratePayloadBuilding
nodes.summary: Tests forwarded payload structure: prompt passthrough, routing metadata in context, preservation of existing context, prefer_local default (true), generate_response default (false), limit field presence/absence, and resolved profile in return value.

nodes.name: TestOrchestrateTelemetryHeaders
nodes.summary: Tests telemetry header contract: header names are stable across refactors, route_alias header value matches input alias, profile_resolved header value matches resolve_route_alias output.

nodes.name: tests/test_route_aliases.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite for RouteAliasResolver and module-level functions. Covers default config loading, standard alias resolution, case-insensitive lookup, unknown alias fallback, validation predicates, custom JSON config, missing/invalid config handling, performance benchmarks, hot-reload, stats, singleton, and backward compatibility.

nodes.name: TestRouteAliasResolver
nodes.summary: Primary test class for RouteAliasResolver: validates default config loading, all standard alias mappings, case-insensitive resolution, unknown alias fallback, is_valid_alias/is_valid_profile predicates, get_all_aliases, custom config loading, missing config fallback, invalid JSON resilience, invalid alias target handling, performance under load, reload after config change, and get_stats output.

nodes.name: TestGlobalResolver
nodes.summary: Tests module-level singleton: get_resolver() returns the same instance on repeated calls, and resolve_route_alias convenience function resolves aliases correctly.

nodes.name: TestBackwardCompatibility
nodes.summary: Tests backward compatibility guarantees: existing profile names still resolve correctly after refactors, None and empty string inputs are handled safely, whitespace around alias names is stripped before lookup.

nodes.name: alert_engine.py
nodes.summary: Core alerting engine providing alert lifecycle management (create/acknowledge/resolve), deduplication via SHA-256 fingerprinting, WebSocket broadcast notifications, and pluggable remediation workflow dispatch. Defines Alert and AlertRule data models along with AlertSeverity/AlertStatus enums.

nodes.name: anomaly_alert_integration.py
nodes.summary: Bridge layer that converts anomaly detections from BaselineProfiler into structured alerts in AlertEngine, with severity mapping, remediation workflow selection, and an async polling loop for continuous anomaly monitoring.

nodes.name: baseline_profiler.py
nodes.summary: Statistical baseline profiler that tracks per-service/component metrics (latency, errors, throughput), detects anomalies via z-score and IQR methods, and persists baselines and anomaly records to a local SQLite database with async periodic flushing.

nodes.name: metrics_middleware.py
nodes.summary: aiohttp middleware that intercepts every HTTP request/response to record latency, error rate, and throughput metrics into BaselineProfiler, with optional alert engine wiring for real-time anomaly alerting.

nodes.name: class:AlertSeverity
nodes.summary: Enum defining alert severity levels (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, INFO) used throughout the alerting pipeline.

nodes.name: class:AlertStatus
nodes.summary: Enum defining alert lifecycle states (ACTIVE, ACKNOWLEDGED, RESOLVED, SUPPRESSED) tracked by AlertEngine.

nodes.name: class:Alert
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single alert instance with fields for identity, severity, timestamps, occurrence tracking, and optional remediation workflow reference; provides to_dict serialization.

nodes.name: class:AlertRule
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing an alert rule loaded from YAML config, carrying condition expression, threshold, severity, duration, and auto-remediation settings.

nodes.name: class:AlertEngine
nodes.summary: Central alert processing engine managing active alerts, deduplication via fingerprint cache, multi-channel notification routing (WebSocket + pluggable handlers), and remediation workflow dispatch with auto-resolve support.

nodes.name: class:AnomalyAlertIntegration
nodes.summary: Bridges anomaly detections from BaselineProfiler to AlertEngine by mapping anomaly severity scores to AlertSeverity levels, formatting descriptive alert messages, and running an async polling loop to process newly detected anomalies.

nodes.name: function:record_metric_with_alert
nodes.summary: Convenience function that records a metric value into BaselineProfiler and immediately triggers alert creation for any resulting anomalies via a transient AnomalyAlertIntegration instance.

nodes.name: class:MetricType
nodes.summary: Enum enumerating supported metric categories: LATENCY, ERROR_RATE, THROUGHPUT, MEMORY, CPU, CUSTOM.

nodes.name: class:AnomalyMethod
nodes.summary: Enum enumerating anomaly detection algorithms: Z_SCORE, IQR, PERCENTILE.

nodes.name: class:MetricBaseline
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding a rolling statistical baseline (mean, stddev, median, Q1/Q3, IQR, p95, p99) for a specific service/component/metric combination, with an update_statistics method that recalculates all stats from the samples deque.

nodes.name: class:Anomaly
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing a detected anomaly event with observed vs. expected value, deviation magnitude, detection method used, severity score, and reference to the baseline snapshot.

nodes.name: class:BaselineProfiler
nodes.summary: Main profiler that ingests metric observations, maintains per-key rolling baselines, detects anomalies using z-score and IQR methods, persists baselines and anomaly records to SQLite, and runs async background tasks for periodic persistence.

nodes.name: function:get_profiler
nodes.summary: Module-level singleton factory that instantiates and returns a shared BaselineProfiler instance.

nodes.name: class:MetricsMiddleware
nodes.summary: aiohttp middleware class that wraps request handlers to automatically record request latency, error count, and throughput into BaselineProfiler, extracting component name from the URL path for per-endpoint baseline tracking.

nodes.name: function:create_metrics_middleware
nodes.summary: Factory function that constructs and returns a configured MetricsMiddleware instance bound to a named service, profiler, and optional alert engine.

nodes.name: dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Core AI task dispatch module. Implements multiple runner strategies (DirectRunner, HybridRunner, RalphRunner, AgentRunner), prompt classification, grounding injection, training event emission, code block validation, and a full CLI interface. Orchestrates all local LLM inference for the harness.

nodes.name: slot_scheduler.py
nodes.summary: Lightweight inference slot scheduler. Polls the llama.cpp /slots endpoint and blocks until an idle slot is available before dispatching a request.

nodes.name: task_config.py
nodes.summary: TaskConfig dataclass and role normalization helpers. Encapsulates dispatch mode, role, token budget, timeouts, and service URLs resolved from CLI args and environment variables.

nodes.name: task_registry.py
nodes.summary: Persistent file-based task registry with fcntl locking for multi-process safety. Tracks dispatched tasks, status updates, token usage, in-flight locks, heartbeats, and integrates with PENDING.json and HANDOFF.md.

nodes.name: _scale_timeout
nodes.summary: Computes effective request timeout by scaling against max_tokens, capped by an explicit override. Prevents under-timeout on large completions.

nodes.name: _write_progress
nodes.summary: Atomically writes a JSON progress file (tokens out, elapsed, tok/s, ETA, status) using a tmp+rename pattern to avoid partial reads by watchers.

nodes.name: _emit_training_event
nodes.summary: Emits a JSONL training event record (query, response, token counts, role, timestamp) to the training dataset directory for the ML learning loop.

nodes.name: classify_tokens
nodes.summary: Classifies the token budget tier (small/medium/large/xlarge) from prompt content using keyword heuristics. Drives max_tokens selection in dispatch.

nodes.name: classify_mode
nodes.summary: Determines the dispatch mode (direct/hybrid/agent/ralph) from prompt keywords. Local-first routing: defaults to direct for most tasks.

nodes.name: classify_task_type
nodes.summary: Classifies prompt into task type category (code/analysis/plan/review/general) using keyword matching. Used to select tool manifests and adjust runner behavior.

nodes.name: _load_grounding
nodes.summary: Reads and returns the grounding text from the repo grounding file. Returns empty string if file absent.

nodes.name: _prepend_grounding
nodes.summary: Injects grounding text as a system message into the messages list before the first user turn. Used by DirectRunner to ground all llama.cpp requests.

nodes.name: _augment_prompt_with_grounding
nodes.summary: Appends grounding context to a plain prompt string. Used by AgentRunner which takes a string prompt rather than a messages list.

nodes.name: DirectRunner
nodes.summary: Runs a prompt directly against llama.cpp via streaming SSE HTTP. Waits for an inference slot, prepends grounding, streams the response, writes progress, and emits a training event on completion.

nodes.name: HybridRunner
nodes.summary: Routes the prompt through the hybrid-coordinator HTTP endpoint. Posts a JSON body with role, mode, and limit; writes response or error to the output file.

nodes.name: RalphRunner
nodes.summary: Sends prompt to the Ralph (RAG/Qdrant-backed) endpoint. Returns the retrieved answer with source metadata written to the output file.

nodes.name: _compute_agent_wall_clock
nodes.summary: Computes wall-clock timeout for AgentRunner subprocess calls, scaling by max_calls and capping at a configurable maximum to prevent runaway agent loops.

nodes.name: AgentRunner
nodes.summary: Invokes the aq-agent-loop subprocess for agentic (multi-tool) tasks. Augments the prompt with grounding, builds CLI args, runs subprocess with wall-clock timeout, tails the progress file for output.

nodes.name: _service_ok
nodes.summary: Health-checks a service URL (HTTP GET) to verify it is reachable before dispatch. Returns bool; used to gate runner selection.

nodes.name: _detect_code_lang
nodes.summary: Heuristically detects programming language (python/nix/bash) from the first 400 chars of the prompt. Drives embedded-assist prefetch language selection.

nodes.name: _embedded_assist_prefetch
nodes.summary: Prefetches code-assist suggestions from the switchboard before the main dispatch completes. Uses SSE streaming to a switchboard code-assist profile; result enriches the final output.

nodes.name: _validate_code_blocks
nodes.summary: Extracts fenced code blocks from LLM output text and lints/type-checks each one (python: py_compile; nix: nix-instantiate; bash: bash -n). Returns a validation report string.

nodes.name: dispatch_task
nodes.summary: Top-level dispatch orchestrator. Selects runner based on config.mode, optionally prefetches assist suggestions, runs the inference, validates code blocks in output, records token usage, and updates task registry status.

nodes.name: _build_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse CLI parser with subcommands: dispatch (run a task), list, status, check, cancel, kill-all, watch.

nodes.name: _cmd_watch
nodes.summary: CLI watch command implementation. Tails the output file, displays real-time progress (tok/s, ETA) from the progress JSON file, and exits when the task completes or times out.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point. Parses arguments, auto-classifies mode/tokens/task-type when not specified, resolves TaskConfig, registers the task, and invokes dispatch_task. Also handles list/status/check/cancel/watch subcommands.

nodes.name: wait_for_slot
nodes.summary: Polls the llama.cpp /slots endpoint in a loop until at least one slot reports state=idle. Respects a timeout. Called by DirectRunner before submitting an inference request.

nodes.name: normalize_role
nodes.summary: Maps role alias strings (e.g. 'impl', 'reviewer') to canonical role names (implementer, reviewer, orchestrator, architect) via _ROLE_ALIASES dict.

nodes.name: TaskConfig
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass encapsulating all dispatch parameters: mode, role, max_tokens, timeout_secs, llama_url, hybrid_url, ralph_url, task_type, tool_manifest. Constructed via from_args() classmethod which validates and resolves from CLI args and env vars.

nodes.name: _now
nodes.summary: Returns current UTC time as an ISO 8601 string (Z-suffix). Shared timestamp helper used throughout TaskRegistry.

nodes.name: TaskRegistry
nodes.summary: File-backed multi-process-safe task registry using fcntl advisory locking. Maintains a JSONL registry file for task metadata, a PENDING.json for in-flight lock tracking and heartbeats, and appends dispatch/completion entries to HANDOFF.md. Provides CLI-facing cmd_* methods for list/status/check/cancel/kill-all.

nodes.name: inventory.py
nodes.summary: Core PPTX shape/text inventory extraction library. Provides data model classes (ShapeData, ParagraphData, ShapeWithPosition) and utility functions for extracting, validating, and analyzing text shapes from PowerPoint files. Detects text frame overflow using PIL-based text measurement, bullet formatting issues, and shape overlaps. The primary dependency for replace.py and thumbnail.py.

nodes.name: replace.py
nodes.summary: PPTX text replacement tool. Reads replacement mappings from a JSON file, validates them against the shape inventory, applies paragraph/font/bullet properties to matched text frames, then verifies no new overflow was introduced by re-running inventory on a temporary copy. Imports extract_text_inventory from inventory.py.

nodes.name: thumbnail.py
nodes.summary: PPTX thumbnail grid generator. Converts slides to images via LibreOffice (soffice) PDF export and poppler (pdftoppm) rasterization, handles hidden slides with placeholder images, and composites slide images into a labeled grid with optional placeholder region overlay rectangles. Imports extract_text_inventory from inventory.py for placeholder region detection.

nodes.name: ShapeWithPosition
nodes.summary: Lightweight dataclass-style container pairing a python-pptx shape with its absolute (x, y) position in inches after accumulating group-shape offsets. Used by collect_shapes_with_absolute_positions and sort_shapes_by_position.

nodes.name: ParagraphData
nodes.summary: Data model for a single paragraph extracted from a PPTX text frame. Captures text content, alignment, indentation level, space-before/after, bullet character, font name/size/color/bold/italic flags, and line-spacing. Serializes to dict via to_dict(). Instantiated by ShapeData.paragraphs and _estimate_frame_overflow.

nodes.name: ShapeData
nodes.summary: Central analysis class for a single PPTX text-frame shape (473 lines, 15 methods). Captures position/dimensions in inches and pixels, placeholder type, default font size from slide master/layout, and runs three issue-detection passes: _estimate_frame_overflow (PIL-based text measurement), _calculate_slide_overflow (bounds check against slide dimensions), and _detect_bullet_issues (Unicode/manual bullet detection). Serializes full shape data to dict.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for inventory.py. Parses argparse arguments (pptx path, output path, --issues-only flag), validates input file, calls extract_text_inventory, saves JSON via save_inventory, and prints summary statistics including shape count and issue count.

nodes.name: is_valid_shape
nodes.summary: Predicate that filters shapes suitable for text inventory. Accepts shapes with a non-empty text_frame, rejects picture/media placeholders and shapes whose text is purely numeric (slide-number placeholders).

nodes.name: collect_shapes_with_absolute_positions
nodes.summary: Recursively traverses a shape (including grouped shapes) and accumulates absolute (x, y) offsets. Returns a list of ShapeWithPosition objects for every leaf shape that passes is_valid_shape. Handles python-pptx group shapes by recursing into their children.

nodes.name: sort_shapes_by_position
nodes.summary: Sorts ShapeWithPosition objects into reading order (top-to-bottom, left-to-right). Groups shapes into horizontal rows based on vertical proximity (tolerance-based), sorts each row by left position, then combines rows. Returns a flat ordered list.

nodes.name: calculate_overlap
nodes.summary: Calculates the overlap area between two axis-aligned rectangles (rect1, rect2) given as (left, top, width, height) tuples. Returns overlap area in square inches, or 0 if within tolerance. Used by detect_overlaps.

nodes.name: detect_overlaps
nodes.summary: O(n²) pairwise overlap scan over a list of ShapeData objects. Calls calculate_overlap for each pair and accumulates a list of overlap records (slide_key, shape_key pairs with overlap area) for shapes that overlap beyond threshold.

nodes.name: extract_text_inventory
nodes.summary: Main extraction pipeline. Opens a PPTX via python-pptx, iterates slides, calls collect_shapes_with_absolute_positions for each slide, constructs ShapeData per shape, optionally filters to issue-only shapes, runs detect_overlaps, and returns a nested dict keyed by slide_N -> shape_K -> ShapeData. The primary public API consumed by replace.py, thumbnail.py, and get_inventory_as_dict.

nodes.name: get_inventory_as_dict
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper around extract_text_inventory that serializes the result to a plain nested dict (calls ShapeData.to_dict() on each shape). Returns a JSON-serializable representation of the full inventory.

nodes.name: save_inventory
nodes.summary: Serializes inventory (nested ShapeData dict) to a JSON file at output_path by calling to_dict() on each ShapeData. Used by the CLI main function.

nodes.name: clear_paragraph_bullets
nodes.summary: Removes all bullet-related XML child elements (buChar, buAutoNum, buClr, buSzPct) from a paragraph's pPr element via direct OOXML manipulation. Called before re-applying paragraph properties to ensure a clean slate.

nodes.name: apply_paragraph_properties
nodes.summary: Applies a full paragraph property set from a ParagraphData dict to a python-pptx paragraph object. Handles alignment, indentation, space-before/after, bullet character (via OxmlElement buChar), bullet size, and font properties. Delegates font application to apply_font_properties. Clears existing bullets first via clear_paragraph_bullets.

nodes.name: apply_font_properties
nodes.summary: Applies font size, bold, italic, color (hex RGB), and underline properties to a python-pptx run from a para_data dict. Parses hex color string to RGBColor via int conversion.

nodes.name: detect_frame_overflow
nodes.summary: Scans an inventory dict for shapes with frame_overflow or slide_overflow flags set to True and returns a nested dict of overflow metadata keyed by slide_key -> shape_key. Used to capture pre- and post-replacement overflow state.

nodes.name: validate_replacements
nodes.summary: Validates a replacements dict against the shape inventory. Checks for invalid slide keys, unknown shape keys, and warns on unused shapes that already had content. Returns a list of error strings and a list of unused-but-content warnings.

nodes.name: check_duplicate_keys
nodes.summary: Raises ValueError if any key appears more than once in a list of (key, value) pairs. Guards against duplicate shape keys in the JSON replacements file.

nodes.name: apply_replacements
nodes.summary: Main replacement pipeline (139 lines). Loads the PPTX, extracts initial inventory, detects pre-replacement overflow, loads JSON replacements, validates them, iterates slides/shapes, clears text frames, re-populates paragraphs by calling apply_paragraph_properties for each para_data entry, then saves to a temp file, re-runs inventory/overflow detection, compares before/after overflow, raises ValueError on newly introduced overflow. Saves final file on success.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for replace.py. Validates argc (3 args: pptx, json, output), checks input file existence, and calls apply_replacements.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for thumbnail.py. Parses argparse arguments (pptx path, output path, --cols, --width, --dpi), validates input, calls get_placeholder_regions, convert_to_images, and create_grids. Prints summary of grid files generated.

nodes.name: create_hidden_slide_placeholder
nodes.summary: Creates a gray PIL Image with diagonal cross lines to represent a hidden/skipped slide in the thumbnail grid. Returns the PIL Image object.

nodes.name: get_placeholder_regions
nodes.summary: Uses extract_text_inventory from inventory.py to extract shape positions, then builds a list of placeholder region dicts (slide_num, shape_key, left/top/width/height fractions) for overlay rendering in create_grid.

nodes.name: convert_to_images
nodes.summary: Two-stage conversion pipeline: (1) runs LibreOffice (soffice) to export PPTX to PDF in a temp directory, (2) runs poppler pdftoppm to rasterize each PDF page to PNG at the given DPI. Handles hidden slides by substituting placeholder images. Returns list of (slide_num, image_path) tuples.

nodes.name: create_grids
nodes.summary: Partitions image list into pages of grid cells, calls create_grid for each page, saves output images to disk with sequential filenames (e.g. output-1.png, output-2.png). Returns list of saved grid file paths.

nodes.name: create_grid
nodes.summary: Composites a set of slide images into a single grid image (125 lines). Calculates cell dimensions from target width and col count, draws slide number labels, optionally overlays semi-transparent colored rectangles for placeholder regions on each slide cell, resizes/pastes each slide image into the grid canvas. Returns the composited PIL Image.

nodes.name: inventory.py
nodes.summary: Core PPTX shape/text inventory extraction engine. Parses python-pptx presentations to produce a structured inventory of all text frames, their paragraph formatting, font metrics, overflow state, and slide-relative positions. Exposes ShapeData (full shape analyser with text-wrap and overflow detection), ParagraphData (paragraph metadata), ShapeWithPosition (positional wrapper), and utility functions for collecting, sorting, deduplicating and serialising shape data.

nodes.name: replace.py
nodes.summary: Applies structured text replacements to PPTX slides. Loads a JSON replacements map, validates it against the current inventory, writes new text into each target text frame while preserving paragraph and font properties, then verifies that no new overflow was introduced. Depends on inventory.py for shape discovery and overflow checking.

nodes.name: thumbnail.py
nodes.summary: Generates thumbnail image grids from PPTX slides. Converts each slide to an image via LibreOffice + pdftoppm, handles hidden slides with placeholder images, and composes tiled grid sheets with slide-number labels and placeholder-region overlays. Uses inventory.py to locate placeholder shapes for overlay annotation.

nodes.name: ShapeWithPosition
nodes.summary: Lightweight dataclass that pairs a python-pptx shape object with its computed absolute left/top coordinates (in EMU), enabling correct positioning after unwrapping group shapes.

nodes.name: ParagraphData
nodes.summary: Captures all formatting metadata for a single paragraph: text content, alignment, indentation, bullet type/character, font name, size, bold/italic/underline/color flags, and spacing values. Serialises to a dict for JSON output.

nodes.name: ShapeData
nodes.summary: Full text-frame analyser for a single PPTX shape. Measures usable frame dimensions, estimates per-paragraph line wrapping with Pillow font metrics, detects frame overflow and slide-boundary overflow, flags bullet formatting issues, and serialises the full analysis to a dict. Central data structure consumed by replace.py and thumbnail.py.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for inventory.py. Parses arguments (pptx-path, output-path, --issues-only), validates input, calls extract_text_inventory, saves JSON output, and prints a summary of shapes and issues found.

nodes.name: extract_text_inventory
nodes.summary: Main extraction loop. Opens a PPTX file, iterates slides, collects all text shapes with absolute positions via collect_shapes_with_absolute_positions, constructs a ShapeData for each, sorts by reading order, detects inter-shape overlaps, and returns a nested dict keyed by slide/shape index.

nodes.name: get_inventory_as_dict
nodes.summary: Public API wrapper: calls extract_text_inventory and converts ShapeData objects to plain dicts for downstream JSON consumption.

nodes.name: save_inventory
nodes.summary: Serialises the inventory dict (converting ShapeData objects to dicts) and writes JSON to the specified output path.

nodes.name: is_valid_shape
nodes.summary: Predicate that accepts a shape only if it has a non-empty text frame and is not a numeric-only placeholder (e.g. slide-number placeholders are excluded).

nodes.name: collect_shapes_with_absolute_positions
nodes.summary: Recursively traverses a shape tree (handling GroupShape children) to collect all valid text shapes with their absolute EMU coordinates accumulated from parent offsets.

nodes.name: sort_shapes_by_position
nodes.summary: Sorts a list of ShapeWithPosition objects into visual reading order: groups shapes into horizontal bands (within a tolerance) then sorts each band left-to-right.

nodes.name: calculate_overlap
nodes.summary: Computes the fractional overlap between two axis-aligned rectangles as the ratio of intersection area to the area of the smaller rectangle.

nodes.name: detect_overlaps
nodes.summary: O(n²) pairwise overlap detection across a list of shapes. Returns a list of (i, j, overlap_fraction) tuples for pairs that exceed a threshold.

nodes.name: clear_paragraph_bullets
nodes.summary: Removes all bullet-related XML child elements (buChar, buAutoNum, buNone, buFont) from a paragraph's pPr element, resetting it to plain text.

nodes.name: apply_paragraph_properties
nodes.summary: Applies a full set of paragraph formatting from a ParagraphData dict to a python-pptx paragraph: alignment, indentation, bullet character/auto-number, space-before/after/line, and delegates font properties to apply_font_properties.

nodes.name: apply_font_properties
nodes.summary: Sets font size, bold, italic, underline, and RGB color on a run from a ParagraphData dict.

nodes.name: detect_frame_overflow
nodes.summary: Scans an inventory dict and returns a nested dict of shape keys where frame overflow was detected by ShapeData analysis.

nodes.name: validate_replacements
nodes.summary: Cross-validates a replacements dict against the current inventory: checks that every slide/shape key exists, that no shape is unused while it has content, and aggregates error and warning messages.

nodes.name: check_duplicate_keys
nodes.summary: Raises ValueError if any key appears more than once in the supplied key-value pairs (guards against JSON-level duplicate keys).

nodes.name: apply_replacements
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full replacement workflow: loads the PPTX and replacements JSON, validates them, clears and rewrites each target text frame with new paragraphs preserving formatting, writes to a temp file to check for introduced overflow, then saves to the final output path.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for replace.py. Accepts pptx-file, replacements-json, and output-file positional arguments, validates paths, and delegates to apply_replacements.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for thumbnail.py. Parses arguments (pptx-path, output-dir, --cols, --dpi), coordinates the full thumbnail pipeline: placeholder region extraction, image conversion, and grid composition.

nodes.name: create_hidden_slide_placeholder
nodes.summary: Creates a grey PIL image with a diagonal cross pattern to represent hidden/skipped slides in the output grid.

nodes.name: get_placeholder_regions
nodes.summary: Uses extract_text_inventory to get all shapes, then maps each shape's bounding box to a normalised (0–1) region within its slide for use as overlay annotations in the thumbnail grid.

nodes.name: convert_to_images
nodes.summary: Converts a PPTX to per-slide PNG images by invoking LibreOffice headlessly to produce a PDF, then pdftoppm to rasterise at the requested DPI. Handles hidden slides by inserting placeholder images for missing pages.

nodes.name: create_grids
nodes.summary: Splits the full image list into pages of up to cols*rows slides each, calls create_grid for each page, saves the resulting PIL images to numbered files in the output directory.

nodes.name: create_grid
nodes.summary: Composes a single thumbnail grid image from a batch of slide images: lays out slides in a col×row grid with slide-number labels, and overlays semi-transparent highlight rectangles for each placeholder region.

nodes.name: gap_detection.py
nodes.summary: Core capability gap detection module. Defines gap type/severity enums, a CapabilityGap dataclass, and the detection pipeline: FailurePatternAnalyzer (regex-based failure history), GapClassifier (keyword scoring), PriorityScorer (log/exp weighting), and the top-level GapDetector that composes them to identify gaps from agent failures and user feedback.

nodes.name: gap_remediation.py
nodes.summary: Gap remediation execution module. Implements four remediation strategies: ToolIntegrator (nix-locate + package manager discovery), KnowledgeImporter (structured markdown knowledge files), SkillSynthesizer (SKILL.md generation from examples), and PatternExtractor (pattern file generation). RemediationOrchestrator selects strategy, dispatches, validates results, and persists to JSON state.

nodes.name: remediation_learning.py
nodes.summary: Remediation learning and feedback loop module. OutcomeTracker records remediation results with completeness/effectiveness/efficiency scores and lesson extraction. StrategyOptimizer ranks strategies per gap type from historical data. PlaybookLibrary persists and retrieves reusable playbooks. QualityImprover generates prioritized improvement recommendations from quality trends.

nodes.name: GapType
nodes.summary: Enum defining capability gap categories: TOOL_MISSING, KNOWLEDGE_GAP, SKILL_DEFICIT, PATTERN_MISSING, and related variants used throughout the detection and remediation pipeline.

nodes.name: GapSeverity
nodes.summary: Enum defining severity levels (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) assigned to detected capability gaps based on failure count and feedback signals.

nodes.name: CapabilityGap
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a detected capability gap with gap_id, gap_type, severity, failure_patterns, affected_tasks, user_feedback, priority_score, impact_score, frequency_score, and context. Implements __hash__ and __eq__ for deduplication by gap_id.

nodes.name: FailurePatternAnalyzer
nodes.summary: Analyzes failure messages using per-gap-type regex pattern sets. Maintains a bounded deque failure_history and per-type pattern_cache. Provides get_failure_frequency and get_recent_failures for downstream scoring.

nodes.name: GapClassifier
nodes.summary: Classifies gap descriptions by scoring keyword overlap against gap-type keyword sets, returning ranked (gap_type, score) pairs. determine_severity sets CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW based on failure count and feedback count thresholds.

nodes.name: PriorityScorer
nodes.summary: Scores gap priority using a weighted formula: logarithmic failure_frequency, capped impact from affected_tasks count, and exponential time-decay recency weighting. Populates gap.priority_score, impact_score, frequency_score.

nodes.name: GapDetector
nodes.summary: Top-level gap detection orchestrator. Composes FailurePatternAnalyzer, GapClassifier, and PriorityScorer. detect_from_failure identifies gaps in error messages, merges duplicates, and scores them. detect_from_feedback parses user feedback for gap indicators. Provides get_top_gaps, get_gaps_by_type, get_critical_gaps queries.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Demo/smoke-test entry point. Instantiates GapDetector, exercises detect_from_failure with three sample errors, calls detect_from_feedback, and logs top gaps and gap counts by type.

nodes.name: RemediationStrategy
nodes.summary: Enum of available remediation strategies: TOOL_INTEGRATION, KNOWLEDGE_IMPORT, SKILL_SYNTHESIS, PATTERN_EXTRACTION. Drives strategy dispatch in RemediationOrchestrator.

nodes.name: RemediationStatus
nodes.summary: Enum tracking remediation lifecycle states: PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED.

nodes.name: RemediationPlan
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a remediation plan with plan_id, gap_id, strategy, steps list, estimated_effort, requires_approval flag, timestamps (created_at, started_at, completed_at), status, and error field.

nodes.name: RemediationResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing the result of a remediation execution: success flag, actions_taken list, artifacts_created list, validation_passed flag, and error_message.

nodes.name: ToolIntegrator
nodes.summary: Discovers missing tools via nix-locate subprocess and falls back to package managers. Extracts Nix package names from search output. integrate_tool reads existing nix config, appends package lines, and returns a RemediationResult with actions and artifacts.

nodes.name: KnowledgeImporter
nodes.summary: Imports structured knowledge from configured sources (URLs, local paths, API endpoints) into markdown knowledge files in a knowledge_dir. Formats references and tracks artifacts created.

nodes.name: SkillSynthesizer
nodes.summary: Synthesizes new skill documents from a list of examples. Extracts common action steps by frequency, formats them as numbered steps, and writes SKILL.md files to skills_dir.

nodes.name: PatternExtractor
nodes.summary: Extracts reusable patterns from pattern instances. Analyzes structure of instances to find common keys, generates JSON pattern files in patterns_dir for future remediation reuse.

nodes.name: RemediationValidator
nodes.summary: Validates remediation results by strategy: checks actions_taken count for TOOL_INTEGRATION; checks artifact file existence for KNOWLEDGE_IMPORT, SKILL_SYNTHESIS, and PATTERN_EXTRACTION.

nodes.name: RemediationOrchestrator
nodes.summary: Top-level remediation orchestrator. Selects strategy from gap type mapping, dispatches to KnowledgeImporter/SkillSynthesizer/PatternExtractor, validates results via RemediationValidator, and persists results to a JSON state file. Duplicate of validation methods from RemediationValidator also present internally.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Demo entry point exercising all four remediation components individually: ToolIntegrator.discover_tool, KnowledgeImporter.import_knowledge, SkillSynthesizer.synthesize_skill, PatternExtractor.extract_pattern, and RemediationValidator.

nodes.name: RemediationOutcome
nodes.summary: Dataclass recording a remediation outcome: gap_id, plan_id, strategy, success flag, duration_seconds, validation_passed, timestamp, completeness_score, effectiveness_score, efficiency_score, lessons_learned list, and improvements_suggested list.

nodes.name: RemediationPlaybook
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a reusable playbook for a specific gap_type and strategy combination with success_rate, usage_count, avg_duration_seconds, preconditions/postconditions, and optimization_history.

nodes.name: OutcomeTracker
nodes.summary: Tracks remediation outcomes grouped by strategy and gap type. Calculates per-outcome scores: completeness (actions + artifacts), effectiveness (validation + success), efficiency (actual vs expected duration). Extracts lessons from failures and generates improvement suggestions. Provides get_strategy_success_rate and get_avg_duration queries.

nodes.name: StrategyOptimizer
nodes.summary: Optimizes strategy selection per gap type using historical OutcomeTracker data. Ranks strategies by average effectiveness score; falls back to type-specific default orderings when no history exists.

nodes.name: PlaybookLibrary
nodes.summary: Persistent library of remediation playbooks. Loads existing JSON playbooks from disk on init, creates new playbooks from outcomes, finds similar playbooks by gap_type ranked by success_rate, and updates playbooks with new performance data and optimization history entries.

nodes.name: QualityImprover
nodes.summary: Analyzes quality trends across all tracked outcomes (avg completeness, effectiveness, efficiency rates, success rate, validation rate). Generates prioritized improvement recommendations based on thresholds and aggregated improvement suggestions from outcomes.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Demo entry point. Creates synthetic CapabilityGap, RemediationPlan, and RemediationResult instances, records an outcome via OutcomeTracker, exercises StrategyOptimizer.optimize_for_gap_type, creates a playbook via PlaybookLibrary, and runs QualityImprover trend analysis.

nodes.name: test_workflow_executor.py
nodes.summary: Pytest test suite for WorkflowExecutor and WorkflowPhaseExecutor. Covers session discovery, phase processing, token budget enforcement, error handling, and coordinator HTTP delegation with nested spawn-instance result extraction.

nodes.name: workflow_executor.py
nodes.summary: Core workflow execution engine for the hybrid-coordinator. Defines RetryPolicy (exponential backoff), WorkflowExecutor (async session poller that executes multi-phase plans via LLM or delegated HTTP), WorkflowPhaseExecutor (delegates individual phases to the coordinator /spawn endpoint), and run_standalone_executor entrypoint.

nodes.name: workflow_executor.py
nodes.summary: Single-line re-export shim that forwards all public symbols from workflow/workflow_executor.py to maintain a flat import path for consumers.

nodes.name: RetryPolicy
nodes.summary: Dataclass-style configuration for exponential backoff retry logic. Properties: max_attempts, initial_delay_s, backoff_factor, max_delay_s. Methods: delay_for(attempt) -> float, to_dict(), from_dict(d). Used by WorkflowExecutor to schedule phase retries.

nodes.name: WorkflowExecutor
nodes.summary: Async polling engine that loads multi-phase workflow sessions from a JSON file, tracks running asyncio tasks, applies token budget guards, dispatches phases to either an LLM client or the phase executor, records trajectory events, and handles retries with exponential backoff. Key methods: run(), stop(), _load_sessions(), _save_sessions(), _find_pending_sessions(), _execute_session(), _execute_with_llm(), _update_session().

nodes.name: WorkflowPhaseExecutor
nodes.summary: Delegates individual workflow phase execution to the hybrid-coordinator HTTP /spawn endpoint via httpx.AsyncClient. Builds the phase task description from phase metadata, enforces a token budget cap, and handles both flat and nested spawn-instance result structures. Methods: _build_phase_task(), _delegate_phase_execution(), execute_phase().

nodes.name: run_standalone_executor
nodes.summary: Standalone async entrypoint that constructs a WorkflowExecutor from env-var-driven config (sessions file path, poll interval) and runs it until interrupted, then calls stop(). Used for process-level invocation outside the MCP server context.

nodes.name: temp_sessions_file
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture that writes a pre-populated workflow sessions dict to a NamedTemporaryFile (JSON) and yields the path; cleans up on teardown. Provides a real sessions file for executor tests that require file-backed state.

nodes.name: test_executor_finds_pending_sessions
nodes.summary: Verifies WorkflowExecutor._find_pending_sessions returns the expected set of sessions with status 'pending' from a sessions file. Uses temp_sessions_file fixture.

nodes.name: test_executor_processes_session
nodes.summary: Integration-style test that patches _execute_phase with a fake, runs the executor briefly via asyncio.create_task + sleep + stop, then reloads the sessions file and asserts at least one session reached a terminal state.

nodes.name: test_executor_respects_budget
nodes.summary: Tests that WorkflowExecutor skips sessions whose token budget is exhausted (_find_pending_sessions returns empty for over-budget sessions).

nodes.name: test_executor_handles_errors
nodes.summary: Tests that errors raised inside _execute_session are caught and the session file updated to reflect a failed/error state rather than crashing the executor loop.

nodes.name: test_workflow_phase_executor_uses_delegate_helper
nodes.summary: Patches the delegation helper on WorkflowPhaseExecutor with a fake and asserts execute_phase routes through it, returning the expected result structure.

nodes.name: test_workflow_phase_executor_reads_nested_spawn_instance_result
nodes.summary: Tests the nested spawn-instance result extraction path in _delegate_phase_execution. Uses monkeypatch to inject a fake httpx session returning a body with a nested 'spawn_instance' key, asserts the executor unwraps it correctly.

nodes.name: inventory.py
nodes.summary: PPTX text inventory extraction library. Provides shape traversal with absolute position computation, text/paragraph analysis, frame overflow detection via Pillow font rendering, overlap detection, and JSON serialization. Entry point main() wraps CLI. Key exports: extract_text_inventory, get_inventory_as_dict, ShapeData, ParagraphData.

nodes.name: replace.py
nodes.summary: PPTX text replacement tool. Reads replacement map JSON, validates against inventory, applies paragraph/font formatting properties (bullets, spacing, color, size) via python-pptx OxmlElement manipulation, checks post-replacement frame overflow, saves output. Imports extract_text_inventory from inventory.py.

nodes.name: thumbnail.py
nodes.summary: PPTX thumbnail grid generator. Converts PPTX slides to images via LibreOffice subprocess (pptx→pdf→png via pdftoppm), assembles slide grids with Pillow, overlays placeholder region highlights. Imports extract_text_inventory from inventory.py for placeholder region mapping.

nodes.name: ShapeWithPosition
nodes.summary: Lightweight dataclass holding a python-pptx shape object alongside its computed absolute left/top position (accounting for group nesting).

nodes.name: ParagraphData
nodes.summary: Captures paragraph-level metadata from a python-pptx paragraph: text, alignment, indent level, space before/after, bullet character, font name/size/bold/italic/color, run-level properties. Serializes via to_dict().

nodes.name: ShapeData
nodes.summary: Core analysis class for a single text-frame shape. Computes EMU→inches/pixels conversions, resolves font paths per OS, detects frame overflow using Pillow text rendering simulation, detects slide-boundary overflow, detects manual bullet symbol issues. Aggregates ParagraphData list. Serializes to dict.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for inventory.py. Parses --input/--output/--issues-only args, validates .pptx path, calls extract_text_inventory, saves JSON output, prints summary stats.

nodes.name: is_valid_shape
nodes.summary: Filters shapes: accepts text-frame shapes with non-empty text, rejects purely numeric content, rejects slide-number/date/footer placeholders.

nodes.name: collect_shapes_with_absolute_positions
nodes.summary: Recursively traverses shape tree (including GroupShapes) accumulating parent offsets to compute absolute left/top. Filters via is_valid_shape, returns list of ShapeWithPosition.

nodes.name: sort_shapes_by_position
nodes.summary: Reading-order sort: groups shapes into horizontal rows by proximity threshold, then sorts rows top-to-bottom and within rows left-to-right.

nodes.name: calculate_overlap
nodes.summary: Computes overlap area (in inches²) between two bounding rectangles with a tolerance parameter. Returns 0.0 if no significant overlap.

nodes.name: detect_overlaps
nodes.summary: O(n²) pairwise overlap scan across all shapes on a slide. Returns list of overlap records with shape indices and overlap area.

nodes.name: extract_text_inventory
nodes.summary: Main extraction function. Iterates slides, collects shapes with positions, constructs ShapeData per shape (with overflow/bullet analysis), sorts by reading order, runs overlap detection. Returns dict keyed by slide/shape identifiers.

nodes.name: get_inventory_as_dict
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper: calls extract_text_inventory and serializes all ShapeData objects to plain dicts via to_dict(). Returns nested dict suitable for JSON output.

nodes.name: save_inventory
nodes.summary: Serializes inventory dict (ShapeData objects) to JSON file at output_path via json.dump with indent.

nodes.name: clear_paragraph_bullets
nodes.summary: Removes bullet-related OxmlElements (buChar, buNone, buAutoNum, buClr) from a paragraph's pPr element by direct XML manipulation.

nodes.name: apply_paragraph_properties
nodes.summary: Applies paragraph-level formatting from a para_data dict: alignment, indent, space before/after, bullet character (via OxmlElement buChar), line spacing, then adds a text run and delegates font formatting to apply_font_properties.

nodes.name: apply_font_properties
nodes.summary: Applies run-level font properties from para_data: font name, size (Pt), bold, italic, underline, color (RGBColor from hex). Uses getattr for attribute-based property setting.

nodes.name: detect_frame_overflow
nodes.summary: Scans inventory dict for shapes where estimated_overflow_lines > 0, returns dict mapping slide/shape keys to overflow line counts.

nodes.name: validate_replacements
nodes.summary: Validates replacement map against inventory: checks slide key format, verifies shape keys exist, reports unused shape keys that have content. Returns list of error/warning strings.

nodes.name: check_duplicate_keys
nodes.summary: Raises ValueError if any key appears more than once in a list of (key, value) pairs, preventing silent overwrites in JSON replacement maps.

nodes.name: apply_replacements
nodes.summary: Main replacement engine: loads PPTX + JSON replacements, validates, iterates slides/shapes, clears text frames, applies new paragraphs with formatting. Performs pre/post overflow check via temp save + re-inventory. Raises ValueError on overflow regression. Saves final output.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for replace.py. Requires 3 positional args: input.pptx, replacements.json, output.pptx. Validates paths, calls apply_replacements.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for thumbnail.py. Parses --input/--output/--cols/--width args, orchestrates get_placeholder_regions → convert_to_images → create_grids pipeline. Exits on conversion failure.

nodes.name: create_hidden_slide_placeholder
nodes.summary: Creates a gray placeholder PIL image with a diagonal cross pattern for hidden/skipped slides in the thumbnail grid.

nodes.name: get_placeholder_regions
nodes.summary: Extracts text-frame placeholder bounding boxes per slide from inventory (via extract_text_inventory). Returns list of region dicts with slide index and normalized coordinates for overlay rendering.

nodes.name: convert_to_images
nodes.summary: Converts PPTX to slide images: runs LibreOffice subprocess to produce PDF, then pdftoppm to rasterize at target DPI. Inserts create_hidden_slide_placeholder images for hidden slides. Returns ordered list of image paths.

nodes.name: create_grids
nodes.summary: Splits image list into pages of slides-per-grid, calls create_grid for each page, saves resulting PIL images to output path variants, returns list of saved file paths.

nodes.name: create_grid
nodes.summary: Assembles a single thumbnail grid image: computes cell sizes, pastes slide thumbnails into grid layout, adds slide number labels, overlays semi-transparent placeholder region highlights (alpha composite) with configurable color.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Docker image for the AIDB MCP server. Based on python:3.11-slim; installs system deps (build-essential, curl, aria2), PyTorch 2.11.0 CPU-only via --index-url (preventing CUDA bleed from sentence-transformers), then project requirements.txt. Copies aidb/, shared/, and config/config.yaml into /app. Creates /data and /app/.mcp_cache directories, exposes port 8091, and starts via start_with_discovery.sh which launches both the MCP JSON-RPC server and a background tool-discovery daemon. Uses BuildKit cache mounts for pip to accelerate rebuilds.

nodes.name: aidb-mcp-server
nodes.summary: Runtime container for the AIDB MCP server: Python 3.11-slim image exposing port 8091. Entrypoint is /app/start_with_discovery.sh which runs the MCP JSON-RPC server and a background tool-discovery daemon. Persistent data at /data; tool-discovery cache at /app/.mcp_cache. Env includes PYTHONPATH=/app, HTTP_TIMEOUT=300, and PIP_ONLY_BINARY=:all: (CPU-only wheel enforcement). PYTHONPATH set to /app to allow imports from aidb/ and shared/ co-located packages.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Docker image definition for the aider-wrapper MCP server. Builds from python:3.12-slim, installs git and Python dependencies (requirements.txt + aider-chat==0.16.0), copies shared auth middleware and server.py, configures a global git identity for Aider, and exposes port 8099. The AIDER_BIN env var is injected by the NixOS service to allow the Nix-managed aider-chat package to override the pip-installed version at runtime.

nodes.name: aider-wrapper
nodes.summary: Containerised aider-wrapper service running on port 8099. Uses Python 3.12, ships aider-chat==0.16.0 as a fallback (Nix service may override via AIDER_BIN), and loads shared authentication middleware from the /app/shared directory.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Dockerfile for the container-engine MCP server. Builds a Python 3.13-slim image that installs Podman CLI tools (podman, curl, netcat-openbsd), copies shared libraries and server.py, exposes port 8095, and runs the MCP server via `python server.py`. Provides Docker/Podman container management capabilities as an MCP tool.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Container build definition for the MCP embeddings service, based on python:3.11-slim. Installs PyTorch (CPU), Flask, and sentence-transformers, then exposes the embedding server on port 8081.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Dockerfile for the health-monitor self-healing MCP service. Builds a Python 3.11-slim container that runs self_healing_daemon.py as a headless daemon. Installs curl and podman at runtime, configures pip with caching and retry settings, copies shared Podman API client libraries, and mounts a /data/logs volume. No ports are exposed — the daemon manages other containers directly via Podman API rather than accepting inbound connections.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage OCI/Docker build for the hybrid-coordinator MCP server. Builder stage (python:3.13-slim) compiles Python wheel dependencies into /build/.local using pip cache mounts. Final stage copies the compiled packages, application modules, shared utilities, and config, creates a non-root 'coordinator' user, sets /data/* directories for telemetry/fine-tuning/collections, exposes port 8092, and launches via start_with_learning.sh.

nodes.name: builder
nodes.summary: Builder stage based on python:3.13-slim. Installs gcc/g++ build tools, upgrades pip/wheel/setuptools, and installs all Python requirements from requirements.txt into /build/.local using pip cache mounts for layer efficiency.

nodes.name: final
nodes.summary: Final runtime stage based on python:3.13-slim. Copies compiled packages from builder, all coordinator Python modules, shared utilities, and config. Runs as non-root 'coordinator' user. Exposes port 8092. Entrypoint is start_with_learning.sh which starts both the FastAPI/MCP server and the continuous-learning daemon.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Docker build for the nixos-docs MCP server. Uses python:3.11-slim as base, installs PyTorch CPU-only explicitly before other deps to prevent CUDA dependency pull-in, installs from a hash-pinned requirements.lock, copies shared auth middleware and application code, creates data directories for cache/repos/embeddings, exposes port 8094, and starts via start_server.sh. HEALTHCHECK directive commented out with a note that OCI format requires Kubernetes probe configuration instead.

nodes.name: nixos-docs
nodes.summary: Main (only) build stage: python:3.11-slim base, installs system deps (build-essential, curl, git, ca-certificates), configures pip with 300s timeout and 5 retries, installs PyTorch 2.5.1 CPU-only from pytorch.org/whl/cpu to avoid CUDA, then installs locked Python deps, copies shared/ and nixos-docs/ application code, creates /data/cache /data/repos /data/embeddings /app/.nixos_cache, chmod +x start_server.sh, exposes 8094.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Docker build definition for the qdrant-populator MCP server. Uses python:3.12-slim, installs sentence-transformers==3.3.1 and qdrant-client==1.12.1, then runs populate.py to seed the Qdrant vector store.

nodes.name: qdrant-populator (python:3.12-slim)
nodes.summary: Single-stage Python 3.12 container that installs sentence-transformers and qdrant-client, then executes populate.py to bulk-load embeddings into Qdrant at startup.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Docker build definition for the Ralph Wiggum Loop MCP server — a continuous autonomous agent orchestrator. Builds from python:3.12-slim, installs system deps (git, curl, jq, build-essential), uses BuildKit pip cache mounts for fast layer caching, copies application modules (server.py, orchestrator.py, loop_engine.py, state_manager.py, hooks.py, config/), shared auth middleware, and sets RALPH_LOOP_ENABLED=true. Creates /data/telemetry, /data/state, /workspace data directories. Exposes port 8004. Entrypoint: python server.py.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Docker build definition for the dashboard backend Python service. Uses python:3.13-slim base image, installs system packages (curl, gcc, libpq-dev for psycopg/postgres support), installs Python dependencies from requirements.txt, copies source, exposes port 8889, and launches uvicorn serving api.main:app.

nodes.name: dashboard-backend-container
nodes.summary: Single-stage Python 3.13 slim container: apt-installs curl, gcc, libpq-dev (enables psycopg2/3 compilation), pip-installs from requirements.txt, exposes port 8889, and starts with CMD ["python", "-m", "uvicorn", "api.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8889"].

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Dockerfile for a Python 3.11-slim embeddings microservice. Installs PyTorch (CPU-only), Flask, and sentence-transformers, copies server.py into /app, exposes port 8081, and launches the Flask server via CMD. Intended as a containerised embedding endpoint for MCP integration.

nodes.name: gitleaks.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow: runs Gitleaks secret scanning on push/PR to main and weekly (cron). Installs gitleaks v8.24.3, scans repo non-blockingly (exit-code 0), outputs SARIF, uploads to GitHub Security tab.

nodes.name: harness-sdk-release.yml
nodes.summary: Harness SDK release pipeline: validates version parity, builds Python and npm packages, generates provenance/SBOM, conditionally publishes to PyPI and npm on harness-sdk-v* tags or manual dispatch.

nodes.name: memory-benchmarks.yml
nodes.summary: Memory regression benchmark workflow: triggers on aidb or memory-regression-tests.py changes. Uses Python 3.13 + uv, executes scripts/testing/memory-regression-tests.py.

nodes.name: nix-build.yml
nodes.summary: NixOS flake build workflow: installs Nix (nixos-25.11 channel, flakes enabled), runs flake check, builds hyperd-ai-dev system toplevel. Non-blocking (|| true). Writes step summary.

nodes.name: security.yml
nodes.summary: Comprehensive security audit workflow (305 lines): security-audit.sh, Trivy container scans for 7 core + 4 custom images (matrix), Dockerfile hadolint, blocking Gitleaks secret detection, aggregated summary job. Produces SARIF reports uploaded to GitHub Security tab.

nodes.name: shellcheck.yml
nodes.summary: ShellCheck analysis workflow: runs ludeeus/action-shellcheck on scripts/ on push/PR when .sh files change. Severity=error, continue-on-error=true. Excludes SC1090,SC1091,SC2034,SC2154,SC2086,SC2046.

nodes.name: test-coverage.yml
nodes.summary: Targeted coverage workflow: installs coverage+pytest-cov against dashboard/backend and hybrid-coordinator requirements. Executes scripts/testing/run-targeted-coverage.sh, uploads .reports/coverage-targeted artifact.

nodes.name: test.yml
nodes.summary: Primary CI Test & QA workflow (794 lines, ~17 jobs): repo/governance linting (13 checks), skill governance lint, Nix flake validation+static analysis, shellcheck, bash syntax+contract validation, project structure check, BATS unit tests, CLI smoke tests, SDK and skill bundle parity, advanced parity suite, security scan, and release build on version tags.

nodes.name: tests-legacy.yml
nodes.summary: Legacy tests workflow: sequential unit tests (BATS, non-blocking) then integration tests requiring KVM/libvirt environment (qemu-kvm, libvirtd). Both jobs skip gracefully when test files are absent.

nodes.name: tests.yml
nodes.summary: Current tests workflow (90 lines): repo structure lint, flake-first roadmap verification (with gitleaks-action guard), PAR-002 parity scorecard gate, and service unit test matrix (aidb/hybrid-coordinator/nixos-docs) with pytest+coverage.

nodes.name: validate-shell-scripts.yml
nodes.summary: Shell script validation workflow: validates color echo usage and ANSI output support (posts PR comment on failure), runs ShellCheck at warning severity, and lints script timeout annotations.

nodes.name: scan
nodes.summary: Gitleaks Secret Scan job: full-depth checkout, installs gitleaks v8.24.3, scans with --no-git --redact --exit-code 0 (non-blocking), outputs SARIF, uploads to GitHub Security tab when not a forked PR.

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Validate and Build SDK Artifacts: checks version parity (check-harness-sdk-version-parity.sh with optional tag arg), runs SDK parity smoke, builds Python dist and npm tarball from hybrid-coordinator, generates provenance/SBOM, uploads harness-sdk-artifacts.

nodes.name: publish-pypi
nodes.summary: Publishes Python SDK to PyPI via pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish using OIDC (id-token: write). Gated on harness-sdk-v* tag or workflow_dispatch with publish=true. Depends on validate job.

nodes.name: publish-npm
nodes.summary: Publishes JS SDK to npm using NPM_TOKEN secret. Skips gracefully when token is absent. Same gate condition as publish-pypi. Depends on validate job.

nodes.name: memory-benchmark-regressions
nodes.summary: Runs scripts/testing/memory-regression-tests.py with Python 3.13 + uv + pyyaml/psutil. Detects memory regressions in the aidb stack.

nodes.name: nix-build
nodes.summary: Installs Nix with flakes experimental feature (nixos-25.11), runs flake check (--all-systems || true), builds .#nixosConfigurations.hyperd-ai-dev.config.system.build.toplevel (non-blocking). Writes step summary.

nodes.name: security-audit
nodes.summary: Security Configuration Audit: runs scripts/security/security-audit.sh, uploads results artifact (30-day retention), blocks CI on BLOCKING keyword, warns on CRITICAL keyword, posts full results as PR comment via github-script.

nodes.name: trivy-scan-core
nodes.summary: Trivy vulnerability scan matrix across 7 core infrastructure images (postgres:17.9, redis:7.4.8, qdrant:v1.17.0, nginx:1.28.2, grafana:12.4.1, prometheus:v3.10.0, jaeger:2.16.0). Outputs per-image SARIF, uploads to Security tab. Non-blocking (exit-code 0).

nodes.name: trivy-scan-custom
nodes.summary: Trivy scan matrix for 4 custom project images (aidb, embeddings-service, hybrid-coordinator, nixos-docs). Builds each from ai-stack/mcp-servers Dockerfiles, scans CRITICAL+HIGH findings. Non-blocking. Depends on security-audit.

nodes.name: dockerfile-lint
nodes.summary: Dockerfile linting with hadolint v2.14.0 across all Dockerfiles in ai-stack/mcp-servers. Iterates discovered Dockerfiles, fails on errors (--failure-threshold error). Uploads hadolint-results artifact.

nodes.name: secrets-scan
nodes.summary: Blocking Gitleaks secret detection (exit-code 1). Full-depth checkout, uses .gitleaks.toml config, uploads SARIF to Security tab. Unlike standalone gitleaks.yml, this job is blocking.

nodes.name: security-summary
nodes.summary: Aggregates results from all 5 security jobs and writes a markdown step summary showing pass/fail per check. Always runs (if: always()). Depends on security-audit, trivy-scan-core, trivy-scan-custom, dockerfile-lint, secrets-scan.

nodes.name: shellcheck
nodes.summary: Runs ludeeus/action-shellcheck on scripts/ at error severity with exclusions SC1090,SC1091,SC2034,SC2154,SC2086,SC2046. continue-on-error=true. Writes step summary.

nodes.name: targeted-coverage
nodes.summary: Targeted coverage: Python 3.11 + uv, installs coverage+pytest-cov plus requirements for dashboard/backend and hybrid-coordinator. Runs scripts/testing/run-targeted-coverage.sh, uploads .reports/coverage-targeted artifact.

nodes.name: repo-structure-lint
nodes.summary: Runs 13 governance scripts: repo-structure-lint, allowlist integrity, root file hygiene, shim consistency, root shim-only policy, doc link integrity, doc metadata standards, doc script-path migration, naming/label consistency, archive path consistency, legacy deprecated root, generated artifact hygiene, deprecated docs location.

nodes.name: harness-first-owner-approval
nodes.summary: PR-only gate: enforces platform-owner approval for changes to harness-first high-impact paths via check-harness-first-platform-owner-approval.py using GITHUB_TOKEN.

nodes.name: skill-governance-lint
nodes.summary: Validates agent skill governance: check-skill-source-of-truth.sh, lint-skill-external-deps.sh, lint-skill-template.sh. Ensures skills conform to registry SSOT, dependency, and template standards.

nodes.name: flake-validation
nodes.summary: Comprehensive NixOS flake validation: flake show/check (NIXPKGS_ALLOW_UNFREE=1), profile eval smoke tests (ai-dev/gaming/minimal × all hosts), package count drift check, flake input validation+reporting, non-Nix tool management policy, declarative runtime wiring. Uploads flake-validation-report.

nodes.name: nix-static-analysis
nodes.summary: Nix static analysis: runs statix (anti-patterns), deadnix (dead bindings), and alejandra (formatter check) via nix shell. Non-blocking (--non-blocking flag to script).

nodes.name: shellcheck
nodes.summary: Shellcheck + linting in test.yml: collects all .sh files via git ls-files (excluding deprecated), plus extras (mcp-db-validate, mcp-server), runs shellcheck at warning severity. Uploads shellcheck.log artifact.

nodes.name: syntax
nodes.summary: Multi-check syntax job: bash -n on all .sh files, aq-report JSON/metric contracts, PRSI cycle/bootstrap/phase7 static gates, harness-first static gates, tool security auditor regression smoke, cross-client compat smoke (best-effort).

nodes.name: validate-structure
nodes.summary: Validates ~30 required project files are present: README, LICENSE, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, all 8 phase scripts, health checks, MCP scripts, templates, harness-first docs/config/scripts.

nodes.name: bats-tests
nodes.summary: BATS unit tests: installs bats from apt, runs tests/run-unit-tests.sh.

nodes.name: smoke-tests
nodes.summary: CLI smoke tests: validates phase inventory (8 required core phases), centralized config settings, nixos-quick-deploy.sh --help/--version/--list-phases, AQD CLI --version/workflows-list/skill-validate. Depends on syntax and validate-structure.

nodes.name: harness-sdk-parity
nodes.summary: SDK parity smoke: Python 3.12, installs build backend, runs smoke-harness-sdk-packaging.sh and generate-harness-sdk-api-docs.sh. Depends on syntax.

nodes.name: skill-bundle-parity
nodes.summary: Skill bundle distribution smoke: Python 3.12 + pyyaml + jq, runs smoke-skill-bundle-distribution.sh. Depends on syntax.

nodes.name: advanced-parity-tooling
nodes.summary: Advanced parity suite: Nix + Python 3.12 + pyyaml/jq, runs run-advanced-parity-suite.sh (chaos harness, boot/shutdown integration, API auth hardening, AI SLO runtime, cross-client compat, skill registry signing/verification, SDK provenance). Depends on syntax.

nodes.name: security
nodes.summary: Basic security scan in test.yml: git grep for hardcoded credentials in .sh/.nix/.py (non-blocking, informational), find world-writable files (blocking if found).

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Release build job (tags only): creates tarball excluding .git/.github/deprecated/*.log, generates SHA256 checksum, uploads release-package artifact, creates GitHub Release via softprops/action-gh-release. Depends on shellcheck+syntax+validate-structure+smoke-tests+security.

nodes.name: unit-tests
nodes.summary: Legacy unit tests: runs BATS tests from tests/unit/*.bats. Non-blocking (on failure continues with exit 0). Skips when no test files found.

nodes.name: integration-tests
nodes.summary: Legacy integration tests: installs qemu-kvm/libvirt/bridge-utils, starts libvirtd, runs tests/integration/*.sh scripts. Skips when no files found. Depends on unit-tests.

nodes.name: repo-structure-lint
nodes.summary: Repo structure lint: runs repo-structure-lint.sh --all and check-root-script-shim-only.sh to enforce repository layout and shim-only root policy.

nodes.name: flake-first-roadmap-verifier
nodes.summary: Flake-first roadmap verification: bash -n syntax checks on deploy entrypoints, runs verify-flake-first-roadmap-completion.sh, guards against deprecated gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2 usage via rg.

nodes.name: parity-scorecard-gate
nodes.summary: PAR-002 parity gate: Python 3.11, runs harness-runner.sh --offline --skip-parity (eval pack schema) then harness-runner.sh --offline --skip-schema (parity scorecard). Both must pass.

nodes.name: unit-tests
