nodes.summary: Service unit test matrix (aidb, hybrid-coordinator, nixos-docs): Python 3.11 + pytest with coverage report. Installs per-service requirements. Skips gracefully on pytest exit code 5 (no tests collected).

nodes.name: validate-shell-color-echo
nodes.summary: Validates color echo usage (lint-color-echo-usage.sh) and ANSI output support (validate-echo-colors.sh). On failure in a PR, posts a github-script comment with fix instructions.

nodes.name: shellcheck
nodes.summary: ShellCheck at warning severity (ignoring node_modules/.git) via ludeeus/action-shellcheck, plus lint-timeouts.sh --ci for script timeout annotations. Both continue-on-error.

nodes.name: 001_temporal_facts.sql
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL migration 001 that establishes the temporal facts knowledge store for the multi-agent AI stack. Creates the temporal_facts table with validity-window tracking and pgvector embeddings, an audit log table, 9 indexes, 4 agent-isolated diary views, a backward-compat memories view, helper functions, and auto-timestamp/audit triggers. Also provides an optional migrate_old_memories() utility for lifting data from the legacy memories table.

nodes.name: temporal_facts
nodes.summary: Core knowledge store table. Each row is a versioned, time-bounded fact identified by UUID. Key columns: content (TEXT), content_hash (SHA256 for dedup), project/topic/type taxonomy (type constrained to decision|preference|discovery|event|advice|fact), valid_from/valid_until for temporal windowing (NULL valid_until = indefinite), agent_owner (NULL = shared, non-null = private diary), tags (TEXT[]), confidence (0-1 float), embedding_vector (vector(1536) for cosine similarity search), full audit trail (created/updated timestamps and agents, version counter). Unique constraint on (content_hash, project, valid_from).

nodes.name: temporal_facts_audit
nodes.summary: Append-only audit log table. Captures field-level changes to temporal_facts rows (primarily valid_until expiration and content edits). Columns: audit_id (SERIAL PK), fact_id (FK → temporal_facts CASCADE DELETE), field_changed, old_value/new_value (TEXT), changed_by, changed_at, reason. Populated automatically by the audit_temporal_changes trigger.

nodes.name: memories
nodes.summary: Backward-compatibility view over temporal_facts. Surfaces only currently valid (valid_until IS NULL OR > NOW()) shared (agent_owner IS NULL) facts, projecting columns to match the old memories table schema: id, content, project, category (mapped from topic), type, embedding, timestamp (from created_at), author (from created_by).

nodes.name: agent_qwen_diary
nodes.summary: Filtered view of temporal_facts for Qwen3 agent private diary entries (agent_owner='qwen', project='agent-qwen'). Provides row-level isolation so each agent reads only its own diary.

nodes.name: agent_codex_diary
nodes.summary: Filtered view of temporal_facts for Codex agent private diary entries (agent_owner='codex', project='agent-codex').

nodes.name: agent_claude_diary
nodes.summary: Filtered view of temporal_facts for Claude agent private diary entries (agent_owner='claude', project='agent-claude').

nodes.name: agent_gemini_diary
nodes.summary: Filtered view of temporal_facts for Gemini agent private diary entries (agent_owner='gemini', project='agent-gemini').

nodes.name: is_valid_at
nodes.summary: IMMUTABLE plpgsql function. Accepts a temporal_facts row and a timestamp; returns BOOLEAN true if check_time falls within the fact's [valid_from, valid_until] window (NULL valid_until treated as open-ended).

nodes.name: is_stale
nodes.summary: IMMUTABLE plpgsql function. Returns BOOLEAN true when a temporal_facts row has a non-null valid_until that is strictly before current_time, indicating the fact has expired.

nodes.name: get_valid_facts
nodes.summary: STABLE set-returning plpgsql function. Returns all temporal_facts rows whose validity window covers at_time (defaults to NOW()). Primary query entry-point for current knowledge retrieval.

nodes.name: get_stale_facts
nodes.summary: STABLE set-returning plpgsql function. Returns all temporal_facts rows where valid_until is non-null and before current_time. Used for maintenance sweeps and cache invalidation.

nodes.name: update_temporal_facts_timestamp
nodes.summary: BEFORE UPDATE trigger function. Sets NEW.updated_at = NOW() and increments NEW.version by 1 on every row update in temporal_facts. Enforces optimistic-locking version tracking.

nodes.name: audit_temporal_changes
nodes.summary: AFTER UPDATE trigger function. Inserts into temporal_facts_audit when valid_until or content changes on a temporal_facts row. Content stored truncated to 200 chars for space efficiency. Drives the full audit trail for knowledge expiration and correction events.

nodes.name: migrate_old_memories
nodes.summary: Optional one-shot migration helper. Reads rows from the legacy memories_old table (if it exists) and inserts them into temporal_facts using MD5 content hash, mapping category→topic and using ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotency. Returns (migrated_count, skipped_count, error_count) tuple. Call manually: SELECT migrate_old_memories();

nodes.name: V20__world_model_query_patterns.sql
nodes.summary: Flyway migration V20 for the World Model predictive context-warming subsystem. Creates the `query_sequence_patterns` table and three supporting indexes to record temporal query-sequence patterns used for proactive cache warming.

nodes.name: query_sequence_patterns
nodes.summary: Stores observed query-sequence patterns keyed by a hash of the triggering query, capturing hour-of-day, day-of-week, follow-on query hashes, and occurrence counts to enable time-aware predictive context warming.

nodes.name: idx_qsp_hash
nodes.summary: B-tree index on `query_hash` for fast lookup of all pattern records associated with a specific originating query.

nodes.name: idx_qsp_hour
nodes.summary: B-tree index on `hour_of_day` to support time-of-day filtering when selecting patterns relevant to the current hour.

nodes.name: idx_qsp_last_seen
nodes.summary: B-tree index on `last_seen` timestamp to efficiently retrieve or expire stale pattern records ordered by recency.

nodes.name: 006_autonomous_improvement.sql
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL migration defining schema for autonomous self-improvement: improvement cycles, trigger events, experiment results, system timeseries metrics, design decisions, metric trends, optimization hypotheses, and improvement recommendations with associated views.

nodes.name: improvement_cycles
nodes.summary: Tracks autonomous improvement cycles including cycle type, accepted/rejected experiment counts, total improvement percentage, cost, status, and timestamps.

nodes.name: trigger_events
nodes.summary: Records events that trigger improvement cycles: type (event/cron/critical), threshold values, context, and resolution timestamps. References improvement_cycles via cycle_id.

nodes.name: experiment_results_history
nodes.summary: Stores results of individual experiments within improvement cycles including type, sample size, acceptance status, and timing. FK to improvement_cycles.

nodes.name: system_metrics_timeseries
nodes.summary: TimescaleDB hypertable for storing system metric samples over time: metric name, value, unit, partitioned by time for efficient range queries.

nodes.name: design_decisions
nodes.summary: Records architectural and configuration design decisions with type, workflow scope, expected metric impact, confidence, reviewer, and revert tracking.

nodes.name: metric_trends
nodes.summary: Aggregated trend data per metric over configurable time windows with baseline values and volatility flags.

nodes.name: optimization_hypotheses
nodes.summary: Stores generated optimization hypotheses with evidence, priority, test status, and result linkage. FK to improvement_cycles.

nodes.name: improvement_recommendations
nodes.summary: Actionable improvement recommendations with type (CONFIGURATION/etc), risk assessment, evidence, implementation and review timestamps.

nodes.name: metrics_hourly
nodes.summary: Hourly aggregation view over system_metrics_timeseries for time-bucketed metric analysis.

nodes.name: metrics_daily
nodes.summary: Daily aggregation view over system_metrics_timeseries for trend and dashboard use.

nodes.name: active_improvement_cycles
nodes.summary: Filters improvement_cycles to currently active (non-cancelled, non-completed) cycles for live monitoring.

nodes.name: recent_experiments
nodes.summary: Recent experiment results joined with cycle context for dashboard and reporting.

nodes.name: top_recommendations
nodes.summary: High-priority pending improvement recommendations ranked by priority for agent consumption.

nodes.name: system_health_summary
nodes.summary: Cross-table health summary aggregating cycle status, recent experiments, and key metric trends.

nodes.name: 007_federated_learning.sql
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL migration for cross-agent federated learning: agent pattern tracking, cross-agent recommendations, capability matrix, pattern application history, and federated learning session management.

nodes.name: agent_patterns
nodes.summary: Core pattern store for agents: captures agent type, task domain, success rate, usage count, token efficiency, and tags for cross-agent sharing.

nodes.name: cross_agent_recommendations
nodes.summary: Records cross-agent pattern recommendations with source/target agents, confidence score, adoption status, and outcome metadata.

nodes.name: agent_capability_matrix
nodes.summary: Capability scores per agent per task domain: success rate, completion time, quality score, token usage, sample size for routing decisions.

nodes.name: pattern_application_history
nodes.summary: Audit trail for pattern applications: which agent applied which pattern on what task, outcome (success, quality, tokens), and error details.

nodes.name: federated_learning_sessions
nodes.summary: Tracks federated learning sessions that aggregate patterns and update capability matrices across the agent fleet.

nodes.name: top_patterns_by_agent
nodes.summary: Ranks patterns per agent type by success rate and usage for routing and recommendation engines.

nodes.name: cross_agent_learning_effectiveness
nodes.summary: Measures how effectively cross-agent recommendations are adopted and their resulting impact.

nodes.name: agent_capability_rankings
nodes.summary: Ranks agents by capability score per task domain for intelligent task routing.

nodes.name: recent_pattern_discoveries
nodes.summary: Highlights newly discovered high-success patterns surfaced from recent federated learning sessions.

nodes.name: 008_meta_optimization.sql
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL migration for harness meta-optimization: improvement proposals targeting harness components, evolution history tracking applied changes, and performance baselines for regression detection.

nodes.name: harness_improvement_proposals
nodes.summary: Improvement proposals targeting specific harness components with full lifecycle: proposed change, expected impact, implementation steps, rollback plan, and review/apply timestamps.

nodes.name: harness_evolution_history
nodes.summary: Records applied harness changes with actual vs estimated improvement, validation status, and rollback tracking. FK to harness_improvement_proposals.

nodes.name: harness_performance_baselines
nodes.summary: Captures performance baseline snapshots per component and metric for regression detection after harness changes.

nodes.name: active_improvement_proposals
nodes.summary: Filters harness_improvement_proposals to pending/in-review state for agent action queues.

nodes.name: harness_evolution_timeline
nodes.summary: Chronological timeline of harness changes with proposal context for audit and rollback analysis.

nodes.name: meta_optimization_impact_summary
nodes.summary: Aggregates actual vs expected improvement across all applied proposals for meta-optimization effectiveness reporting.

nodes.name: component_evolution_frequency
nodes.summary: Ranks harness components by change frequency to identify hotspots and churn in the meta-optimization loop.

nodes.name: 009_multi_agent_collaboration.sql
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL migration for multi-agent collaboration infrastructure: collaboration sessions, inter-agent messaging, collaborative plan synthesis, peer-contribution tracking, and consensus decision management.

nodes.name: collaborations
nodes.summary: Top-level collaboration session record with unique collaboration_id, objective, type (research/hierarchical), and status tracking.

nodes.name: collaboration_messages
nodes.summary: Inter-agent messages within collaborations: sender, recipient, content, priority, response requirements, and delivery timestamps.

nodes.name: collaborative_plans
nodes.summary: Synthesized plans from multi-agent contributions: overall strategy, estimated timeline, critical risks, phase count, and synthesis timestamp. FK to collaborations.

nodes.name: plan_contributions
nodes.summary: Individual agent contributions to collaborative plans: capability score, phase type, approach, effort estimate, dependencies, risks, and confidence. FK to collaborative_plans.

nodes.name: consensus_decisions
nodes.summary: Consensus voting outcomes per reviewed item: approval rate, weighted average score, escalation flag, decision method (majority/expert_override). FK to collaborations.

nodes.name: consensus_reviews
nodes.summary: Individual agent reviews for consensus decisions: verdict, overall/criteria scores, feedback, suggested improvements, confidence, and review time. FK to consensus_decisions.

nodes.name: active_collaborations_summary
nodes.summary: Live collaboration sessions with participant count, message count, and plan status for orchestrator dashboard.

nodes.name: collaboration_effectiveness
nodes.summary: Completed collaboration outcomes: plan quality, consensus approval rates, and completion time for effectiveness benchmarking.

nodes.name: agent_collaboration_metrics
nodes.summary: Per-agent collaboration stats: contribution count, average confidence, approval rates for capability assessment.

nodes.name: consensus_decision_summary
nodes.summary: Summary of all consensus decisions with review counts, approval rates, and escalation flags.

nodes.name: 010_platform_maturity.sql
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL migration for platform maturity features: agent marketplace, performance benchmarks, cost/quality profiles, federated pattern sharing across deployments, federation node registry, cost budgeting, cost tracking, and audit events.

nodes.name: agent_marketplace
nodes.summary: Registry of available agents with provider, tier, status, capabilities, supported domains, pricing, and tags for marketplace discovery.

nodes.name: agent_performance_benchmarks
nodes.summary: Benchmark data per agent per task domain: success rate, quality score, latency, token usage, and cost. FK to agent_marketplace.

nodes.name: agent_cost_quality_profiles
nodes.summary: Composite cost/quality/speed/reliability profiles per agent per domain for routing and budget optimization. FK to agent_marketplace.

nodes.name: federated_patterns
nodes.summary: Cross-deployment federated patterns identified by problem hash, category, and domain with sharing-level and effectiveness metrics.

nodes.name: federated_metric_contributions
nodes.summary: Metric data contributed by federation nodes for aggregated cross-deployment analytics.

nodes.name: federation_nodes
nodes.summary: Registry of federation peer nodes with sharing level, trusted node list, and endpoint configuration.

nodes.name: cost_budgets
nodes.summary: Per-agent budget limits: daily/monthly caps in USD, cost per token, and alert threshold percentage. FK to agent_marketplace.

nodes.name: cost_tracking
nodes.summary: Per-task cost and token usage records for real-time budget consumption tracking. FK to agent_marketplace.

nodes.name: audit_events
nodes.summary: Immutable audit log of agent platform events with type, agent, and detail payload for compliance and debugging.

nodes.name: active_marketplace_listings
nodes.summary: Filters agent_marketplace to active status with capability and domain summary for agent discovery UIs.

nodes.name: top_agents_by_domain
nodes.summary: Ranks agents by benchmark performance per task domain for routing engine decisions.

nodes.name: federation_network_health
nodes.summary: Health summary of federation nodes: connectivity, last-seen, sharing level distribution.

nodes.name: budget_utilization_summary
nodes.summary: Current budget utilization per agent vs configured daily/monthly limits with alert threshold flags.

nodes.name: audit_event_summary
nodes.summary: Aggregated audit event counts by type and agent for compliance reporting and anomaly detection.

nodes.name: AQ-CHAT-PHASE-A-COMPLETE.md

Documents the outcome of expanding TOOL_SCHEMAS in local_agent_runtime.py, fixing /tools display, adding aq-qa CI parity check, and implementing per-iteration tool hot-swap. Includes endpoint verification results and pre-existing failure notes.

nodes.name: AQ-CHAT-ROUTING-KNOWLEDGE-SUMMARY.md
nodes.summary: Compact summary of aq-chat routing knowledge: repo artifacts produced, key captured design decisions (ToolMode enum, fast-path bypass at aq-chat-direct, PRD v0.3 locked), and local provenance for routing-related files.

nodes.name: AQ-CHAT-ROUTING-PLAN-CONSOLIDATED.md
nodes.summary: Locked consolidated implementation plan (v1.1) for aq-chat unified routing and tool-calling. Covers 5 phases: A (tool registry fix), B (shared classifier + ToolMode), C (HUD transparency + training fix), D (conversational fast-path), E (agent loop event streaming). Includes 51 sections with code-level step definitions, cross-phase concerns, open implementation questions, and a commit strategy.

nodes.name: AQ-CHAT-ROUTING-PRD-CONSOLIDATED.md
nodes.summary: Locked consolidated PRD (v0.3) for aq-chat unified routing and tool-calling architecture. Consolidates Claude, Codex, and Gemini team drafts. Defines mission, scope, current state gaps (tool registry, intent classifier, fast-path, HUD transparency, event emission), proposed architecture with priority-ordered components (P0–P5), 5 implementation phases, validation criteria, and resolved divergences D1–D6.

nodes.name: AQ-CHAT-ROUTING-PRD-codex.md
nodes.summary: Codex team's draft PRD for aq-chat unified routing. Most detailed team PRD (795 lines, 55 sections). Identifies 7 gaps (HUD/profile split, dual tool registry, fragile keyword bypass, coordinator overhead, dual controls), proposes shared intent classifier, HUD transparency, conversational fast-path design, unified tool control, and event emission. 6 implementation phases.

nodes.name: AQ-CHAT-ROUTING-PRD-gemini.md
nodes.summary: Gemini team's draft PRD for aq-chat unified routing. Covers gap analysis from seven perspectives, proposes shared turn classifier with ToolMode enum, fast-path direct route, tool set parity contract, HUD transparency, and coordinator alignment. 5 implementation phases (A–E).

nodes.name: SYSTEMS-SOFTWARE-INSTRUCTIONS.md
nodes.summary: Systems software domain instruction payload. Scope covers NixOS module authoring, kernel/systemd configuration, performance profiling, Rust integration. Eligible tasks include nix flake work, systemd unit hardening, AppArmor policy, llama.cpp tuning. Tool preferences with forbidden list. AIDB namespace binding. Non-negotiable architecture constraints (declarative-only, no hardcoded ports). Review requirements, routing summary, activation checklist.

nodes.name: TRADING-AGENTS-INSTRUCTIONS.md
nodes.summary: Trading agents domain instruction surface. Covers domain mandate (market analysis, strategy research), methodology (data-first, backtesting required), safety guardrails (no live trading without explicit approval, paper-trading by default), and AIDB interaction for market pattern retrieval.

nodes.name: WORKFLOW-CANON.md
nodes.summary: Canonical 8-step agent workflow shared across all agents: (1) ORIENT, (2) RESEARCH with multi-agent review board extension, (3) PRD/PLAN with flat collaborative design protocol (7 phases including dynamic expert assembly, independent drafting, consolidation, consensus, delegation), (4) MEMORY CHECKPOINT, (5) EXECUTE per slice, (6) VALIDATE with mandatory gates and OWASP Agentic Top 10 checklist, (7) DOC-UPDATE with RAG seeding, (8) COMMIT. Includes context engineering rules, compression toolchain reference, service coverage contract, bounded validation primitive rule, and quick reference card.

nodes.name: agent.md
nodes.summary: Empty placeholder file in the .agent directory.

nodes.name: phase173-PLAN-CONSOLIDATED.md
nodes.summary: Consolidated Phase 173 implementation plan for training pipeline fixes across 3 slices. Slice 173-A (Claude): 5 changes to training_ingest.py — _scrub_text helper import, _STRUCTURED_MARKERS expansion, _is_useful_hybrid_event filter, scrub+floor in _ingest_hybrid_events, source field tracking. Slice 173-B (Gemini): continuous_learning.py RAGAS gate constant and PRELIMINARY status gate. Slice 173-E (Gemini): health API route, aq-qa checks 0.13.1-0.13.4, dashboard training card. Includes inter-slice dependency table, dispatch plan, and risk register.

nodes.name: phase173-PLAN-claude.md
nodes.summary: Claude's detailed slice 173-A implementation plan for training_ingest.py. 5 changes with precise file locations and code snippets: (1) import redact_secrets + _scrub_text helper after _REPO_ROOT, (2) _STRUCTURED_MARKERS expansion with tool_use/tool_result/thinking, (3) _is_useful_hybrid_event rewrite with structural content check, (4) scrub call + floor selection in _ingest_hybrid_events, (5) source field population in sample dict. Integration contract with 173-E slice, dependency table, validation sequence, risk assessment.

nodes.name: phase173-PLAN-gemini.md
nodes.summary: Gemini's Phase 173 implementation plan for slices 173-B and 173-E. Slice 173-B: RAGAS_MIN_SAMPLES constant addition and PRELIMINARY status gate in generate_optimization_proposals for insufficient sample protection. Slice 173-E: aq-qa monitoring checks (0.13.1-0.13.4) and dashboard training pipeline card. Integration contract proposal for 173-A↔173-E boundary and validation plan.

nodes.name: Phase 173 Training Pipeline Review — Consolidated PRD
nodes.summary: Multi-agent consolidated PRD for Phase 173 (training pipeline review). Surfaces divergences across Claude, Gemini, and Qwen3 on root cause of dataset stagnation, dual-pipeline architecture, PII scrubbing gap, and feedback closure. Defines slices 173-A (training_ingest.py + PII guard), 173-B (RAGAS gate), 173-E (aq-qa/dashboard). Includes full consensus sign-off protocol and addendum on local agent inference failures (stagnation guard, delegate-to-gemini output contract gap).

nodes.name: Phase 173 Training Pipeline Review — Claude Independent PRD
nodes.summary: Claude's independent PRD for Phase 173. Audits dual pipeline architecture (training_ingest.py vs continuous_learning.py), identifies event type gaps (tool_result missing), RAGAS sample-count fragility, dataset growth stagnation, missing feedback closure metric. Proposes unified event router, tool_result ingestion, RAGAS statistical gate, and aq-qa/dashboard coverage. Addendum analyzes local agent/inference failures including stagnation guard firing and Gemini output contract misdiagnosis.

nodes.name: Phase 173 Training Pipeline Review — Gemini PRD
nodes.summary: Gemini's PRD for Phase 173 training pipeline review. Covers telemetry ingestion and continuous learning architecture, proposes multi-modal quality scoring, evaluator hardening, and ingestion resilience. Shorter summary-form compared to Claude's version; addendum addresses pipeline reliability impact and monitoring gaps from local agent failures.

nodes.name: Phase 173 Training Pipeline Review — Qwen3 Proxy Code Analyst PRD
nodes.summary: Code-analyst PRD for Phase 173, written by Claude as Qwen3 proxy (local agent failed). Provides code-level inspection of training_ingest.py constants, _quality_score() logic, _is_useful_hybrid_event() accepted types, and continuous_learning.py rotation detection. Identifies 6 code-level failures (F1-F6) including tool_result missing from accepted types, is_structured misses short tool calls, synchronous file open in ingest loop, and _mark_proposal() full-rewrite inefficiency.

nodes.name: Phase 175 Consolidated Implementation Plan
nodes.summary: Consolidated implementation plan for Phase 175 (local inference + aq-chat). Three slices: A (immediate correctness fixes — no rebuild: routing substring fix, collective_memory_search backend, context pruner pair preservation, shell injection blocklist, ISO timestamp fix, CoT elicitation, coordinator streaming, KeyboardInterrupt orphan fix), B (rebuild-required: switchboard SSOT guards, llama.cpp circuit breaker, token budget fix, streaming regex fix, grammar-constrained generation, fallback bypass removal, zombie subprocess fix, _sanitize_json fix), C (feedback loop + collaborative review protocol: review board tools, AIDB seeding from PRD, WORKFLOW-CANON update, delegate script injection). Includes per-slice validation criteria and commit strategy.

nodes.name: Phase 175 Local Inference + aq-chat — Consolidated Expert Review PRD
nodes.summary: Consolidated PRD for Phase 175 synthesizing 4 expert roles (CLI/UX, Inference Systems, Agent Coordination, AI Research). Surfaces divergences on routing classification severity, context truncation strategy, Qwen3 CoT, and fallback policy. Severity matrix: P0 (shell injection, switchboard SSOT bypass, no circuit breaker, token budget overflow, coordinator streaming, routing misclassification, grammar-constrained generation, context pruner orphaning tool pairs, feedback loop deficit), P1 (sanitize_json control chars, enable_thinking in proxy path, tool loop no progress signal). Defines collaborative review board anti-recency-bias protocol and 6 architecture recommendations.

nodes.name: Phase 175 Local Inference + aq-chat — Expert Review PRD (Claude)
nodes.summary: Claude's expert review PRD for Phase 175, written across 4 roles. CLI/UX findings: substring routing misclassification (P0), coordinator path black box, KeyboardInterrupt orphan subprocess, no cold-start indicator. Inference findings: main proxy bypasses build_llama_payload() SSOT entirely (P0), no circuit breaker, local-tool-calling token budget exceeds n_ctx, enable_thinking not verified in proxy path. Agent coordination: collective_memory_search_handler wrong backend, _sanitize_json misses control chars, tool loop no progress signal. AI Research: no structured output enforcement, PRE-FLIGHT RESEARCH unconditional, no episodic memory, feedback loop deficit (P0 systemic). Includes feedback loop architecture and severity matrix.

nodes.name: Phase 175 Local Inference + aq-chat — Gemini PRD
nodes.summary: Gemini's expert review PRD for Phase 175. Summary-form covering CLI/UX, inference systems, agent coordination, and AI research critical findings. Severity matrix and architecture recommendations. Shorter than Claude's version; consistent with the 4-role framework.

nodes.name: Phase 175 Local Inference + aq-chat — Qwen3 Self-Review PRD
nodes.summary: Qwen3's self-assessment PRD for Phase 175. Identifies self-observed gaps: implicit (not enforced) tool call format, rules-heavy but reasoning-light system prompt, unconditional PRE-FLIGHT RESEARCH block, tight 1200-token synthesis budget. Tool calling critique: unbounded tool result size, missing read_review_board tool. Inference: 12000-token local-tool-calling input budget dangerous; enable_thinking=false correctly placed in chat_template_kwargs. Capability gaps: no telemetry self-observation, no mechanism to request context reset. Severity table included.

nodes.name: Expert Review: Local Agent Coordination Layer
nodes.summary: Senior agent systems engineer deep-dive review of the local agent coordination layer for Phase 175. Critiques subprocess spawning per request, two parallel runtime paths (local_agent_runtime.py vs agent_executor.py), coordinator-runtime-executor hop count. Tool calling: role:tool vs role:function fixed, mixed prose+JSON parse partial robustness, tool result size cap, schema mismatch between runtimes, security tool whitelist. Memory: two-phase token budget (512/1200) fragility, context pruning breaks tool-call/result pairs. AIDB: collective_memory_search_handler incomplete backend routing, fallback direct Qdrant. Reliability: subprocess crash handling, timeout config mismatch, synthesis truncation bug verified fixed, resource leaks. Training data: inconsistent ISO timestamps, tool_result event format, agent_step_complete signal. Ranked P1-P4 priority recommendations.

nodes.name: Expert Review: Local Inference Stack vs State of the Art
nodes.summary: AI research scientist expert review comparing the local inference stack to SOTA for Phase 175. Covers: ReAct implementation (adequate), ReWOO/Plan-then-Execute gap, Reflexion absent, Tree of Thoughts not needed at scale, grammar-constrained generation gap (P10). Tool design: 14 tools adequate, call format inconsistency (prose+JSON), unbounded tool result size, missing code-execution and browser-automation categories. Memory: working memory pinned+sliding strategy, episodic memory critical gap, embedding model assessment. Multi-agent: two-runtime problem, gaps in modern patterns. Context: system prompt quality, chain-of-thought without thinking tokens, task decomposition. Observability: telemetry infrastructure, training pipeline, feedback loop architecture. Recommends: grammar-constrained tool calling (sev-10), unified runtime (sev-9), ReWOO (sev-8), explicit CoT elicitation (sev-7), episodic memory indexing (sev-6), relevance-scored pruning (sev-5).

nodes.name: Expert Review: aq-chat CLI & Intent Routing System
nodes.summary: Senior CLI/UX engineer expert review of aq-chat and intent routing for Phase 175. Architecture: fast-path vs coordinator routing boundary, misclassification consequences, agentic classification too aggressive. UX issues: error messages, timeout UX (30-300s spinner), streaming vs non-streaming inconsistency, missing production CLI features. Reliability: single points of failure, service-down behavior, retry gaps, race conditions. Intent classification critique: substring over-matching (ok/sure/yes/proceed falsely convert imperative prompts), SNAPSHOT_KEYWORDS bypass, intent classification boundary. Top 10 severity findings (SEV-1 to SEV-10): coordinator spinner, Ctrl-C orphan subprocess, fast-path error drop, substring misclassification, file descriptor leak, brittle chat_intent.py path injection, routing opacity, SNAPSHOT_KEYWORDS bypass, stale /search endpoint, no retry for transient errors.

nodes.name: Expert Review: Switchboard Routing Layer & llama.cpp Integration
nodes.summary: Inference systems expert review of switchboard routing and llama.cpp integration for Phase 175. Architecture: profile system mostly coherent but one critical inconsistency, switchboard doing too much, stream override logic partially broken. Payload construction: build_llama_payload() bypass in main proxy path (major), enable_thinking in chat_template_kwargs partially correct, frequency_penalty absent from proxy path, token budget leaks at two sites. Streaming: run_id NameError fix correct, remaining closure scope issues, SSE parsing completeness, partial stream handling. Profile analysis: local-tool-calling misconfigured (12000 tokens > n_ctx), continue-local correct, local-agent over-provisioned, coordinator-internal no profile card. Reliability: semaphore under load, circuit breaker absence (SEV-1), timeout chain, silent failures inventory. Top 12 severity findings with code-level locations.

nodes.name: Phase 175 Switchboard run_id NameError Fix
nodes.summary: Post-mortem and fix documentation for the switchboard run_id NameError that caused aq-chat HTTP 500 errors. Root cause: run_id variable referenced in streaming closure before assignment in switchboard proxy path (line 2880). Fix: initialize run_id=None before the streaming block. Includes full error cascade trace, files changed, and post-fix validation steps.

nodes.name: Phase 175-A Implementation Evidence
nodes.summary: Implementation evidence log for Phase 175 Slice A (immediate correctness fixes, no rebuild). Documents 8 fixes: A1 routing substring over-matching in chat_intent.py, A2 collective_memory_search_handler wrong backend (ai_coordination.py:838), A3 context pruner pair integrity in agent_executor.py, A4 shell injection blocklist in local_agent_runtime.py:90, A5 malformed ISO timestamp in agent_executor.py:466, A6 CoT elicitation prefix in agent_executor.py, A7 coordinator streaming to aq-chat, A8 KeyboardInterrupt orphan fix in aq-chat.

nodes.name: Phase 175-B Implementation Evidence
nodes.summary: Implementation evidence log for Phase 175 Slice B (rebuild-required switchboard changes). Documents 5 completed fixes: B1 switchboard proxy() SSOT guards via _apply_local_ssot_guards(), B2 llama.cpp circuit breaker, B3 local-tool-calling token budget reduced 12000→6000, B6 local_agent_runtime.py fallback bypass removed, B8 _sanitize_json control char catch-all. Items B4, B5, B7 deferred.

nodes.name: Phase 175-C Implementation Evidence
nodes.summary: Implementation evidence log for Phase 175 Slice C (feedback loop and collaborative review protocol). Documents 4 completed items: C1 collaborative review board tools added to ai_coordination.py, C2 --from-prd flag added to seed-rag-knowledge.py, C3 WORKFLOW-CANON.md Step 2a review board step added, C5 AIDB seeding from consolidated PRD executed. C4 deferred.

nodes.name: Tool Parity Contract
nodes.summary: JSON configuration defining the canonical tool parity contract for agent runtimes. Specifies schema_version, token budget ceiling, required_tools list (tools all agent runtimes must implement identically), deferred_tools list, deferred_reason, and generated_at timestamp. Used to enforce cross-runtime tool schema consistency referenced in Phase 175 expert reviews.

nodes.name: DEEP-TECHNICAL-ANALYSIS-REPORT.md
nodes.summary: Archived technical analysis of the Pi Harness minimalist runtime and agentic engineering benchmarks. Covers hierarchical context loading via AGENTS.md, branchable JSONL session trees, runtime extensions (jiti), Gherkin intent schemas, dual-layer evaluation (selection vs. execution), token arbitrage, model tiering, software factory patterns, and supply-chain hardening gaps.

nodes.name: DOMAIN-ROLE-MATRIX.md

References docs/architecture/role-matrix.md as SSOT; includes cross-domain rules and per-domain skill loading quick reference.

nodes.name: issues-backlog.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive issue tracking backlog for the NixOS-Dev-Quick-Deploy AI harness, cataloguing bugs, monitoring items, and fixes across dashboard, NixOS/systemd, AppArmor, SOPS secrets, delegation, MACC, and benchmark tooling in states: OPEN, PENDING-REBUILD, IN-FLIGHT, RESOLVED, and DEFERRED.

nodes.name: OPEN ISSUES
nodes.summary: Active unresolved issues including gemini-cli 429 provisioning blocker, codex startup warnings, aidb vector index silent-noop, stagnation guard false positives, and opencode undici crash requiring active investigation or user action.

nodes.name: PENDING-REBUILD
nodes.summary: Issues with declarative NixOS fixes committed but not yet activated, including codex TERMINFO env noise and home-manager insecure Gradio policy block requiring nixos-rebuild or home-manager switch.

nodes.name: IN-FLIGHT
nodes.summary: Issues currently in active progress awaiting completion or verification before closure.

nodes.name: RESOLVED / DONE
nodes.summary: Completed fixed issues spanning dashboard field-name mismatches, SOPS secrets cascade failures, home-dir DAC traversal, intent-routing map permission denial, MACC collaborative planning crashes, AppArmor BPF OOM, delegation token cap errors, coordinator spinner swallowing streaming, and benchmark calibration bugs.

nodes.name: Software Factory Readiness Gaps (Phase 150)
nodes.summary: Readiness gaps catalogued during Phase 150 software factory assessment covering toolchain, workflow, and observability shortfalls identified for the agent harness.

nodes.name: DEFERRED
nodes.summary: Issues deferred pending hardware availability, external vendor resolution, or multi-phase project work; explicitly excluded from self-improvement grep-OPEN slices.

nodes.summary: AI-generated Qwen harness insights from 2026-05-23 reporting zero active traffic, 21,327 rows missing backend labels inflating route_search latency, weak cache efficiency, and unanswered greeting queries with zero relevance score.

nodes.name: agent-roles.yaml
nodes.summary: SSOT YAML config defining five spawnable agent roles (coordinator, coder, reviewer, researcher, planner) with system prompts and tool grants. Loaded by ai-stack/local-agents/agent_spawner.py at startup; Python AGENT_ROLES dict is the fallback.

nodes.name: coordinator
nodes.summary: Coordinator role: orchestrates agent teams, delegates sub-tasks to specialist agents, aggregates and synthesizes results. Tools: shell, file_read, delegate. max_tool_calls: 20.

nodes.name: coder
nodes.summary: Coder role: implements code changes, writes tests, fixes bugs. Follows project conventions, declarative-first for NixOS, no hardcoded secrets. Tools: shell, file_read, file_write, code_execution. max_tool_calls: 15.

nodes.name: reviewer
nodes.summary: Reviewer role (spawnable): checks correctness, security, performance, code quality, and test coverage. Reports PASS/WARN/FAIL verdicts. Tools: shell, file_read, code_execution. max_tool_calls: 10.

nodes.name: researcher
nodes.summary: Researcher role: gathers context from local knowledge base, searches codebase and docs, identifies patterns, summarizes findings with sources. Tools: shell, file_read, file_search. max_tool_calls: 12.

nodes.name: planner
nodes.summary: Planner role: breaks complex tasks into phased plans (Discovery, Implementation, Validation, Deployment) with file lists, commands, rollback plans, and parallelization opportunities. Tools: shell, file_read, file_search. max_tool_calls: 8.

nodes.name: architect.md
nodes.summary: Architect role instruction payload for the Lead Architect. Operates in Orient/Research/Plan phases. Responsibilities: design decisions, risk assessment, PRD generation, blueprint selection. Read-only; evidence-bound; no direct commits.

nodes.name: implementer.md
nodes.summary: Implementer role instruction payload for the Software Engineer. Operates in Execute phase. Responsibilities: code execution per approved plan, unit/integration testing, syntax checks/linting, doc updates. Scope-locked; nsjail for untrusted tools; rollback-ready.

nodes.name: orchestrator.md
nodes.summary: Orchestrator role instruction payload for the Engineering Lead. Manages workflow sessions, delegates to Gemini/Codex/Local/Claude sub-agents via delegate-to-* scripts, maintains PENDING.json and HANDOFF.md, performs final integration commit. Includes delegation decision matrix and session close checklist.

nodes.name: Delegation Decision Matrix
nodes.summary: Matrix mapping task types to preferred delegatees and modes: bounded edits → Gemini (auto_edit/yolo), large refactors → Codex (--prompt-file), RAG/reasoning → Local (direct/agent), high-quality analysis → Claude sub-agent (Agent tool), review gate → Gemini or Claude (--role reviewer).

nodes.name: Session Close Checklist
nodes.summary: Six-step checklist before closing a session or issuing final commit: live test, fix issues, update HANDOFF.md, seed RAG with bug/fix patterns, run tier0-validation-gate.sh --pre-commit, commit with correct format.

nodes.name: reviewer.md
nodes.summary: Reviewer role instruction payload for the Technical Reviewer & Auditor. Operates in Validate/Commit phases. Responsibilities: Tier-0 validation gate, code review (bugs, anti-patterns, security), PASS/FAIL/REVISE verdicts, commit message enforcement. No self-review; evidence-backed verdicts.

nodes.name: sdet.md
nodes.summary: SDET role instruction payload for the Software Development Engineer in Test. Operates alongside the Implementer. Responsibilities: Pytest/Playwright/k6 automation, coverage analysis, chaos testing, performance benchmarking. Test isolation in domain shell; evidence-first reporting.

nodes.name: sre.md
nodes.summary: SRE/DevOps role instruction payload for the Reliability Engineer. Operates in Homeostasis and Emergency loops. Responsibilities: aq-report/aq-qa telemetry monitoring, auto-remediation, NixOS config and service lifecycle management, resource optimization. Declarative-dominance; PRSI audit logging.
