nodes.name: 3p-updates.md
nodes.summary: Template and instructions for writing 3P (Progress, Plans, Problems) status updates targeting executives and leadership. Covers scope clarification, data gathering from Slack/Drive/email/calendar, workflow steps, and a strict single-paragraph-per-section formatting rule for weekly team updates.

nodes.name: company-newsletter.md
nodes.summary: Instructions and example format for composing company-wide newsletter updates. Guides aggregation from Slack, email, calendar, documents, and external press; defines section structure (announcements, priorities, leadership, social), prioritization rules, and emoji-decorated bullet-point formatting for large organizations (1000+ employees).

nodes.name: faq-answers.md
nodes.summary: Instructions for surfacing and answering frequently asked employee questions company-wide. Defines tool sources (Slack reactions, email, docs), Q&A bullet formatting, holistic scope guidance across teams, and answer quality guidelines emphasizing official communications and authoritative links.

nodes.name: general-comms.md
nodes.summary: Fallback template for internal communications that do not fit standard formats (3P updates, newsletters, FAQs). Directs the agent to clarify audience, purpose, tone, and formatting requirements before drafting, applying general clarity and active-voice principles.

nodes.name: evaluation.md
nodes.summary: MCP Server Evaluation Guide: comprehensive reference for generating and running Q&A evaluation datasets against MCP server implementations. Covers question/answer quality guidelines, a 5-step evaluation process, STDIO/SSE/HTTP transport test runners, output formats, and troubleshooting for connection errors, low accuracy, and timeouts.

nodes.name: Question Guidelines
nodes.summary: Guidelines for crafting high-quality evaluation questions: core requirements, complexity/depth expectations, tool testing strategies, and stability criteria for reproducible evaluations.

nodes.name: Answer Guidelines
nodes.summary: Guidelines for evaluation answer quality: verification methods, readability standards, stability requirements, and diversity expectations to ensure robust ground-truth answers.

nodes.name: Evaluation Process
nodes.summary: 5-step evaluation process: documentation inspection, tool inspection, developing understanding, read-only content inspection, and task generation — systematic approach to building MCP evaluations.

nodes.name: Evaluation Examples
nodes.summary: Concrete examples of good vs. poor evaluation questions with rationale, covering tool invocation patterns, expected outputs, and common anti-patterns to avoid.

nodes.name: Running Evaluations
nodes.summary: Operational guide: setup, evaluation file format (JSONL), running against local STDIO, SSE, and streamable HTTP servers, command-line options, output reading, report saving, and complete example workflow.

nodes.name: Complete Example Workflow
nodes.summary: End-to-end walkthrough of the full evaluation cycle: writing questions, running the evaluator, reading results, and iterating on server improvements.

nodes.name: mcp_best_practices.md
nodes.summary: Canonical MCP server best practices reference: naming conventions (server and tool), tool design principles, response format options (JSON/Markdown), pagination patterns, transport selection (stdio vs. streamable HTTP), security practices (auth, input validation, DNS rebinding protection), tool annotations, error handling patterns, testing requirements, and documentation standards.

nodes.name: Tool Naming and Design
nodes.summary: MCP tool naming conventions (snake_case, verb-prefixed) and tool design principles: single responsibility, clear input schemas, deterministic outputs where possible.

nodes.name: Transport Options
nodes.summary: Guidance on MCP transport selection: streamable HTTP for remote/multi-client servers, stdio for local integrations; trade-offs and decision criteria.

nodes.name: Security Best Practices
nodes.summary: Security hardening for MCP servers: authentication/authorization patterns, input validation rules, error handling that avoids leaking internals, and DNS rebinding attack protection.

nodes.name: Pagination
nodes.summary: Pagination patterns for MCP tools returning large result sets: cursor-based pagination, page size limits, and response envelope conventions.

nodes.name: node_mcp_server.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Node.js/TypeScript MCP server implementation guide using the official MCP TypeScript SDK: key imports, server initialization, tool registration with Zod schema validation, response format options, pagination, character limits/truncation, error handling, shared utilities, async/await patterns, TypeScript best practices, package.json/tsconfig configuration, complete working example, advanced features (resource registration, SSE/stdio transports, notifications), code composability, build/run instructions, and quality checklist.

nodes.name: Tool Implementation
nodes.summary: Core tool registration pattern in TypeScript: tool naming conventions, tool handler structure with request/response typing, input schema definition, and output formatting — the primary pattern for defining MCP tools in Node.js.

nodes.name: Zod Schemas for Input Validation
nodes.summary: Using Zod for MCP tool input validation in TypeScript: schema definition patterns, optional/required fields, enum types, nested objects, and integration with MCP tool registration.

nodes.name: Complete Example
nodes.summary: Full working TypeScript MCP server example demonstrating all patterns together: imports, server init, tool registration with Zod, error handling, response formatting, and stdio transport startup.

nodes.name: Advanced MCP Features
nodes.summary: Advanced TypeScript SDK capabilities: resource registration (URI-based resources), streamable HTTP and stdio transport setup, server-sent notification support, and composable module patterns.

nodes.name: TypeScript Best Practices
nodes.summary: TypeScript-specific guidance for MCP servers: strict typing, discriminated unions for tool results, avoiding `any`, utility type usage, and type-safe error propagation.

nodes.name: Package Configuration
nodes.summary: Reference package.json and tsconfig.json configurations for MCP Node.js servers: required dependencies, build scripts, module system settings, and compiler options.

nodes.name: python_mcp_server.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Python MCP server implementation guide using FastMCP: key imports, server initialization, tool registration with Pydantic v2 validation, response format options, pagination, error handling, shared utilities, async/await patterns, type hints, tool docstrings, complete working example, advanced FastMCP features (context injection, resource registration, structured outputs, lifespan management, transport options), code composability, Python-specific best practices, and quality checklist.

nodes.name: Tool Structure with FastMCP
nodes.summary: Core tool registration pattern using FastMCP decorator API: `@mcp.tool()` decorator, function signature as schema, Pydantic type annotations for input validation, and return type handling.

nodes.name: Pydantic v2 Key Features
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 patterns for MCP tool input validation: BaseModel subclassing, Field annotations, validators, model_config, and integration with FastMCP type inference.

nodes.name: Complete Example
nodes.summary: Full working Python FastMCP server example demonstrating all patterns: imports, server init, tool registration with Pydantic models, error handling, async tools, response formatting, and transport startup.

nodes.name: Advanced FastMCP Features
nodes.summary: Advanced FastMCP capabilities: context parameter injection for logging/progress, resource registration (URI-based), structured output types, lifespan management with asynccontextmanager, and streamable HTTP vs. stdio transport configuration.

nodes.name: Context Parameter Injection
nodes.summary: FastMCP context injection: injecting the MCP context object into tool functions for access to logging, progress reporting, client capabilities, and request metadata.

nodes.name: LICENSE.txt
nodes.summary: Anthropic proprietary license governing use of the PDF skill materials. Restricts redistribution, copying, derivative works, sublicensing, and reverse engineering. Grants no rights beyond Anthropic service agreement.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Primary PDF processing skill guide for agents. Covers Python libraries (pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab), CLI tools (pdftotext, qpdf, pdftk), and common tasks: merge/split, text/table extraction, PDF creation, watermarking, OCR, image extraction, and password protection. Entry point that delegates to forms.md and reference.md.

nodes.name: forms.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step agent workflow for filling PDF forms. Covers two paths: fillable-field PDFs (extract field metadata JSON, inspect via images, build field_values.json, run fill script) and non-fillable PDFs (visual analysis, bounding box definition, annotation creation). Enforces strict sequential execution.

nodes.name: reference.md
nodes.summary: Advanced PDF processing reference. Covers pypdfium2 (PDFium binding for fast rendering), JavaScript libraries (pdf-lib for creation/modification, pdfjs-dist for reading/parsing), advanced CLI operations (poppler-utils, qpdf encryption/repair), advanced Python techniques, complex batch workflows, performance optimization, and troubleshooting (encrypted/corrupted PDFs, text extraction issues).

nodes.name: Quick Start
nodes.summary: Minimal pypdf example: load a PDF, count pages, and extract all text in a loop.

nodes.name: pypdf - Basic Operations
nodes.summary: Core pypdf patterns: merge multiple PDFs via PdfWriter, split into per-page files, extract document metadata (title/author/subject/creator), rotate pages. Covers PdfReader/PdfWriter API.

nodes.name: pdfplumber - Text and Table Extraction
nodes.summary: pdfplumber usage for layout-preserving text extraction, simple table extraction per page, and advanced table extraction with pandas to Excel. Covers open/page/extract_text/extract_tables API.

nodes.name: reportlab - Create PDFs
nodes.summary: PDF creation using reportlab: basic canvas with text and lines, multi-page document template using Platypus (SimpleDocTemplate, Paragraph, Spacer, PageBreak, getSampleStyleSheet).

nodes.name: Command-Line Tools
nodes.summary: CLI-based PDF operations: pdftotext for text extraction with layout preservation, qpdf for merge/split/rotate/decrypt, pdftk for merge/split/rotate/repair (when available).

nodes.name: Common Tasks
nodes.summary: Recipes for frequent PDF operations: OCR text extraction from scanned PDFs (pytesseract + pdf2image), watermark overlay with pypdf, image extraction with pdfplumber, and password protection with PdfWriter.encrypt().

nodes.name: Fillable Fields Path
nodes.summary: Workflow for fillable PDF forms: run check_fillable_fields script, extract field metadata JSON (field_id, page, rect, type, checkbox/radio/choice options), convert PDF pages to PNGs for visual inspection, create field_values.json, run fill_fillable_fields.py to produce output PDF.

nodes.name: Non-Fillable Fields Path
nodes.summary: Workflow for non-fillable PDFs requiring visual analysis and text annotation. Enforces strict sequential steps: visual analysis, fields.json + validation images creation, bounding box validation (automated intersection check + manual image inspection), and annotation application.

nodes.name: Step 1: Visual Analysis
nodes.summary: Required first step for non-fillable forms: convert PDF to images and visually identify each form field's type, label, position, and expected value format before writing any code.

nodes.name: Step 2: Create fields.json and validation images
nodes.summary: Create fields.json defining each field's bounding box in PDF coordinates, and generate validation overlay images to verify placement accuracy before proceeding.

nodes.name: Step 3: Validate Bounding Boxes
nodes.summary: Validate bounding boxes with automated intersection check (detect overlapping fields) and manual image inspection before applying annotations.

nodes.name: pypdfium2 Library
nodes.summary: pypdfium2 Python binding for PDFium (Chromium's PDF engine). Covers high-resolution page rendering to PIL images at configurable scale, and text extraction per page. Positioned as PyMuPDF replacement with Apache/BSD license.

nodes.name: pdf-lib (JavaScript)
nodes.summary: pdf-lib JavaScript library patterns: load and manipulate existing PDFs (add pages, text, images, metadata), create complex PDFs from scratch with tables and layout, and advanced merge/split operations across PDF documents.

nodes.name: pdfjs-dist (JavaScript)
nodes.summary: Mozilla PDF.js (pdfjs-dist) patterns: load and render PDF pages to canvas, extract text with coordinates, and extract annotations and form field data. Apache licensed.

nodes.name: Advanced Command-Line Operations
nodes.summary: Advanced poppler-utils usage (text with bounding box coordinates, high-quality image conversion, embedded image extraction) and advanced qpdf operations (complex page manipulation, optimization/repair, advanced encryption with user/owner passwords).

nodes.name: Advanced Python Techniques
nodes.summary: Advanced pdfplumber usage with precise coordinate extraction and custom table extraction settings, plus reportlab advanced features for creating professional reports with tables using TableStyle.

nodes.name: Complex Workflows
nodes.summary: End-to-end workflows: figure/image extraction (pdfimages CLI or pypdfium2+PIL), batch PDF processing with error handling and per-file output tracking, and advanced PDF cropping with coordinate transformation.

nodes.name: Performance Optimization Tips
nodes.summary: PDF processing performance tips: lazy loading for large PDFs, pdfplumber text vs. character extraction trade-offs, pypdfium2 scale optimization for images, form filling strategies, and memory management with context managers and garbage collection.

nodes.name: Troubleshooting Common Issues
nodes.summary: Diagnosis and resolution for: encrypted PDFs (password handling, decryption scripts), corrupted PDFs (qpdf --qdf reconstruction), and text extraction issues (scanned PDF detection, OCR fallback strategy).

nodes.name: LICENSE.txt
nodes.summary: License file governing the pptx skill module — defines usage rights for the PPTX creation and editing skill assets.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Primary agent skill reference for PPTX creation, editing, and analysis — covers design principles, color palettes, layout tips, workflow for template and non-template presentation creation, thumbnail grid generation, slide-to-image conversion, code style guidelines, and dependencies.

nodes.name: Creating a new PowerPoint presentation without a template
nodes.summary: Workflow and design principles for building PowerPoint presentations from scratch — covers color palette selection, visual detail options, layout tips, and step-by-step creation workflow without a base template. Spans lines 47–171 (124 lines).

nodes.name: Editing an existing PowerPoint presentation
nodes.summary: Workflow for modifying existing PPTX files — provides the procedural steps for safe editing of existing presentations. Spans lines 171–182 (11 lines).

nodes.name: Creating a new PowerPoint presentation using a template
nodes.summary: Comprehensive workflow for template-based PowerPoint creation — the largest skill section covering slide duplication, placeholder management, OOXML manipulation, and template-safe operations. Spans lines 182–408 (226 lines).

nodes.name: Creating Thumbnail Grids
nodes.summary: Instructions for generating thumbnail grid layouts from PowerPoint slides — covers grid composition and image arrangement patterns. Spans lines 408–440 (32 lines).

nodes.name: Converting Slides to Images
nodes.summary: Guidance for converting PowerPoint slides to image formats (PNG/JPG) for downstream use. Spans lines 440–467 (27 lines).

nodes.name: Dependencies
nodes.summary: Lists Python and system dependencies required by the pptx skill — python-pptx, PptxGenJS, html2pptx, and related packages. Spans lines 473–484 (11 lines).

nodes.name: html2pptx.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide for HTML-to-PowerPoint conversion — covers HTML slide authoring (layout dimensions, supported elements, text rules, styling, shapes, icons/gradients), html2pptx library API reference with validation and placeholder handling, and PptxGenJS usage for images, text, shapes, charts (scatter/line/pie/multi-series), and tables.

nodes.name: Creating HTML Slides
nodes.summary: Authoring guide for HTML slides used as PowerPoint input — covers layout dimensions (914×514pt), supported HTML elements, critical text rules, styling properties, DIV shape styling, icons, gradients, and a full slide example. Spans lines 13–183 (170 lines).

nodes.name: Using the html2pptx Library
nodes.summary: API reference and usage guide for the html2pptx Python library — covers installation, basic usage, function signature, parameters, return values, validation, placeholder handling, and a complete working example. Spans lines 183–302 (119 lines).

nodes.name: Using PptxGenJS
nodes.summary: PptxGenJS usage guide covering critical rules (color formats), adding images, text, shapes, charts (scatter/line/pie/multi-series with chart colors), and tables (basic/formatted/merged cells/table options). Spans lines 302–625 (323 lines).

nodes.name: ooxml.md
nodes.summary: Office Open XML technical reference for PowerPoint — covers schema compliance, basic slide structure, text box / shape XML, text formatting, lists, shapes, images, tables, slide layouts, file update procedures (zipfile manipulation), slide CRUD operations, common XML errors, and template validation checklists.

nodes.name: Technical Guidelines
nodes.summary: Schema compliance rules for OOXML — defines required XML namespaces, element ordering constraints, and schema validation rules for valid PowerPoint files. Spans lines 5–15 (10 lines).

nodes.name: Presentation Structure
nodes.summary: Core XML markup reference for PowerPoint slide content — covers basic slide structure, text box/shape XML, text formatting (bold/italic/color/size), lists (bulleted/numbered), shape types, image embedding (blipFill/pic), and table XML (tbl/tr/tc elements). Spans lines 15–315 (300 lines).

nodes.name: File Updates
nodes.summary: Procedures for updating PPTX files via Python zipfile manipulation — covers reading/modifying/writing XML parts within the ZIP archive while preserving relationships. Spans lines 315–353 (38 lines).

nodes.name: Slide Operations
nodes.summary: CRUD operations for slides in OOXML format — adding new slides, duplicating slides (with relationship copying), reordering via presentation.xml sldIdLst manipulation, and safe slide deletion. Spans lines 353–405 (52 lines).

nodes.name: Validation Checklist for Template-Based Presentations
nodes.summary: Pre-pack validation checklist for template-based PowerPoint files — lists required checks before zipping (relationship integrity, unique slide IDs, sldIdLst ordering) and common template duplication pitfalls. Spans lines 413–427 (14 lines).

nodes.name: dml-chart.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 DrawingML Chart schema (1499 lines). Defines complex types for chart elements: bar, line, pie, scatter, bubble, surface, stock, radar, and area charts; series definitions; value/category/date axes; data labels; legend; chart space container; and chart title.

nodes.name: CT_Chart
nodes.summary: Root chart complex type containing plot area, title, legend, data table, and display properties controlling auto-titling and 3D perspective.

nodes.name: CT_PlotArea
nodes.summary: Plot area container holding the chart series type groups, axis definitions, layout, and data table for all supported chart types.

nodes.name: CT_DLbls
nodes.summary: Data labels complex type controlling label position, number format, separator, show-category/series/value/percent/leader-lines flags, and individual label overrides.

nodes.name: dml-chartDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 DrawingML Chart Drawing schema (146 lines). Defines absolute and relative anchor types for positioning shapes, connectors, and pictures embedded inside a chart canvas.

nodes.name: dml-diagram.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 DrawingML Diagram (SmartArt) schema (1085 lines). Defines types for diagram data models, layout definitions, style definitions, color transforms, and the algorithm-driven shape arrangement system used in SmartArt graphics.

nodes.name: CT_DiagramDefinition
nodes.summary: Diagram layout definition complex type specifying shape arrangement algorithms (linear, cycle, hierarchy, pyramid, etc.) and presentation constraints for SmartArt.

nodes.name: CT_DataModel
nodes.summary: Diagram data model complex type holding point tree (nodes) and connection list (edges) that populate SmartArt shapes with content.

nodes.name: dml-lockedCanvas.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 DrawingML Locked Canvas schema (11 lines). Minimal wrapper schema defining the lockedCanvas element that encapsulates grouped drawing shapes and prevents interactive editing.

nodes.name: dml-main.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 core DrawingML schema (3081 lines). Foundational type library for all OOXML drawing: color models (RGB/HSL/scheme/system/preset), font/typeface, shape geometry presets, line and fill styles, visual effects, text body and paragraph formatting, theme definitions, and 3D scene settings.

nodes.name: CT_Color
nodes.summary: Color model type group supporting sRGB, HSL, scheme colors, system colors, and preset colors with transform modifiers (tint, shade, alpha, luminance, hue).

nodes.name: CT_ShapeProperties
nodes.summary: Shape visual properties complex type combining geometry (preset or custom path), fill (solid/gradient/blip/pattern/none), line style, effect list, and optional 3D scene settings.

nodes.name: CT_TextBody
nodes.summary: Text body container complex type with body properties (anchoring, wrap, overflow, columns), list style, and one-or-more paragraph/run definitions including character properties.

nodes.name: CT_EffectList
nodes.summary: Effect list type grouping composited visual effects applied to drawing elements: outer-shadow, inner-shadow, glow, reflection, soft-edge, and blur.

nodes.name: dml-picture.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 DrawingML Picture schema (23 lines). Defines CT_Picture complex type combining non-visual picture properties, shape properties, and blip fill referencing embedded or linked image data.

nodes.name: dml-spreadsheetDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 DrawingML Spreadsheet Drawing schema (185 lines). Defines absolute, one-cell, and two-cell anchor types for positioning shapes, charts, and pictures on Excel worksheets using cell-coordinate offsets.

nodes.name: dml-wordprocessingDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 DrawingML Word Processing Drawing schema (287 lines). Defines inline and floating (anchor) drawing placements within Word document text flow, with text wrapping mode, polygon, distance margins, and position references.

nodes.name: pml.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 Presentation Markup Language schema (1676 lines). Full PowerPoint structure: presentations, slide lists, slide masters, slide layouts, note masters, handout masters, transitions, animations (timing/time node trees), and build/click sequences.

nodes.name: CT_Presentation
nodes.summary: Root presentation complex type containing slide ID list, slide master list, notes master reference, handout master reference, slide size, notes size, embedded fonts, and custom shows.

nodes.name: CT_Slide
nodes.summary: Slide complex type with common slide data (shape tree containing sp, grpSp, cxnSp, pic, graphicFrame elements), customer data extensions, and slide-specific show/hide properties.

nodes.name: CT_SlideLayout
nodes.summary: Slide layout complex type inheriting from slide master, defining named placeholder arrangement patterns (title, content, two-column, blank, picture-with-caption, etc.).

nodes.name: CT_Transition
nodes.summary: Slide transition complex type defining transition effect type (blinds/checker/dissolve/fade/fly/strips/wipe/zoom/etc.), speed, optional sound, and advance-on-click/time conditions.

nodes.name: shared-additionalCharacteristics.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 shared additional characteristics schema (28 lines). Defines characteristic name/value/relation/vocabulary/grammar descriptors for document capability declaration.

nodes.name: shared-bibliography.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 shared bibliography schema (144 lines). Defines CT_SourceType and source field complex types for books, book sections, journals, web sites, interviews, film, and other reference materials.

nodes.name: shared-commonSimpleTypes.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 shared common simple types (174 lines). Base XSD constraints including ST_Percentage, ST_Angle, ST_Coordinate, ST_PositiveCoordinate, ST_TwipsMeasure, ST_String255, and boolean toggle type used across all OOXML schemas.

nodes.name: ST_Percentage group
nodes.summary: Foundational measurement simple types: ST_Percentage (thousandths of a percent), ST_Angle (60000ths of a degree), ST_Coordinate (EMU), ST_PositiveCoordinate, ST_TwipsMeasure — referenced by all OOXML schemas.

nodes.name: shared-customXmlDataProperties.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 shared custom XML data binding properties schema (25 lines). Defines CT_CustomXmlElement with dataBinding element linking to an XML data store using XPath and storeItemID.

nodes.name: shared-customXmlSchemaProperties.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 shared custom XML schema properties schema (18 lines). Associates external XML namespace schemas with embedded XML data islands via schema reference list.

nodes.name: shared-documentPropertiesCustom.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 shared custom document properties schema (59 lines). Defines CT_CustomProperties with named property bag entries typed by variant values for user-defined document metadata fields.

nodes.name: shared-documentPropertiesExtended.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 shared extended document properties schema (56 lines). Defines CT_ExtendedProperties with title, subject, author, keywords, last-modified-by, revision, total editing time, and character/word/line/page counts.

nodes.name: shared-documentPropertiesVariantTypes.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 shared variant types schema (195 lines). Defines VARIANT discriminated union and VT_* scalar types (bool, i1, i2, i4, i8, r4, r8, decimal, lpstr, lpwstr, bstr, date, blob, array, empty, null, stream, storage) for typed property values.

nodes.name: CT_Variant
nodes.summary: Variant complex type as a discriminated union (choice) over all VT_* primitive types enabling heterogeneous typed storage of document property values.

nodes.name: shared-math.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 shared math schema (OMML — Office Math Markup Language, 582 lines). Defines complex types for mathematical expressions: fractions, radicals, integrals, matrices, delimiters, super/subscripts, n-ary operators, equation arrays, limit/log functions, and run properties.

nodes.name: CT_OMath
nodes.summary: Office Math expression complex type — root container for inline math with math runs (CT_R), fractions (CT_F), radicals (CT_Rad), and all other math element types.

nodes.name: CT_OMathPara
nodes.summary: Math paragraph complex type grouping one or more CT_OMath display-mode expressions with horizontal alignment and inter-equation spacing properties.

nodes.name: shared-relationshipReference.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 shared relationship reference schema (25 lines). Defines the ST_RelationshipId simple type (xs:string) used as the r:id attribute to reference Open Packaging Convention relationships across all OOXML parts.

nodes.name: sml.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO-IEC 29500-4 Spreadsheet Markup Language schema (4439 lines). Comprehensive Excel workbook schema defining workbooks, worksheets, rows, cells, formulas, shared strings, styles, defined names, conditional formatting rules, data validation, auto-filters, pivot tables, and calculation chain.

nodes.name: CT_Workbook
nodes.summary: Workbook root complex type containing sheet reference list, defined names, calculation properties (iterative calc, precision settings), workbook view, pivot cache references, and workbook-level protection.

nodes.name: CT_Worksheet
nodes.summary: Worksheet complex type containing sheetData (row/cell tree), column definitions, dimension, merged cells, conditional formatting, data validation, auto-filter, sort state, and drawing/chart references.

nodes.name: CT_Cell
nodes.summary: Cell complex type with value (v), formula (f), inline string (is), data type attribute (t: b/d/e/inlineStr/n/s/str), style index (s), and show-phonetic attribute.

nodes.name: CT_StyleSheet
nodes.summary: Style sheet complex type aggregating fonts, fills, borders, number formats (numFmts), cell formats (xfs), cell styles, differential formats (dxfs), and table styles.

nodes.name: vml-main.xsd
nodes.summary: Core VML (Vector Markup Language) schema for OOXML ISO/IEC 29500-4. Defines attribute groups for VML shapes (AG_CoreAttributes, AG_ShapeAttributes, AG_OfficeCoreAttributes), shape/drawing element types, and simple types like ST_Ext. Imports all other VML sub-schemas and wml.xsd.

nodes.name: AG_CoreAttributes
nodes.summary: Exported attributeGroup combining AG_Id and AG_Style with href, target, class, title, alt, coordsize, coordorigin, wrapcoords, and print attributes. Forms the base attribute contract for all VML shape elements.

nodes.name: AG_ShapeAttributes
nodes.summary: Exported attributeGroup extending AG_Chromakey and AG_Fill with opacity, stroked, strokecolor, strokeweight, and insetpen. Governs visual stroke and fill properties of VML shapes.

nodes.name: AG_OfficeCoreAttributes
nodes.summary: Exported attributeGroup referencing office-namespace attributes (o:spid, o:oned, o:regroupid, o:doubleclicknotify, o:button, o:userhidden, o:bullet). Bridges VML core and office-drawing extension namespaces.

nodes.name: vml-officeDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: Office drawing extension for VML in the urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:office namespace. Defines 50+ top-level attributes (bwmode, connectortype, spid, dgmlayout, etc.) and complex types for B/W rendering, connector behavior, OLE objects, and diagram layout. Imports vml-main.xsd (circular).

nodes.name: vml-presentationDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: Minimal VML extension for PowerPoint presentation drawing in the urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:powerpoint namespace. Exports two elements (iscomment, textdata) and two complex types (CT_Empty, CT_Rel). No imports from this batch.

nodes.name: vml-spreadsheetDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: VML extension for Excel spreadsheet drawing in the urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:excel namespace. Exports the ClientData element and CT_ClientData complex type containing 60+ child element choices for form controls, anchoring, scripting. Imports shared-commonSimpleTypes.xsd.

nodes.name: CT_ClientData
nodes.summary: Exported complexType for Excel embedded control data. Contains an unbounded choice of 60+ elements (MoveWithCells, SizeWithCells, Anchor, Locked, PrintObject, FmlaMacro, TextHAlign, ScriptText, Val, Min, Max, etc.) and a required ObjectType attribute.

nodes.name: ST_ObjectType
nodes.summary: Exported simpleType enumerating 19 Excel embedded object types: Button, Checkbox, Dialog, Drop, Edit, GBox, Label, LineA, List, Movie, Note, Pict, Radio, RectA, Scroll, Spin, Shape, Group, Rect.

nodes.name: vml-wordprocessingDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: VML extension for Word document drawing in the urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:word namespace. Defines border elements (bordertop/left/right/bottom), text wrap (wrap), anchor lock, and associated simple type enumerations. No imports from this batch.

nodes.name: CT_Border
nodes.summary: Exported complexType for VML border styling in Word. Carries type (ST_BorderType), width (positiveInteger), and shadow (ST_BorderShadow) attributes. Used by bordertop/borderleft/borderright/borderbottom elements.

nodes.name: CT_Wrap
nodes.summary: Exported complexType controlling text wrapping around VML shapes in Word. Attributes: type (ST_WrapType), side (ST_WrapSide), anchorx (ST_HorizontalAnchor), anchory (ST_VerticalAnchor).

nodes.name: ST_BorderType
nodes.summary: Exported simpleType enumerating 26 Word border styles: none, single, thick, double, hairline, dot, dash, dotDash, dashDotDot, triple, thinThickSmall, thickBetweenThinSmall, wave, doubleWave, HTMLOutset, HTMLInset, and more.

nodes.name: wml.xsd
nodes.summary: Primary WordprocessingML schema (3646 lines) for OOXML ISO/IEC 29500-4. Defines the full document model: paragraph, run, table, section, style, numbering, header/footer, field, bookmark, comment, revision tracking, and hundreds of complex/simple types. Imports xml.xsd, math, relationships, drawing, and shared type schemas.

nodes.name: CT_OnOff
nodes.summary: Exported complexType representing a boolean on/off toggle. Single optional val attribute of type s:ST_OnOff. Used pervasively across WML for feature flags (bold, italic, hidden, etc.).

nodes.name: CT_Charset
nodes.summary: Exported complexType for character set encoding. Attributes: val (ST_UcharHexNumber, 1-byte hex), characterSet (ST_String, defaults to ISO-8859-1). Used in font definitions.

nodes.name: CT_DecimalNumber
nodes.summary: Exported complexType wrapping a required ST_DecimalNumber (xsd:integer) in a val attribute. Canonical WML pattern for numeric properties like heading levels, list item counts, and column widths.

nodes.name: xml.xsd
nodes.summary: W3C canonical XML namespace schema (http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace). Defines xml:lang, xml:space, xml:base attributes and the specialAttrs attributeGroup. Imported by WML and other schemas requiring language or whitespace control.

nodes.name: specialAttrs
nodes.summary: Exported attributeGroup bundling xml:base, xml:lang, and xml:space for use by any schema needing standard XML namespace attributes. Imported by wml.xsd for element-level language and whitespace tagging.

nodes.name: opc-contentTypes.xsd
nodes.summary: ECMA-376 OPC XSD schema defining content-type mappings (Default and Override) for package parts. Validates MIME-type strings and file extension patterns used in the [Content_Types].xml file of OOXML packages.

nodes.name: opc-coreProperties.xsd
nodes.summary: ECMA-376 OPC XSD schema for package core metadata properties, importing Dublin Core (dc) and Dublin Core Terms (dcterms) to define creator, title, subject, description, timestamps, revision, and keyword fields.

nodes.name: opc-digSig.xsd
nodes.summary: ECMA-376 OPC XSD schema for digital signature elements including SignatureTime (with complex datetime regex patterns), RelationshipReference, and RelationshipsGroupReference for package signing.

nodes.name: opc-relationships.xsd
nodes.summary: ECMA-376 OPC XSD schema defining part-to-part relationships within a package: Relationship elements carry Target URI, Type URI, unique Id, and optional TargetMode (Internal or External).

nodes.name: wml-2010.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2010 WML extension schema (targetNamespace: schemas.microsoft.com/office/word/2010/wordml). Extends core OOXML WML with conflict-tracking elements, DrawingML-style color/gradient/fill types, 3D text effects (scene3d, props3d, glow, shadow, reflection, textOutline, textFill), OpenType typography (ligatures, numForm, numSpacing, stylisticSets), checkbox SDT controls, and image settings. Imports wml.xsd and references DrawingML shared types.

nodes.name: wml-2012.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2012 WML extension schema (targetNamespace: schemas.microsoft.com/office/word/2012/wordml). Adds collaborative authoring support: extended comments with threading (CT_CommentsEx/CT_CommentEx), people/presence tracking (CT_People/CT_Person/CT_PresenceInfo), repeating section SDT, GUID type, SDT appearance control, and extended document identifiers.

nodes.name: wml-2018.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2018 WML extension schema (targetNamespace: schemas.microsoft.com/office/word/2018/wordml). Minimal base for extensibility: defines CT_Extension (lax wildcard + URI attribute) and CT_ExtensionList (sequence of CT_Extension). Used as a dependency by wml-cex-2018.xsd.

nodes.name: wml-cex-2018.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2018 Comments Extensible schema (targetNamespace: .../wordml/cex). Defines CT_CommentsExtensible and CT_CommentExtensible, enabling extensible comment metadata (durableId, dateUtc, intelligentPlaceholder). Imports wml-2018.xsd for CT_ExtensionList and wml.xsd for base types.

nodes.name: wml-cid-2016.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2016 Comment IDs schema (targetNamespace: .../wordml/cid). Maps paragraph IDs (paraId) to durable comment IDs via CT_CommentId; container CT_CommentsIds holds unbounded CT_CommentId entries. Enables stable comment anchoring across edits.

nodes.name: wml-sdtdatahash-2020.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2020 SDT Data Hash schema (targetNamespace: .../sdtdatahash). Minimal: declares a single global attribute storeItemChecksum (type ST_String) for verifying structured document tag data binding integrity.

nodes.name: wml-symex-2015.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2015 Symbol Extension schema (targetNamespace: .../wordml/symex). Defines CT_SymEx with font (ST_String) and char (ST_LongHexNumber) attributes, and a top-level symEx element for extended symbol references beyond the base wml sym element.

nodes.name: CT_Color
nodes.summary: Color type wrapping EG_ColorChoice group — allows either srgbClr (CT_SRgbColor with hex value + transforms) or schemeClr (CT_SchemeColor with theme palette value + transforms). Foundation for fill, glow, shadow, and outline color specifications.

nodes.name: CT_GradientFillProperties
nodes.summary: Gradient fill type with a gradient stop list (CT_GradientStopList, min 2 stops) and optional shade properties (linear or path shade via EG_ShadeProperties group). Used in text fill effects.

nodes.name: CT_Scene3D
nodes.summary: 3D scene container requiring a CT_Camera (preset camera type) and CT_LightRig (light rig type + direction, optional rotation). Used in EG_RPrTextEffects to apply 3D rendering to run-level text.

nodes.name: CT_Props3D
nodes.summary: 3D text properties: optional top/bottom bevels (CT_Bevel), extrusion color, contour color, extrusion height, contour width, and preset material type. Applied per-run for 3D text rendering.

nodes.name: CT_Shadow
nodes.summary: Drop/outer shadow effect with color (EG_ColorChoice), blur radius, distance, direction, x/y scale, x/y skew angles, and alignment. Used in EG_RPrTextEffects for run-level shadow effects.

nodes.name: CT_Reflection
nodes.summary: Text reflection effect with configurable alpha fade (start/end), position, distance, direction, fade direction, scale, skew, and alignment. No color child — uses alpha transparency of the text itself.

nodes.name: CT_TextOutlineEffect
nodes.summary: Text stroke/outline properties: optional fill (EG_FillProperties), dash pattern (EG_LineDashProperties), join style (EG_LineJoinProperties), line width, cap style, compound type, and pen alignment.

nodes.name: EG_RPrTextEffects
nodes.summary: Group aggregating all run-level (rPr) text visual effects: glow, shadow, reflection, textOutline, textFill, scene3d, props3d — all optional. This is the extension point for rich text rendering in Word 2010+ documents.

nodes.name: CT_SdtCheckbox
nodes.summary: Structured document tag checkbox control: optional checked state (CT_OnOff), checked symbol (CT_SdtCheckboxSymbol with font + hex glyph), and unchecked symbol. Exposes the checkbox element.

nodes.name: ST_PresetCameraType
nodes.summary: Enumeration of 3D preset camera positions: legacy oblique/perspective variants (18 each), orthographic, isometric (18), oblique (8), and perspective (14 values). Restricts CT_Camera.prst attribute.

nodes.name: EG_RPrOpenType
nodes.summary: Group for OpenType typography run properties: ligatures (CT_Ligatures), numForm (lining/oldStyle), numSpacing (proportional/tabular), stylisticSets (CT_StylisticSets with up to N styleSet entries), and contextual alternates flag.

nodes.name: CT_LightRig
nodes.summary: 3D light rig: required rig type (ST_LightRigType enum — 28 presets) and direction (8-point compass), optional rotation (CT_SphereCoords with lat/lon/rev angles).

nodes.name: CT_CommentsEx
nodes.summary: Extended comments root element container (commentsEx). Holds unbounded sequence of CT_CommentEx entries. Enables comment threading and done-state tracking introduced in Word 2013.

nodes.name: CT_CommentEx
nodes.summary: Extended comment attributes: required paraId (paragraph anchor), optional paraIdParent (for threading / reply chain), and optional done flag (ST_OnOff). Links to base w:comment via paraId.

nodes.name: CT_People
nodes.summary: People tracking container (people element). Holds unbounded CT_Person entries representing document collaborators with author name and optional presence/identity info.

nodes.name: CT_Person
nodes.summary: Document collaborator: required author name (ST_String) and optional presenceInfo child (CT_PresenceInfo with providerId + userId strings for identity provider integration).

nodes.name: CT_SdtRepeatedSection
nodes.summary: Repeating section SDT control properties: optional sectionTitle (display name) and doNotAllowInsertDeleteSection flag. Supports repeating content controls introduced in Word 2013.

nodes.name: CT_Extension
nodes.summary: Generic extension container: lax-processed wildcard child content plus a URI attribute (xsd:token) identifying the extension namespace. Base building block for future Word schema extensions.

nodes.name: CT_ExtensionList
nodes.summary: Container for zero or more CT_Extension entries. Used as extLst in 2018+ schemas to allow forward-compatible schema extension without breaking existing parsers.

nodes.name: CT_CommentsExtensible
nodes.summary: Root element type for commentsExtensible part. Holds unbounded CT_CommentExtensible entries and an optional CT_ExtensionList for forward compatibility.

nodes.name: CT_CommentExtensible
nodes.summary: Extensible comment entry: required durableId (ST_LongHexNumber), optional UTC timestamp (dateUtc), intelligentPlaceholder flag (ST_OnOff), and optional extLst. Provides stable cross-version comment identity.

nodes.name: CT_CommentsIds
nodes.summary: Container for the commentsIds part: unbounded sequence of CT_CommentId entries mapping paragraph anchor IDs to durable comment IDs for stable comment location across document edits.

nodes.name: CT_CommentId
nodes.summary: Single comment ID mapping: required paraId (ST_LongHexNumber — paragraph anchor) and durableId (ST_LongHexNumber — stable cross-session comment identifier). Enables robust comment re-association.

nodes.name: CT_SymEx
nodes.summary: Extended symbol reference: optional font name (ST_String) and character code (ST_LongHexNumber). Exposes symEx element allowing symbol specification beyond the base w:sym element capabilities.

nodes.name: LICENSE.txt
nodes.summary: License text for the slack-gif-creator skill (201 lines). Governs usage terms for the skill package.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for creating Slack-optimized animated GIFs using PIL/Pillow. Documents Slack constraints (128x128 emoji, 480x480 message, 10-30 FPS, 48-128 colors), core GIFBuilder workflow, available utilities (GIFBuilder, Validators, Easing, FrameComposer), and 8 animation concepts. Includes optimization strategies and design philosophy.

nodes.name: Slack Requirements
nodes.summary: Defines Slack GIF constraints: emoji 128x128, message 480x480, FPS 10-30, colors 48-128, emoji duration under 3 seconds.

nodes.name: Core Workflow
nodes.summary: Three-step GIF creation workflow: instantiate GIFBuilder, generate PIL Image frames in a loop, save with optimization flags (num_colors, optimize_for_emoji).

nodes.name: Drawing Graphics
nodes.summary: Guidance for rendering graphics: handling user-uploaded images via PIL Image.open, drawing primitives (ellipse, polygon, line, rectangle), and polishing tips (thick lines, gradients, layered shapes, vibrant colors).

nodes.name: Available Utilities
nodes.summary: Documents four core modules: GIFBuilder (frame assembly + Slack optimization), Validators (validate_gif, is_slack_ready), Easing (interpolate with 7 easing types), FrameComposer (create_blank_frame, create_gradient_background, draw_circle, draw_text, draw_star).

nodes.name: Animation Concepts
nodes.summary: Eight animation patterns with implementation guidance: Shake/Vibrate (sin/cos oscillation), Pulse/Heartbeat (sine wave scaling), Bounce (bounce_out easing + gravity), Spin/Rotate (PIL rotate + wobble), Fade In/Out (RGBA alpha blend), Slide (ease_out/back_out), Zoom (scale + crop), Explode/Particle Burst (radial particles with gravity + fade).

nodes.name: Optimization Strategies
nodes.summary: Five GIF size reduction strategies: fewer frames (lower FPS), fewer colors (num_colors=48), smaller dimensions (128x128), remove duplicate frames, emoji optimization mode. Apply only when size reduction is explicitly requested.

nodes.name: Philosophy
nodes.summary: Skill design principles: provides knowledge + utilities + flexibility via PIL primitives; explicitly excludes rigid templates, emoji font rendering, and pre-packaged graphics. Encourages combining animation concepts creatively.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependencies for slack-gif-creator skill: pillow>=10.0.0, imageio>=2.31.0, imageio-ffmpeg>=0.4.9, numpy>=1.24.0.

nodes.name: AUTONOMOUS-AGENT-HANDOFF-PROMPT.md
nodes.summary: Handoff prompt enabling a local agent to take over autonomous roadmap execution. Covers 3-phase mission (roadmap completion, continuous improvement loop, proactive monitoring), execution pattern with DelegatedTask protocol, safety guardrails (3 tiers: can/approval-required/cannot), circuit breakers, rollback procedure, and success criteria.

nodes.name: AUTONOMOUS-AGENT-SECURITY-POLICY.md
nodes.summary: Security policy v1.0.0 governing autonomous agent operations. Defines autonomous mode (default, restricted) vs interactive mode, restricts computer-use tools, limits file ops to repo paths, enforces shell command allowlist, sandboxes code execution (30s/256MB), limits network to localhost/Anthropic/OpenRouter. Specifies circuit breakers, enforcement via policy file and coordinator integration, audit logging.

nodes.name: AUTONOMOUS-WORKFLOW-COORDINATOR-PROMPT.md
nodes.summary: Role-definition prompt for the Workflow Coordinator agent. Coordinator keeps work flowing without planning or implementing directly — delegates planning/implementation to Claude. Core loop: identify → trigger-plan → slice-tasks → delegate → update-progress → repeat. Includes complete autonomous loop script, monitoring commands, success criteria, and escalation rules.

nodes.name: LOCAL-AGENT-CLI-COORDINATOR-PROMPT.md
nodes.summary: CLI-based workflow coordinator prompt for local agent orchestration without API keys. Enforces search-first rule, mandates aq-delegate over bare delegation, defines routing matrix (local/constrained vs remote/larger-context lanes), documents harness CLI tools (aq-delegate, aq-hints, aq-qa, aq-report), and provides complete coordinator shell script.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Directory contract for .agents/. Plans go in .agents/plans/, command specs in .claude/commands/, PRD/rules/workflow state in .agent/. Documents the layout with plans/README.md, phase-template.md, and per-phase plan files.

nodes.name: Safety Guardrails
nodes.summary: 3-tier permission model: autonomous can-do actions (git ops, file read/write in repo, delegate tasks, run QA, monitor health), actions requiring human approval (nixos-rebuild, service restarts, git push, env-var changes), and hard-blocked actions (delete /run/secrets, modify /etc, execute arbitrary net commands, bypass security policy).

nodes.name: Execution Pattern
nodes.summary: Per-batch execution pattern using AutonomousOrchestrator Python API: read roadmap → identify pending batches → create DelegatedTask per subtask → delegate to Claude → verify with VerificationFramework → approve via ApprovalWorkflow → commit. Covers ApprovalMode, TaskType, AgentPreference imports.

nodes.name: Circuit Breakers
nodes.summary: Automatic halts on: 3+ consecutive task failures, >20% regression in quality score, unexpected system state changes, security policy violations. Trigger orchestrator halt and notify human.

nodes.name: Rollback Procedure
nodes.summary: Git-based rollback: identify failing commit, revert with git revert, run aq-qa to confirm stability, notify human of rollback. Used when circuit breakers fire on quality regression.

nodes.name: Phase 2: Continuous Improvement Loop
nodes.summary: Post-roadmap autonomous loop: analyze system quality → identify gaps → create improvement plan → delegate to Claude → verify/approve → commit → run QA → measure metrics → self-assess until quality_score >= 0.95 and all systems optimal.

nodes.name: Autonomous Mode Restrictions
nodes.summary: Computer use disabled (mouse/keyboard/screenshot blocked). File ops restricted to repo paths (.agents/, .claude/, ai-stack/, scripts/ai/, docs/, nix/modules/); blocks /etc/, /var/, /run/secrets/, ~/.ssh/, ~/.gnupg/. Shell allowlist: git, grep/find/ls/cat, python3/bash/node, systemctl status (read-only), aq-* tools. Code sandboxed 30s/256MB. Network limited to localhost, api.anthropic.com, openrouter.ai.

nodes.name: Circuit Breakers (Security)
nodes.summary: Security-level circuit breakers that halt autonomous execution: blocked path access attempt, blocked command execution attempt, network policy violation, 3+ task failures, security violation detected.

nodes.name: Allowed Workflow Capabilities
nodes.summary: What the agent CAN do: read/write repo files, execute git operations, run sandboxed code, query AI stack (aq-*), delegate tasks to Claude, read system status (journalctl/systemctl status), commit and create branches, search codebase.

nodes.name: Enforcement
nodes.summary: Enforcement via policy file (autonomous_agent_security_policy.json), enforcer module (security_policy_enforcer.py check_operation()), coordinator integration (every operation pre-checked), startup verification loading policy on init.

nodes.name: Audit & Monitoring
nodes.summary: All operations logged to audit trail with timestamp, operation type, path/command, allow/deny decision. Violations escalate to human notification and circuit breaker trigger.

nodes.name: Core Loop
nodes.summary: Main coordinator loop pseudocode: find_next_pending_batch → ask_claude_to_plan → slice_plan_into_tasks → delegate_to_claude per task → verify_and_approve → commit_if_approved → mark_batch_complete → immediately move to next batch. Key mandate: DON'T STOP.

nodes.name: Coordinator Responsibilities
nodes.summary: 5 responsibilities: (1) read roadmaps/extract pending batches, (2) trigger planning via DelegatedTask to Claude, (3) slice plan into executable tasks (5-10 per plan), (4) delegate each implementation task to Claude, (5) update progress and immediately continue.

nodes.name: What Coordinator Does NOT Do
nodes.summary: Hard boundaries: don't plan (delegate to Claude), don't implement (delegate to Claude), don't make architectural decisions (ask Claude). Coordinator role is pure flow management — no direct problem-solving or code writing.

nodes.name: When to Stop and Notify Human
nodes.summary: Escalation triggers: security policy violation, >3 consecutive failures on same task, destructive operation required, ambiguous requirements needing clarification, critical system errors, unexpected state requiring human judgment.

nodes.name: Delegation Rule (aq-delegate)
nodes.summary: Mandatory rule: always use aq-delegate instead of bare delegate-to-claude or qwen. aq-delegate injects project file paths, PRSI locations, and search-first mandate automatically. Bare delegation leaves sub-agents context-blind.

nodes.name: Routing Matrix
nodes.summary: Local lanes: health checks, monitoring, polling, bounded repo inspection, background automation — use aq-qa, aq-report, aq-hints, aq-context-bootstrap. Remote lanes: architecture, policy synthesis, larger-context review, implementation help. Memory policy: aq-memory search before raw HTTP; aq-context-bootstrap for workflow state.

nodes.name: CLI-Based Workflow
nodes.summary: Complete workflow using only CLI: aq-qa 0 health check → aq-hints search → aq-delegate --auto-approve for planning/implementation → git operations → aq-qa verification → commit. All steps use aq-* wrappers, never raw API calls.

nodes.name: CLI Commands Available
nodes.summary: Tool inventory: delegate-to-claude (with --description, --files, --context, --role, --timeout flags), aq-hints (semantic search with --limit, --tags), aq-qa (health check with phase arg), git operations (status/add/commit/push), plus aq-delegate as preferred wrapper for all delegation.

nodes.name: phase-54-agentic-first-elevation.md
nodes.summary: Archived architectural elevation plan introducing six sub-phases: unified memory layer, intent-aware routing, active RAG pipeline, durable DAG execution, observability spine, and continuous spec-driven evaluation — together forming a fully agentic harness.

nodes.name: 54.1 Unified Memory Layer
nodes.summary: Sub-phase defining a unified memory layer across agents with consistent read/write semantics and cross-session persistence.

nodes.name: 54.2 Intent-Aware Routing
nodes.summary: Sub-phase introducing intent classification to route tasks to appropriate agents (local vs. remote) based on task type.

nodes.name: 54.3 Active RAG Pipeline
nodes.summary: Sub-phase building an active retrieval-augmented generation pipeline feeding AIDB context into agent tasks automatically.

nodes.name: 54.4 Durable DAG Execution
nodes.summary: Sub-phase introducing durable directed-acyclic-graph task execution with checkpointing and resume capability.

nodes.name: 54.5 Observability Spine
nodes.summary: Sub-phase creating an observability spine with event emission, structured logging, and dashboard integration for agent task visibility.

nodes.name: 54.6 Continuous Spec-Driven Evaluation
nodes.summary: Sub-phase establishing continuous automated evaluation against defined specs, with scoring thresholds gating further phase execution.

nodes.name: phase-55-temporal-memory-crystalline-agentops.md
nodes.summary: Archived plan for temporal memory (fact supersession/invalidation), crystalline session distillation, and Agent Ops SRE profile for reasoning drift detection — depends on Phase 54.

nodes.name: 55.0 Retrieval Gate Stabilization
nodes.summary: Precondition slice ensuring retrieval gate reliability before temporal memory work; also covers continuous learning runtime contract (55.0b).

nodes.name: 55.1 Supersession Logic
nodes.summary: Implements temporal fact invalidation so outdated memory entries are superseded when newer contradicting facts arrive.

nodes.name: 55.2 Crystalline Memory
nodes.summary: Session distillation pipeline that compresses session ephemera into permanent crystalline memory entries for long-term retention.

nodes.name: 55.3 Agent Ops SRE Profile
nodes.summary: SRE-style profile for detecting agent reasoning drift — monitors output quality, flags regressions, and triggers retraining or profile reset.

nodes.name: phase-56-harness-integration-loop.md
nodes.summary: Largest archived plan — closes the harness integration gap across 8 slices: delegation audit bridge, session crystallization, context hydration, commit-time fact extraction, drift-aware profile activation, agent event bus, remote outcome capture, and cross-agent lesson feed.

nodes.name: 56.1 Delegation Audit Bridge
nodes.summary: Bridges delegation dispatch events to the audit log so every remote agent call is traceable with inputs, outputs, and timing.

nodes.name: 56.2 Automated Session Crystallization
nodes.summary: Automated post-session pipeline distilling ephemeral session data into durable AIDB memory entries.

nodes.name: 56.3 Pre-Task Context Hydration
nodes.summary: Script that hydrates agent context with relevant AIDB facts and session history before task execution begins.

nodes.name: 56.4 Commit-Time Fact Extraction
nodes.summary: Local agent hook that extracts institutional facts from commit content and seeds them into AIDB at commit time.

nodes.name: 56.5 Drift-Aware Profile Auto-Activation
nodes.summary: Automatically activates corrective profiles when agent drift metrics exceed thresholds, without manual intervention.

nodes.name: 56.6 Agent Event Bus
nodes.summary: Shared signal log acting as event bus for cross-agent coordination, enabling publish/subscribe patterns between Claude, Gemini, Codex, and local agent.

nodes.name: 56.7 Remote Agent Outcome Capture
nodes.summary: Captures outcomes from remote agent (Gemini/Codex) runs back into the local harness for audit, scoring, and lesson extraction.

nodes.name: 56.8 Cross-Agent Lesson Feed
nodes.summary: At session start, feeds cross-agent lessons learned from AIDB into each agent's context to prevent re-discovering known patterns.

nodes.name: SHARED-GOALS-BRIEF.md
nodes.summary: Shared context brief issued to all agents before drafting individual PRDs for the Multi-Agent Edge AI Harness (MAEAH). The foundational input document for the four-agent PRD authoring exercise.

nodes.name: RESEARCH-FINDINGS.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive research survey (7 passes, 67 sections) backing the MAEAH design. Covers OS-layer architecture (AIOS, AgentRM, HiveMind, Astraea), model hot-swap lifecycle (SwapServeLLM, llama-swap, Tangram), edge memory (Q4 KV cache, AMV-L), split inference, distributed coordination (gossip protocols), safety and access control (3D guard-layer), workflow orchestration, federated fine-tuning, agent harness frameworks, quantization formats (GGUF/AWQ/GPTQ/EXL2), OTel GenAI SemConv, and dynamic model routing/cascading. Primary research foundation for all four individual PRDs.

nodes.name: PRD-CTO-CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: CTO/Chief Architect perspective PRD authored by Claude. Proposes a three-layer OS model for local AI (kernel/services/application), eight kernel components (Scheduler, Context/Memory/Storage/Tool/Access/Model-Lifecycle/Observability Managers), hardware abstraction for thermal-aware APU inference, and an A2A mesh networking layer. Includes model catalog schema, hot-swap and pre-download flows, design decisions, risk matrix, and comparison hooks against the current system.

nodes.name: PRD-VP-ENG-GEMINI.md
nodes.summary: VP Engineering / Infra Lead perspective PRD authored by Gemini. Concise view (117 lines) focused on production reliability, SLA tiering for hot-swap, signed agent cards, thermal-to-scheduler coupling, downtime budget, and operational governance. Contributes amendments AM-G1 (hot-swap SLA tiering), AM-G2 (signed agent cards day-1), AM-G3 (thermal-scheduler coupling) to the combined PRD.

nodes.name: PRD-STAFF-ENG-CODEX.md
nodes.summary: Senior Staff Software Engineer perspective PRD authored by Codex. Most detailed individual PRD (1048 lines, 64 sections). Covers all kernel components with deep implementation specs: Protocol Gateway, MLFQ Scheduler, Context/Memory/Storage/Tool/Access/Model-Lifecycle/Observability Managers. Defines full interface contracts (OpenAI-compatible API, A2A Gateway, MCP integration, Admin API), data models (Model Registry, Agent Registry, Event Envelope), hot-swap/rollback sequences, and an extensive testing appendix elevated to normative acceptance criteria (AM-C5).

nodes.name: PRD-EDGE-AI-QWEN.md
nodes.summary: Edge AI Specialist perspective PRD proxy-authored by Claude on behalf of Qwen (offline during planning). Focuses on hardware-specific inference optimization: Inference Parameter Manager (IPM) for dynamic n_gpu_layers / quantization tier selection, Unified Memory Budget Manager (UMBM) for 27 GB RAM allocation, thermal monitor integration, quantization format ladder (Q4_K_M → Q2_K fallback), speculative decoding (MTP), and GGUF format validation at download time. Unique contributions: IPM and UMBM kernel components.

nodes.name: AGENT-PARITY-REVIEW.md
nodes.summary: Cross-agent parity review of the four individual PRDs before synthesis. Codex identifies highest-value additions (AM-C1 through AM-C5) and explicit deferrals. Four specialist reviewers (architecture/runtime, security/governance, memory/RAG/eval, edge/ops) provide structured feedback. Resulting plan changes derived from the multi-reviewer pass are recorded and fed into the combined PRD.

nodes.name: COMBINED-PRD.md
nodes.summary: Authoritative synthesis PRD (618 lines, 42 sections) merging all four individual PRDs into a unified specification for MAEAH. Defines the three-layer OS model, ten kernel components (adding IPM and UMBM from Qwen PRD), normalized API surface (AM-C1), full model catalog schema with durable state machine (AM-C4), hot-swap sequence, dashboard model panel, design decisions, risk matrix, research citations, cross-agent module comparison, open questions, Qwen sign-off checklist, amendment records (v0.1→v0.2 Gemini/Codex, v0.3 external parity supplement), and implementation priority order.

nodes.name: SIGNOFF-GEMINI.md
nodes.summary: Gemini VP Engineering sign-off on the combined PRD. Verdict: APPROVE WITH AMENDMENTS. Records three required amendments (AM-G1/G2/G3), open concerns, and confirmed-correct items. Formal sign-off statement gates implementation authorization.

nodes.name: SIGNOFF-CODEX.md
nodes.summary: Codex Senior Staff Engineer sign-off on the combined PRD. Verdict: APPROVE WITH AMENDMENTS. Records five amendments (AM-C1 API normalization, AM-C2 security boundary, AM-C3 schema attribution, AM-C4 durable lifecycle state machine, AM-C5 testing as normative acceptance criteria), implementation feasibility concerns, and formal sign-off statement.

nodes.name: PLAN-SIGNOFF.md
nodes.summary: Master plan sign-off record tracking implementation authorization for all MAEAH phases (A through G). Enumerates all incorporated Gemini (AM-G1/G2/G3) and Codex (AM-C1 through AM-C5) amendments, their impact on the combined PRD, and the Qwen sign-off checklist (reassigned due to Qwen offline). Documents implementation status: Phases B-F complete, Phase A authorized, Phase G pending user action. Includes v0.3 external parity supplement sign-off status.

nodes.name: EXTERNAL-PARITY-AMENDMENTS.md
nodes.summary: v0.3 planning addendum defining 10 external parity amendments (PA-1 through PA-10) required to bring MAEAH to parity with state-of-the-art agentic harnesses. P0 (required before new autonomous capability): bitemporal memory, tool execution sandbox ladder, agent identity/delegation hardening, MCP/A2A governance profile, security contract gates (PA-9), deployment/chaos gates (PA-10). P1 (next cycle): RAG quality gates, observability parity map, scheduler semantic blocks, edge persistence/rollback. P2: deferred tracks.

nodes.name: PARITY-INTEGRATION-PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Implementation roadmap for integrating the v0.3 external parity amendments into the existing phase sequence (Phases 60-63). Maps each PA-item to its target phase, defines slice queue with PRD section cross-references, establishes cross-surface change contracts, and sequences five integration phases: security/governance → memory/RAG → observability/eval → scheduler/resource/deployment → optional future tracks. Includes current reconciliation state (2026-05-23) and non-actions for the cycle.

nodes.name: MAEAH-SECURITY-CONTRACT-GATES.md
nodes.summary: Normative security contract for MAEAH covering nine rules: loopback-is-not-authorization, agent identity lifecycle (mint/rotate/revoke), scoped non-escalating delegation, per-tool sandbox profiles, tainted MCP output by default, model catalog supply-chain boundary, memory provenance and poisoning controls, privacy-safe tamper-aware audit, and mandatory independent review for security-sensitive slices. Includes a Phase 1 static regression gate (Codex 2026-05-21) checklist.

nodes.name: LIVE-VALIDATION-RUNBOOK.md
nodes.summary: Operational runbook for live validation of MAEAH implementation across four phases: Phase 0 (repo-static contract gate), Phase 1 (surface health checks), Phase 2 (Responses API compatibility smoke), Phase 3 (user-defined model lifecycle smoke including download, promote, hot-swap, rollback), Phase 4 (full MAEAH acceptance gate). Specifies preconditions, promotion criteria, and failure handling procedures.

nodes.name: PHASE-A-ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.md
nodes.summary: Normative acceptance criteria (AM-C5) for Phase A (model pre-download + hot-swap dashboard). Ten gates: model catalog load, full AM-C4 state machine, download with SSE progress, promote/hot-swap, SLA tier measurement, rollback, CPU-fallback queue buffer, audit event emission, authentication, and dashboard panel. Defines exact commands to run each gate.

nodes.name: SYSTEM-COMPARISON-PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Detailed module-by-module comparison of the MAEAH target design vs the current NixOS-Dev-Quick-Deploy system (563 lines, 76 sections). Maps 10 implementation priorities (hot-swap, IPM/thermal, MLFQ scheduler, Q4 KV cache, AMV-L memory, OTel migration, A2A agent cards, MCP HTTP standardization, UMBM, ABC cascade routing) with current state, target state, delta files, and prerequisites. Identifies preserved modules (NixOS flake, aq-qa, dashboard, AIDB, governance scripts) and phases A-E delivery sequence. Incorporates all amendments and v0.3 external parity integration track.

nodes.name: QWEN-ITEMS-CODEX-FINDINGS.md
nodes.summary: Claude standing in for Codex/Qwen addresses open design question Q7: CPU-only fallback queue-buffer behavior when GPU is unavailable. Short findings document (34 lines) resolving the sign-off checklist item from the combined PRD.

nodes.name: QWEN-ITEMS-GEMINI-FINDINGS.md
nodes.summary: Gemini hardware validation findings resolving four Qwen sign-off checklist items: Q1 (quantization tier default recommendation), Q2 (n_gpu_layers ceiling confirmation at 12 for Renoir APU), Q5 (UMBM memory budget allocation for 27 GB system), Q6 (quantization tier ladder design). Provides hardware-specific answers required before Qwen sign-off items can be closed.

nodes.name: slice-103-cross-agent-contradiction-detection.md
nodes.summary: Archived phase-103 slice plan: routes memory contradictions and consensus divergence detected by memory_broker.py and consensus_arbiter.py into ATTENTION_ARCHIVE.jsonl so silent failures become passively observable by operators. Scope is limited to two files with auto_ok boundary; no human-gate alerts added.

nodes.name: slice-93-15-dashboard-agent-observability.md
nodes.summary: Archived slice-93.15 plan: frontend implementation for the Agent Observability dashboard panel. Covers wiring dashboard.js to fetch live telemetry (useful tokens, effectiveness scorecard, swimlanes, race comparisons) from backend endpoints, implementing loadAgentReplay(), sendControl(), and D3/Mermaid visualizations.

nodes.name: slice-93-5-useful-token-instrumentation.md
nodes.summary: Archived slice-93.5 plan: adds useful-token ratio telemetry and waste-bucket aggregation to aq-report's --machine JSON output. Distinguishes tokens on accepted artifacts vs rework/failed-tool/abandoned-context. Must return no_data safely when attribution is missing.

nodes.name: context-mode-integration.md
nodes.summary: Design document for integrating context modes into the AI deployment stack. Covers SQLite+FTS5 context store schema, Context Manager Helper, MCP Context Wrapper, and enhancements to deploy CLI and dashboard API for deployment tracking across Phase 2 and 3.

nodes.name: Integration Strategy
nodes.summary: Phase 2 enhancements (context-aware deployment tracking, MCP integration) and Phase 3 foundation. Defines batches 2.1B through 2.3B covering deployment tracking, MCP context integration, and intelligent dashboard queries.

nodes.name: Technical Architecture
nodes.summary: SQLite+FTS5 context store schema, Context Manager Helper class, and MCP Context Wrapper. Defines the data model for storing and querying deployment context across sessions.

nodes.name: Integration with Existing Systems
nodes.summary: Code-level integration points for deploy CLI enhancement and dashboard API enhancement. Shows how context tracking hooks into existing deployment and query workflows.

nodes.name: intelligent-agent-routing.md
nodes.summary: Design document for intelligent multi-tier agent routing and workload distribution. Defines three-tier routing (Local LLM → Free Sub-Agent → Paid Model), decision tree, complexity classification, and cost impact analysis targeting 80-90% cost reduction.

nodes.name: Three-Tier Architecture
nodes.summary: Tier 1 (Local LLM, highest priority — llama.cpp/Qwen3), Tier 2 (Free Sub-Agent — Gemini/Codex), Tier 3 (Paid Model, last resort — Claude). Each tier has defined capabilities, use-cases, and cost profiles.

nodes.name: Intelligent Routing Algorithm
nodes.summary: Decision tree and complexity classification for task routing. Criteria include task complexity, token count, required tools, and availability of local models. Routes simple tasks local-first, complex or multi-step tasks to higher tiers.

nodes.name: Implementation Strategy
nodes.summary: Three implementation phases: Phase 1 hybrid coordinator enhancement, Phase 2 local LLM integration, Phase 3 deployment integration. Includes code-level implementation details for each phase targeting the hybrid-coordinator and deploy CLI.

nodes.name: Cost Impact Analysis
nodes.summary: Comparison of current all-Claude routing vs optimized tiered routing. Projects significant annual savings by shifting 70-80% of tasks to local and free-tier agents.

nodes.name: unified-deploy-cli-architecture.md
nodes.summary: Architecture design for a unified deployment CLI consolidating all NixOS and AI stack management commands. Covers command structure, configuration file format, error handling, logging, backward compatibility, and phased implementation plan.

nodes.name: Command Structure
nodes.summary: Defines all CLI subcommands: deploy system (NixOS rebuild), deploy ai-stack (AI service lifecycle), deploy test, deploy health (service health checks), deploy security, deploy dashboard, deploy config, deploy search (log/event search), deploy recover.

nodes.name: Configuration File
nodes.summary: TOML/YAML configuration file format defining service endpoints, file paths, timeouts, and feature flags for the unified deploy CLI.

nodes.name: Error Handling
nodes.summary: Structured error categories and message format for CLI deployment failures. Defines user-facing error reporting with actionable diagnostics.

nodes.name: Backward Compatibility
nodes.summary: Deprecation strategy and migration guide for transitioning from existing script-based CLI commands to the unified deploy CLI. Includes wrapper shims and deprecation warnings.

nodes.name: Implementation Plan
nodes.summary: Phased implementation plan for the unified deploy CLI with milestones, deliverables, and success criteria across multiple development phases.

nodes.name: high-value-improvements-plan-2026-04-19.md

Includes execution strategy, risk management, and success metrics.

nodes.name: Executive Summary
nodes.summary: Overview of improvement priorities: front-door routing to eliminate agent confusion, local agent prompt understanding fix, workflow executor completion, and memory foundation — ordered by value vs effort.

nodes.name: Priority 1: Layer 1 Front-Door Routing (CRITICAL)
nodes.summary: Slices 0.1–0.3 implement route alias mapping, a front-door request handler, and CLI/documentation integration for the hybrid coordinator to eliminate agent routing confusion.

nodes.name: Priority 2: Local Agent Prompt Understanding Fix (CRITICAL)
nodes.summary: Slices 1.1–1.3 optimize system prompt, context injection, and add a validation suite to fix local agent prompt understanding failures.

nodes.name: Priority 3: Workflow Executor (HIGH)
nodes.summary: Slices 2.3–2.4 complete the workflow executor and hybrid coordinator integration, resuming partially completed Phase 2.3 work.

nodes.name: Priority 4: Memory Foundation (MEDIUM)
nodes.summary: Slices 1.2–1.5 implement temporal validity, metadata filtering, memory CLI tools, and a benchmark harness for the memory subsystem.

nodes.name: Priority 5: Execution Isolation (MEDIUM-LOW)
nodes.summary: Phase 3 execution isolation for sandboxed agent execution to contain runaway or malicious agent actions.

nodes.name: Execution Strategy
nodes.summary: Parallelization plan, delegation protocol, and validation gates governing how slices are assigned and reviewed across agents.

nodes.name: Risk Management
nodes.summary: Identifies high-risk areas (routing logic, prompt changes, memory schema migration) and rollback plans for each priority.

nodes.name: Success Metrics
nodes.summary: Per-priority success criteria: Phase 0 routing correctness, prompt understanding accuracy, workflow executor completeness, and memory system recall metrics.

nodes.name: qwen-36-switch-recovery-plan.md
nodes.summary: Recovery plan for switching to Qwen 3.6 model after a partial download failure: three recovery options (resume, clean restart, smaller model), troubleshooting for auth/disk issues, verification steps, rollback plan, and model comparison.

nodes.name: Problem Summary
nodes.summary: Documents the partial download failure state when switching to Qwen 3.6 and its impact on the local inference stack.

nodes.name: Recovery Options
nodes.summary: Three options: (A) resume partial download via HuggingFace CLI (recommended), (B) clean restart, (C) use a smaller Qwen model variant. Each with commands and trade-offs.

nodes.name: Troubleshooting
nodes.summary: Covers three failure classes: repeated download failure, HuggingFace authentication errors, and disk space exhaustion during download.

nodes.name: Verification Steps
nodes.summary: Checklist to confirm model file completeness, SHA verification, and llama.cpp loading success before switching production traffic.

nodes.name: Rollback Plan
nodes.summary: Instructions for reverting to the previous model if the Qwen 3.6 switch fails, including llama.cpp config restore and service restart.

nodes.name: Model Comparison
nodes.summary: Comparison table of Qwen model variants (size, VRAM requirement, quality) to support selection given hardware constraints.

nodes.name: Next Steps After Model Switch
nodes.summary: Post-switch tasks: service restart, benchmark run, agent loop validation, and status tracking update.

nodes.name: system-maintenance-2026-04-19.md
nodes.summary: NixOS package update maintenance plan: flake input update, system rebuild, home-manager rebuild, verification, handling Chromium version warnings, automated update scheduling with systemd, post-update cleanup, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: Current System State

nodes.name: Update Strategy
nodes.summary: Four-step process: (1) nix flake update, (2) nixos-rebuild switch, (3) home-manager switch, (4) verify updated package versions.

nodes.name: Addressing Out-of-Date Warning
nodes.summary: Three options for Chromium version warnings: (1) accept and ignore, (2) suppress in config, (3) switch to nixpkgs-unstable for latest Chromium.

nodes.name: Automated Update Schedule
nodes.summary: Compares manual vs automated update approaches; option B provides a systemd timer service for scheduled NixOS flake updates.

nodes.name: Full Update Procedure

nodes.name: Post-Update Cleanup
nodes.summary: nix-collect-garbage commands to delete old generations and reclaim disk space after successful updates.

nodes.name: Troubleshooting
nodes.summary: Covers three common NixOS update failures: derivation build failure, lock file out of sync, and disk space exhaustion.

nodes.name: Maintenance Schedule
nodes.summary: Defines the recurring maintenance cadence for system and package updates.
