nodes.name: COMBINED-PRD
nodes.summary: Product requirements document for the Multi-Agent Edge Harness (MAEAH). Defines vision, problem statement (role confusion, context leakage, untracked delegation chains), four goals (agent identity, delegation traceability, parity enforcement, graceful degradation), API surface contracts for POST /v1/responses and /admin/v1/models, and success criteria for Phase 73+.

nodes.name: API Surface Contract (AM-C1/AM-C2)
nodes.summary: Defines two canonical HTTP surfaces: POST /v1/responses (OpenAI Responses API shim via coordinator chat/completions, implemented in extensions/openai_a2a_handlers.py) and /admin/v1/models (admin model lifecycle requiring X-Dashboard-Internal or API key; unauthenticated mutations return 403).

nodes.name: LIVE-VALIDATION-RUNBOOK
nodes.summary: Step-by-step live validation sequence for asserting MAEAH readiness. Defines five sequential phases (repo-static gate, surface health, responses shim smoke, admin auth smoke, full acceptance), promotion criteria requiring all phases pass, and failure handling guidance per phase.

nodes.name: Phase 0 — Repo-static contract gate
nodes.summary: Static contract validation gate: runs edgeai contracts check --json, test-edgeai-cli-contract.sh, test-maeah-api-surface-contract.py, test-maeah-contract-artifacts.py, test-maeah-model-registry-schema.py, and tier0-validation-gate.sh. All must exit 0 and report ok:true before proceeding.

nodes.name: Phase 1 — Surface health
nodes.summary: Service reachability check using edgeai doctor --json (all services green), edgeai models list --json (valid JSON), edgeai a2a card validate --json (A2A card present), edgeai mcp tools list --json (non-empty). Explicitly excludes destructive model lifecycle actions.

nodes.name: Phase 2 — Responses compatibility smoke
nodes.summary: Validates POST /v1/responses shim routes through chat/completions via edgeai traces tail and edgeai chat --json. Expects valid JSON envelope and X-OpenAI-Responses-Compat header in response.

nodes.name: Phase 3 — User-defined model lifecycle smoke
nodes.summary: Auth enforcement test for /admin/v1/models: unauthenticated mutations must return 403, authenticated (X-Dashboard-Internal or API key) add returns 200 or 409. Uses maeah-live-auth-smoke.sh and edgeai models add/delete commands.

nodes.name: Phase 4 — Full MAEAH acceptance
nodes.summary: Runs maeah-acceptance-tests.sh --verbose and aq-memory-recall-benchmark --json. Expects all tests pass and memory recall shows non-zero hit rate on error-solutions, best-practices, and skills-patterns AIDB collections.

nodes.name: Promotion criteria
nodes.summary: All five phases must pass before promoting MAEAH readiness. Commit hash and timestamp must be documented in PARITY-INTEGRATION-PLAN.md under the relevant milestone. No partial promotion allowed.

nodes.name: Failure handling
nodes.summary: Per-phase failure guidance: Phase 0 blocks on static contract fix; Phase 1 triggers service restart then escalation to issues-backlog; Phase 2 inspects coordinator routing/chat_template_kwargs; Phase 3 checks _check_auth() and X-Dashboard-Internal header; Phase 4 failures are blocking regressions requiring issues-backlog entry before promoting.

nodes.name: PARITY-INTEGRATION-PLAN
nodes.summary: Execution companion to COMBINED-PRD.md tracking integration steps for MAEAH parity with the canonical kernel declaration. Contains a parity targets table (P0-P2 capabilities with status) and seven integration slices covering identity envelopes, delegation envelopes, tool-boundary enforcement, trace logging, event streams, spec-variant race harness, and swimlane dashboard.

nodes.name: Parity Targets
nodes.summary: Priority-annotated capability table (P0-P2). P0: agent identity envelope (PARTIAL — X-Agent-* headers in OTel, switchboard enforcement deferred). P1: delegation envelopes, MCP tool-boundary, trace path, event stream (COMPLETE Phase 93), spec-variant race (COMPLETE Phase 93.4), useful-token accounting (COMPLETE Phase 93.5), role eligibility (COMPLETE Phase 58A.5), cross-project isolation (COMPLETE Phase 136), RAGAS auto-scoring (COMPLETE Phase 137). P2: contradiction detection (COMPLETE Phase 103/142), attention queue (COMPLETE Phase 101+).

nodes.name: Integration Slices
nodes.summary: Seven implementation slices: (1) Identity Envelope — source/role/boundary in dispatch, switchboard validation; (2) Delegation Envelopes — 4k token context ceiling per sub-agent; (3) Tool Boundary Enforcement — MCP per-role profiles, switchboard rejects out-of-profile calls; (4) Trace Path Logging — structured events to hybrid-events.jsonl for Gantt panel; (5) Agent Run Event Stream — normalized event types, replayable; (6) Spec-Variant Race — Markdown vs HTML vs visual HTML race; (7) Swimlane/Race Dashboard — single-agent replay, swimlane, and race views.

nodes.summary: Mid-phase status assessment for Phase 2 Workflow Engine. Evaluates all 7 slices (2.1–2.7) with per-slice analysis, decision matrix comparing continuing Phase 2 vs pivoting to Phase 5 GUI, risk assessment, success criteria, and recommended 3-week next-step plan.

nodes.name: session-primer-summary.json
nodes.summary: Machine-readable session primer metadata snapshot. Fields: generated_at, mode, read_only flag, objective, tiers_loaded, agent_file_count, latest_evidence_files, changed_files_count, and next action pointer. Used by aq-prime for progressive disclosure onboarding.

nodes.name: session-summary-2026-04-09.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive session summary for 2026-04-09. Documents 5 major accomplishments (Workflow Executor, Infrastructure Connection, Security Documentation, Performance Optimization Framework, OpenRouter Config), 7 queued workflow sessions, system status, Phase 1 progress, API key configuration options (OpenRouter vs Anthropic), files created, key achievements, innovation highlights, metrics summary, and next steps.

nodes.name: strands-integration-complete.md
nodes.summary: Strands-Agents framework integration completion report. Documents unified .agents/ directory structure, 6 implementation slices (directory structure, decorator tool registration, SOP workflow support, unified agent interface, DAG orchestration, integration testing), consolidation actions, benefits, usage examples, and test results.

nodes.name: token-spending-assessment-2026-06-01.md
nodes.summary: Token spending and effectiveness assessment. Evaluates extension-based/switchboard model costs, CLI agent tooling overheads, progressive memory loading tiers (L0–L3), and identifies gaps for Phase 93 requirements.

nodes.name: ui-ux-completeness-analysis-2026-04-09.md
nodes.summary: Third-pass UI/UX completeness analysis across 10 parts. Compares GUI capabilities (workflow visualization, dashboard monitoring, interactive controls, search, settings, data viz, UX features), documents dashboard reality (what exists vs API endpoints without UI), feature completeness scoring, critical missing features by priority, honest assessment of claims vs reality, user-facing feature matrix, revised recommendations, Phase 5 GUI roadmap (slices 5.1–5.6), final assessment, and honest comparison summary.

nodes.name: baseline_20260409_215323.json
nodes.summary: AI stack baseline performance snapshot (7-day window, generated 2026-04-10 04:54 UTC). Contains 41 metric sections covering tool call performance, routing split (100% local), semantic cache (75.7% hit rate), eval trends, RAG posture, remote profile utilization, latency decomposition, agent lessons, and governance compliance. First of three pre-optimization captures taken at 21:53:23.

nodes.name: baseline_20260409_215323.txt
nodes.summary: Human-readable text rendering of the baseline_20260409_215323 performance report (193 lines). Presents tool call performance table with P50/P95 latencies, routing split (100% local), semantic cache stats, eval trend (STABLE 50%), recommended prompts, RAG posture, and agent lessons in formatted terminal output.

nodes.name: baseline_20260409_215600.json
nodes.summary: AI stack baseline performance snapshot (second capture at 21:56:00, same day). Structurally identical to baseline_215323 — 41 sections, same JSON schema. Captured approximately 2.5 minutes after the first baseline to confirm stable pre-optimization state.

nodes.name: baseline_20260409_215600.txt
nodes.summary: Human-readable text rendering of the baseline_20260409_215600 performance report (193 lines). Second baseline capture confirming stable pre-optimization state; same structure and metrics as baseline_215323.txt.

nodes.name: baseline_20260409_215726.json
nodes.summary: AI stack baseline performance snapshot (third capture at 21:57:26). Final pre-optimization baseline, captured immediately before the optimized run at 21:59:03. Structurally identical to the first two baselines — 41 sections, same JSON schema.

nodes.name: baseline_20260409_215726.txt
nodes.summary: Human-readable text rendering of the baseline_20260409_215726 performance report (193 lines). Third and final pre-optimization baseline text report; immediately precedes the optimized capture.

nodes.name: optimized_20260409_215903.json
nodes.summary: AI stack post-optimization performance snapshot (1-day window, generated 2026-04-10 04:59 UTC). Slightly fewer lines than baselines (982 vs 1036) reflecting a smaller 1d tool call set. Shows improved semantic cache hit rate (78.5% vs 75.7% baseline) and reduced route_search P95 latency (19740ms vs 22537ms). ai_coordinator_delegate absent — not called in the 1d window.

nodes.name: optimized_20260409_215903.txt
nodes.summary: Human-readable text rendering of the optimized_20260409_215903 performance report (186 lines, 1-day window). Shows post-optimization metrics: cache hit rate 78.5%, 100% local routing, eval STABLE 50%. Confirms all coordinator tools healthy with no errors. ai_coordinator_delegate not present in 1d window.

nodes.name: tool_performance
nodes.summary: Per-tool call metrics section (~158 lines, lines 10-167). Tracks valid call counts, P50/P95 latency ms, OK percentage, error counts, and 4xx rates for all coordinator tools: hints (4181 calls, P50=147ms), a2a_agent_card (4169), discovery (886), route_search (867, P95=22537ms), recall_agent_memory (322), workflow_plan (292), ai_coordinator_delegate (7, 28.6% OK — primary failure indicator), and others.

nodes.name: route_search_latency_decomposition
nodes.summary: Detailed route_search latency decomposition (~95 lines, lines 581-675). Breaks end-to-end route search timing into sub-phases (embedding generation, Qdrant vector search, reranking, result serialization) to pinpoint bottlenecks. Contains P50/P95 distributions per decomposition step across the 7d window.

nodes.name: remote_profile_utilization_windows
nodes.summary: Remote profile utilization tracked across rolling time windows (~83 lines, lines 498-580). Records which switchboard remote profiles (anthropic, gemini, openrouter, etc.) were invoked per 1h/24h/7d window with utilization percentages and call volumes. Baseline shows 0% remote usage (100% local).

nodes.name: route_retrieval_breadth_windows
nodes.summary: Route retrieval breadth tracked across rolling time windows (~73 lines, lines 695-767). Measures candidate route counts retrieved per query across 1h/24h/7d periods, indicating AIDB search coverage quality and whether retrieval is returning sufficient context candidates for routing decisions.

nodes.name: agent_lessons
nodes.summary: Accumulated agent lessons section (~69 lines, lines 932-1000). Stores patterns, anti-patterns, and institutional knowledge extracted from agent sessions. These entries feed the harness learning loop, inform hint generation, and drive routing improvement. Represents the self-improvement knowledge base at the time of the baseline snapshot.

nodes.name: continue_editor
nodes.summary: Continue.dev editor integration statistics (~49 lines, lines 318-366). Tracks usage of the Continue IDE plugin — session counts, query types, model routing decisions, and per-tool performance metrics for editor-assisted development workflows in the 7d baseline window.

nodes.name: intent_contract_compliance
nodes.summary: Intent contract compliance audit section (~38 lines, lines 828-865). Evaluates whether agent tool usage matched declared intent contracts — verifying that tools called align with the registered intent for each query type. Used for governance enforcement and detecting orchestrator rule violations.

nodes.name: delegated_prompt_failure_windows
nodes.summary: Delegated prompt failure rates across rolling time windows (~43 lines, lines 430-472). Records which prompt types failed during delegation to remote agents or local inference, with 1h/24h/7d window breakdowns enabling failure trend detection and delegation reliability monitoring.

nodes.name: .aider.md
nodes.summary: Aider AI coding-agent conventions for the harness: critical rules (NixOS declarative-only, commit format, port SSOT), recurring-error quick-reference, key file index, pre-commit checklist.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Environment variable template: POSTGRES_PASSWORD, GRAFANA_ADMIN_USER, GRAFANA_ADMIN_PASSWORD, AGENT_WALL_CLOCK_SECS. Reference for local secret injection without SOPS.

nodes.name: .gitleaks.toml
nodes.summary: Gitleaks secret-scan config extending the default ruleset with a project-specific allowlist to suppress false positives in test fixtures and example files.

nodes.name: .pre-commit-config.yaml
nodes.summary: Pre-commit hook pipeline: gitleaks secret scan, shell-check, nixpkgs-fmt, and repo-structure lint gates run before every commit.

nodes.name: .sops.yaml
nodes.summary: SOPS encryption policy: creation_rules binding nix/secrets/** files to age/PGP key fingerprints, enforcing secrets stay out of plain-text Nix store.

nodes.name: .vscode.configs.import.cspell.config.json
nodes.summary: CSpell spell-check config for VSCode: imports project dictionary, lists custom dictionaries covering NixOS, AI-stack, and harness-specific terminology.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Canonical AI-agent onboarding guide (307 lines, 28 sections): workflow contract, skill index, multi-agent collaboration, async drop-zones, OWASP security checklist, lights-out factory rules, role/architecture, issue logging, commit discipline, key CLIs and port SSOT.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code agent instructions (238 lines, 18 sections): slash commands, required shared knowledge, session-start protocol, behavioral rules (14), delegation/role defaults, commit discipline, architecture constraints, service port map, on-demand context table.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Top-level project automation: up/down/restart/ps/logs for service lifecycle; health and metrics checks; security-audit, security-check, security-scan targets; hospital-gate pre-deploy gate; test runner.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project README (642 lines, 61 sections): executive summary, architecture overview, AI stack services (inference/RAG/monitoring), CLI tools, agent integrations, MCP servers, skills library, declarative config, repository structure, validation and license.

nodes.name: dashboard.html
nodes.summary: Single-file AI-stack monitoring dashboard (1233 lines): real-time service health tiles, metrics charts, agent activity feed; polls coordinator/AIDB/llama.cpp HTTP endpoints via fetch; no external deps.

nodes.name: nixos-quick-deploy.sh
nodes.summary: Main NixOS deployment orchestrator (4755 lines, 124 functions): end-to-end pipeline from pre-flight validation and model download through nixos-rebuild switch, home-manager switch, AI service restart, embedding migration, to post-flight health checks and completion summary.

nodes.name: pyrightconfig.json
nodes.summary: Pyright type-checker config (59 lines): pythonVersion, extraPaths covering all service subdirs, exclude patterns for generated/vendored code, 30+ per-rule severity overrides tuned to the project's async Python stack.

nodes.name: pytest.ini
nodes.summary: Pytest root config: test discovery paths, asyncio mode, log format, and marker definitions for the project's Python test suite.

nodes.name: usage
nodes.summary: Prints full CLI usage and option reference (149 lines) covering all deploy modes (build/switch/boot/disko), flags, and phase descriptions.

nodes.name: list_phases
nodes.summary: Lists available deploy phases with descriptions; used by --list-phases flag.

nodes.name: setup_ui
nodes.summary: Initialises terminal UI: detects color/emoji support, sets global formatting variables used throughout output functions.

nodes.name: run_timed_step
nodes.summary: Wraps an arbitrary command with wall-clock timing, section header, and success/failure logging; used for major pipeline stages.

nodes.name: should_skip_discovery_by_cache
nodes.summary: Checks whether host-discovery results are still valid via a mtime-bounded cache file; returns true to skip re-discovery if cache is fresh.

nodes.name: prepare_filtered_flake_projection
nodes.summary: Creates a temporary filtered copy of the flake restricted to the target host, reducing Nix eval scope and speeding up builds.

nodes.name: print_deploy_completion_summary
nodes.summary: Renders the final deploy-completion banner: generations diff, elapsed time, activated services, and next-step hints.

nodes.name: print_completion_test_results
nodes.summary: Runs and renders an extensive post-deploy smoke-test suite (529 lines): checks all AI services, ports, AppArmor status, systemd units, Nix store integrity, and dashboard reachability; emits PASS/WARN/FAIL per check.

nodes.name: run_script_runtime_contract_check
nodes.summary: Validates the script's own runtime preconditions: bash version, required external commands, and environment variable contracts before execution begins.

nodes.name: resolve_hybrid_api_key
nodes.summary: Resolves the hybrid-coordinator API key from /run/secrets or env fallback for use in AI service HTTP calls.

nodes.name: hybrid_post_json_capture
nodes.summary: Posts JSON to the hybrid-coordinator HTTP API, captures the response body; used for service capability verification.

nodes.name: verify_repo_backed_ai_service_capabilities_once
nodes.summary: One-shot verification of all repo-backed AI service capabilities (133 lines): checks llama.cpp inference, embedding, AIDB collections, coordinator routing, and switchboard profiles; results cached for the session.

nodes.name: should_manage_repo_backed_ai_services
nodes.summary: Determines whether the deploy should restart/verify AI services based on flake profile flags and changed files.

nodes.name: restart_repo_backed_ai_services_if_needed
nodes.summary: Conditionally restarts AI systemd units (llama.cpp, AIDB, hybrid-coordinator, switchboard) after rebuild; respects service dependency order and waits for readiness.

nodes.name: verify_repo_backed_ai_services_are_live_if_needed
nodes.summary: HTTP health-polls each AI service endpoint after restart, failing fast if any service does not become live within the timeout budget.

nodes.name: run_declarative_postflight_converge
nodes.summary: Re-applies declarative convergence steps (tmpfiles, activation scripts) that must run after switch to ensure filesystem state matches Nix declarations.

nodes.name: run_nonfatal_postflight_check
nodes.summary: Runs a non-fatal postflight check: logs WARN on failure but does not abort the deploy pipeline.

nodes.name: run_required_postflight_check
nodes.summary: Runs a required postflight check: dies with error on failure, aborting the deploy pipeline.

nodes.name: guard_stateful_service_downgrades
nodes.summary: Detects and blocks downgrade of stateful services (e.g., Redis) that would corrupt on-disk data formats; compares current vs. target version strings.

nodes.name: run_inline_postflight_script_if_present
nodes.summary: Executes an optional inline postflight script defined in the host's deploy config, allowing per-host custom post-switch logic.

nodes.name: run_postflight_convergence
nodes.summary: Orchestrates all postflight steps in order (41 lines): declarative converge, required checks, non-fatal checks, inline script, service downgrade guard.

nodes.name: assert_post_switch_desktop_outcomes
nodes.summary: Validates desktop environment outcomes after switch: checks that Wayland/X11 session targets, display manager, and dconf schemas are consistent.

nodes.name: run_git_safe
nodes.summary: Wraps git commands with safe-directory override and error handling; prevents git from refusing the repo due to ownership mismatches in sudo context.

nodes.name: resolve_local_flake_path
nodes.summary: Resolves the canonical absolute path to the local flake directory, supporting --flake override and auto-detection from CWD or git root.

nodes.name: resolve_host_from_flake_if_needed
nodes.summary: Auto-detects the target NixOS hostname from the flake's nixosConfigurations when --host is not explicitly provided.

nodes.name: has_configuration_name
nodes.summary: Checks whether a given configuration name exists in nixosConfigurations or homeConfigurations; used to validate --host and --home args.

nodes.name: ensure_flake_visible_to_nix
nodes.summary: Stages untracked flake files to git index so Nix can read them (Nix requires git-tracked files in flake dirs); cleans up staging after build.

nodes.name: cleanup_auto_staged_flake_files
nodes.summary: Removes the temporary git staging done by ensure_flake_visible_to_nix, restoring the index to its pre-build state.

nodes.name: snapshot_generated_repo_files
nodes.summary: Snapshots generated/auto-modified repo files (lock files, hardware-config) before rebuild so they can be restored if the build is aborted.

nodes.name: restore_generated_repo_files
nodes.summary: Restores generated repo files from snapshot on abort or unexpected error, preventing partial-update commits.

nodes.name: cleanup_on_exit
nodes.summary: EXIT trap handler: cleans temp files, restores staged files, resumes paused systemd units, and emits final status line.

nodes.name: pause_activation_memory_relief_units
nodes.summary: Stops memory-intensive systemd units (e.g., llama.cpp) before nixos-rebuild to free RAM for the Nix build, preventing OOM on 27 GB Renoir APU.

nodes.name: resume_activation_memory_relief_units
nodes.summary: Restarts the units paused by pause_activation_memory_relief_units after nixos-rebuild completes.

nodes.name: on_unexpected_error
nodes.summary: ERR trap: captures the failing command and line number, triggers file restore, and logs a structured error report before exit.

nodes.name: run_roadmap_completion_verification
nodes.summary: Checks deploy roadmap items against the current system state; reports which roadmap goals are satisfied post-switch.

nodes.name: run_readiness_analysis
nodes.summary: Analyses system readiness for deployment: checks disk space, pending Nix GC, flake eval health, and memory headroom before committing to rebuild.

nodes.name: run_pre_deploy_validation_loop
nodes.summary: Interactive pre-deploy validation loop (83 lines): runs all preflight assertions in sequence, pauses on warning for user confirmation in interactive TTY, aborts on fatal failures.

nodes.name: free_blocked_ai_ports
nodes.summary: Identifies and kills processes occupying AI service ports (8080, 8081, 8002, 8003, 8085, 8089, 8889) before deploy to prevent bind failures.

nodes.name: run_nix_eval_with_timeout
nodes.summary: Wraps nix eval with a wall-clock timeout and structured error output; prevents evaluation hangs from stalling the pipeline.

nodes.name: bootstrap_ai_stack_secrets_if_needed
nodes.summary: One-time AI stack secret bootstrap (106 lines): generates age key, adds to SOPS recipients, creates and encrypts secrets.nix for API keys; idempotent via sentinel check.

nodes.name: update_flake_lock
nodes.summary: Updates flake.lock selectively or fully; supports --update-input for pinned inputs; commits the updated lock file.

nodes.name: run_privileged
nodes.summary: Runs a command under sudo with appropriate flags; handles passwordless sudo detection and polkit fallback.

nodes.name: sudo_passwordless_command_allowed
nodes.summary: Checks whether a specific command is allowed without password via sudoers NOPASSWD rules.

nodes.name: sudo_passwordless_ready
nodes.summary: Determines overall sudo passwordless readiness for the deploy; checks nixos-rebuild and home-manager commands.

nodes.name: prime_sudo_session
nodes.summary: Primes the sudo credential cache with an interactive password prompt before the non-interactive rebuild phase, preventing mid-build password prompts.

nodes.name: read_shadow_hash
nodes.summary: Reads the hashed password field for a given user from /etc/shadow; requires privilege escalation.

nodes.name: assert_runtime_account_unlocked
nodes.summary: Verifies that the target user account is not locked (! or * prefix in shadow hash) before deploy; prevents lockout scenarios.

nodes.name: assert_password_unchanged
nodes.summary: Compares the current password hash against the pre-deploy snapshot to detect unintended password changes caused by the switch.

nodes.name: ensure_host_facts_access
nodes.summary: Ensures the host-facts JSON (hardware-config, filesystems, boot mode) is accessible and fresh before preflight assertions consume it.

nodes.name: assert_target_account_guardrails
nodes.summary: Comprehensive account safety guardrails (84 lines): validates UID/GID, group membership, home directory, shell, and that the account is not a system account being deployed over.

nodes.name: assert_previous_boot_fsck_clean
nodes.summary: Reads journal for previous boot filesystem check results; warns on fsck errors that could indicate storage degradation.

nodes.name: assert_host_storage_config
nodes.summary: Validates host storage configuration (64 lines): checks partition table, filesystem types, mount points, free space thresholds, and EFI partition health against host-facts.

nodes.name: assert_bootloader_preflight
nodes.summary: Validates bootloader configuration (67 lines): checks systemd-boot or GRUB is installed, EFI vars writable, and boot entries match expected hostnames.

nodes.name: assert_target_boot_mode
nodes.summary: Asserts that the detected boot mode (UEFI vs BIOS) matches what the Nix config declares; prevents UEFI/BIOS mismatch deploys.

nodes.name: assert_targets_exist
nodes.summary: Verifies that all requested deploy targets (nixosConfigurations + homeConfigurations) exist in the flake before committing to a rebuild.

nodes.name: assert_safe_switch_context
nodes.summary: Checks that the switch is being run in a safe context: not inside a VM that would mask hardware, not over SSH without a fallback TTY.

nodes.name: verify_home_manager_cli_post_switch
nodes.summary: Verifies home-manager CLI is on PATH and returns the correct generation number after home switch.

nodes.name: home_switch
nodes.summary: Executes home-manager switch for the target user profile; handles backup conflicts, activation errors, and generation logging.

nodes.name: persist_home_git_credentials_declarative
nodes.summary: Writes git credential config declaratively into home-manager managed paths (114 lines); handles GitHub token injection without writing plaintext to tracked files.

nodes.name: run_discovery_step
nodes.summary: Host discovery phase (121 lines): collects hardware info, existing NixOS config, disk layout, and AI model inventory; writes host-facts JSON; respects cache to avoid redundant nix evals.

nodes.name: prompt_model_selection
nodes.summary: Interactive model selection wizard (407 lines): lists available GGUF models by size class, shows VRAM/RAM fit for the Renoir APU, prompts user to select primary/embed model, writes selection to deploy-options.local.nix.

nodes.name: resolve_deploy_targets
nodes.summary: Resolves the full set of deploy targets (NixOS host + home config) from CLI args and flake introspection.

nodes.name: run_preflight_phase
nodes.summary: Orchestrates all preflight assertion functions in the correct order before triggering any rebuild.

nodes.name: run_phase0_disko_if_requested
nodes.summary: Conditionally runs disko disk partitioning phase on first-time bare-metal deploys when --disko flag is set.

nodes.name: run_secureboot_enrollment_if_requested
nodes.summary: Handles secure-boot key enrollment via sbctl when --enroll-keys flag is set; validates prerequisites before running enrollment.

nodes.name: run_build_mode_workflow
nodes.summary: Build-only mode workflow: runs nixos-rebuild build without switching, for CI/dry-run validation.

nodes.name: pre_rebuild_model_download
nodes.summary: Downloads selected AI model files before rebuild (111 lines) so model paths are available in the Nix store reference; handles resume, hash verification, and progress display.

nodes.name: verify_or_download_ai_models
nodes.summary: Main model lifecycle manager (284 lines): verifies existing model files by SHA-256, skips downloads if valid, otherwise initiates download with retry and progress tracking for primary + embed models.

nodes.name: run_boot_mode_workflow
nodes.summary: Boot mode workflow: runs nixos-rebuild boot (queues activation for next reboot without switching the running system).

nodes.name: run_switch_mode_workflow
nodes.summary: Switch mode workflow (22 lines): runs nixos-rebuild switch + home switch, pausing memory-intensive units before build and resuming after.

nodes.name: run_post_switch_validations
nodes.summary: Runs immediate post-switch validations: generation bump check, account guardrails re-check, desktop outcomes, password unchanged assertion.

nodes.name: run_flatpak_sync_if_enabled
nodes.summary: Conditionally runs flatpak update/install sync when the profile enables Flatpak; separated from Nix rebuild to avoid privilege conflicts.

nodes.name: wait_for_ai_service_http
nodes.summary: Polls a single AI service HTTP health endpoint with exponential backoff until it responds 200 or the timeout expires.

nodes.name: wait_for_ai_services
nodes.summary: Waits for all AI service health targets in parallel; collects results and fails fast if any service does not come up within budget.

nodes.name: run_general_postflight_checks
nodes.summary: General postflight check suite: systemd failed-units scan, journal error summary, Nix store health, and boot entry consistency.

nodes.name: run_ai_postflight_checks
nodes.summary: AI-specific postflight checks: verifies llama.cpp completion endpoint, AIDB collection count, coordinator routing, and switchboard profiles.

nodes.name: run_embedding_migration_if_needed
nodes.summary: Detects embedding model dimension changes after rebuild (81 lines) and re-indexes all Qdrant collections when the embed model changes; idempotent via dimension sentinel.

nodes.name: check_dashboard_postflight
nodes.summary: Verifies the monitoring dashboard is reachable after deploy and that all expected service tiles return non-error status.

nodes.name: run_switch_postflight
nodes.summary: Orchestrates all postflight phases for switch mode: AI service restart, wait for services, embedding migration, general checks, AI checks, dashboard check.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point (37 lines): argument parsing, mode dispatch (build/switch/boot/disko/enroll-keys), invokes preflight → build → postflight pipeline, handles --help and --version short-circuits.

nodes.name: brownfield.md
nodes.summary: Slash command /brownfield — guided existing-system planning workflow. Discovers current code, generates a brownfield PDR in .agent/workflows/, builds slice plans in .agents/plans/, and executes with commit isolation. Role: architect.

nodes.name: commit.md
nodes.summary: Slash command /commit — commits one logical slice with validation evidence. Enforces atomic commit messages using feat|fix|docs|refactor prefix and pushes after confirmation. Role: implementer.

nodes.name: create-prd.md
nodes.summary: Slash command /create-prd — scaffolds or refreshes a PRD at .agent/PROJECT-PRD.md. Covers executive summary, mission, scope, constraints, architecture, security, phases, validation, and risks. Role: architect.

nodes.name: execute.md
nodes.summary: Slash command /execute — reads a plan file from .agents/plans/ and implements tasks in order. Reports changed files, commands run, validation results, and rollback notes. Role: implementer.

nodes.name: explore-harness.md
nodes.summary: Slash command /explore-harness — runs aqd workflow discovery commands to list available workflows, generate artifacts, and recommend the next agent command. Role: orchestrator.

nodes.name: impeccable.md
nodes.summary: Slash command /impeccable — frontend design intelligence command with 9 sub-commands (audit, critique, polish, craft, animate, colorize, typeset, bolder/quieter, overdrive). Integrates AIDB impeccable-design project and aq-hints for RAG-grounded design references. Role: implementer.

nodes.name: plan-feature.md
nodes.summary: Slash command /plan-feature — creates a phased implementation plan file in .agents/plans/ covering objective, context files, step-by-step tasks, validation commands, evidence, and rollback notes. Role: architect.

nodes.name: prime.md
nodes.summary: Slash command /prime — 6-step session onboarding: fetches aq-hints, inspects repo structure, loads core artifacts (PRD/rules/plans), checks git state, discovers live harness capabilities via coordinator, and outputs project state summary. Role: orchestrator.

nodes.name: primer.md
nodes.summary: Slash command /primer — read-only session primer. Loads minimal context, generates session summary, asks user for focus, and never mutates repository files. Role: orchestrator.

nodes.name: project-init.md
nodes.summary: Slash command /project-init — greenfield initialization workflow. Collects objective and constraints, creates .agent/PROJECT-PRD.md, ensures rules and phase plans exist, and pauses for user confirmation before implementation. Role: architect.

nodes.name: trading-analysis.md
nodes.summary: Slash command /trading-analysis — invokes a 5-team multi-agent stock analysis pipeline (market, fundamentals, news, sentiment analysts; bull/bear researchers; trader; risk manager; portfolio manager) via HTTP coordinator API at :8003. Agent-agnostic HTTP interface callable by any runtime. Role: implementer.

nodes.name: /impeccable audit
nodes.summary: Sub-command for technical quality checks: accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), color contrast, keyboard navigation, semantic HTML, performance (DOM complexity, animation cost), and consistency (token usage, spacing). Uses aq-hints + AIDB /query for reference docs.

nodes.name: /impeccable craft
nodes.summary: Full design implementation sub-command with three phases: shape (propose direction, await confirmation), build (implement confirmed direction), polish (apply finishing details). Enforces user confirmation gate before implementation.

nodes.name: Harness Tool Chain (impeccable)
nodes.summary: Tool sequence for impeccable commands: aq-hints lookup, AIDB /query POST against impeccable-design project, aq-delegate to Qwen local model, and npx impeccable detect for anti-pattern scanning.

nodes.name: Trading Full Pipeline
nodes.summary: 5-team parallel/sequential agent pipeline: 4 parallel analysts (market, fundamentals, news, sentiment) → sequential bull/bear researcher debate loop → trader proposal → risk manager check → portfolio manager final APPROVED/MODIFIED/REJECTED decision.

nodes.name: Trading HTTP API
nodes.summary: HTTP API surface for the trading pipeline at coordinator :8003 — /trading/analyze (full), /trading/forecast (quick signal), /trading/tools (discovery), /trading/debate (researcher round). Agent-agnostic: any runtime can call these endpoints.

nodes.name: Prime Load Steps
nodes.summary: 6-step progressive disclosure sequence: (1) aq-hints fetch, (2) git ls-files structure inspection, (3) load PRD/rules/plans, (4) git status check, (5) coordinator capabilities query at :8003, (6) state summary output.

nodes.name: context.md
nodes.summary: Auto-loaded Gemini CLI project context injected at session start. Covers project overview, non-negotiable port policy (options.nix as SSOT), hardware constraints (Renoir APU, n_gpu_layers≤12, enable_thinking:false), recurring error patterns, and search-before-read protocol for Gemini tool use.

nodes.name: primer-summary.md

nodes.name: evaluation.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide for creating and running MCP server evaluations: question/answer guidelines, a five-step evaluation process, output format specification, worked examples (good vs. poor questions), and a complete CLI workflow for STDIO/SSE/HTTP transports with troubleshooting tips.

nodes.name: mcp_best_practices.md
nodes.summary: Canonical MCP server best-practices reference covering server/tool naming conventions, response formats, pagination patterns, transport selection (Streamable HTTP vs stdio), security (auth, input validation, DNS rebinding), tool annotations, error handling, testing, and documentation requirements.

nodes.name: node_mcp_server.md
nodes.summary: Node/TypeScript MCP server implementation guide covering MCP TypeScript SDK setup, project structure, tool registration with Zod schema validation, response formats, pagination, error handling, shared utilities, async/await patterns, package configuration, a complete working example, advanced features (resources, transport options, notifications), and a quality checklist.

nodes.name: python_mcp_server.md

nodes.name: Question Guidelines
nodes.summary: Guidelines for writing high-quality evaluation questions: core requirements (tool-exercising, verifiable, stable), complexity and depth expectations, tool-testing strategies covering multi-tool, sequential, and conditional flows, and stability rules for avoiding time-sensitive or environment-specific questions.

nodes.name: Answer Guidelines
nodes.summary: Guidelines for writing correct evaluation answers: verification requirements (exact values, JSON keys, field presence), readability rules (structure, specificity), stability rules for avoiding hard-coded UUIDs or timestamps, and diversity requirements ensuring answers differ in form across questions.

nodes.name: Evaluation Process
nodes.summary: Five-step process for generating MCP evaluations: (1) documentation inspection, (2) tool listing via list_tools, (3) developing deep tool understanding, (4) read-only content inspection for dynamic answers, (5) task generation targeting diverse tool combinations and edge cases.

nodes.name: Evaluation Examples
nodes.summary: Annotated examples of good and poor evaluation questions for MCP servers, with explanations of what makes each valid or invalid — covering tool-specific queries, multi-step flows, data retrieval verification, and anti-patterns like over-broad or environment-dependent questions.

nodes.name: Running Evaluations
nodes.summary: Operational reference for executing MCP evaluations: setup, YAML evaluation file format, CLI invocation for STDIO/SSE/HTTP transports, all command-line options (model, timeout, retries, concurrency, verbosity), output interpretation, saving reports, a complete end-to-end workflow, and troubleshooting (connection errors, low accuracy, timeouts).

nodes.name: Tool Naming and Design
nodes.summary: Conventions for MCP tool naming (snake_case, verb-noun pattern, specific prefixes) and tool design principles: single responsibility, comprehensive descriptions, explicit required vs. optional params, and avoiding side effects in read operations.

nodes.name: Transport Options
nodes.summary: Comparison of Streamable HTTP and stdio transports for MCP servers: feature matrix, use-case guidance (remote/multi-client vs. local/single-client), session management, and a decision table for selecting the appropriate transport.

nodes.name: Security Best Practices
nodes.summary: Security requirements for MCP servers: authentication and authorization patterns (API key header, JWT), input validation strategies (type checking, length limits, path traversal prevention), error handling to avoid information leakage, and DNS rebinding protection for local HTTP servers.

nodes.name: Error Handling
nodes.summary: MCP error handling best practices: structured error responses with actionable messages, McpError usage for protocol-level errors, logging for debugging, graceful degradation, and avoiding stack traces or sensitive info in error outputs.

nodes.name: Tool Implementation (Node)
nodes.summary: Node/TypeScript tool registration pattern using server.tool(): naming conventions, inputSchema definition with Zod, handler implementation with type-safe parsed inputs, result construction with content arrays, error propagation, and examples covering read, list, create, and update operations.

nodes.name: Zod Schemas for Input Validation
nodes.summary: Patterns for using Zod in MCP tool input schemas: required vs. optional fields with defaults, enum types, nested objects, array parameters, numeric constraints, and converting Zod schemas to JSON Schema via zodToJsonSchema for MCP protocol compliance.

nodes.name: Complete Example (Node)
nodes.summary: Full working Node/TypeScript MCP server example demonstrating: SDK imports, server instantiation, multiple tool registrations with Zod schemas, shared utility functions, pagination support, error handling, stdio transport setup, and the main entry point pattern.

nodes.name: Advanced MCP Features (Node)
nodes.summary: Advanced Node MCP patterns: resource registration (static and dynamic via resource templates), Streamable HTTP transport setup with Express and session management, stdio transport configuration, and notification support using server.notification() for progress and logging events.

nodes.name: Tool Implementation (Python)
nodes.summary: Python FastMCP tool registration using @mcp.tool() decorator: naming conventions, function signature as schema source, Pydantic v2 type annotations for input validation, docstring-driven descriptions, handler return types, and examples for read, list, create, and update operations.

nodes.name: Pydantic v2 Key Features
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 patterns for MCP input validation: Field() with description/default/constraints, Optional types, Literal for enums, nested model composition, validator decorators, and model_config for additional validation settings — replacing Zod schemas from the TypeScript SDK.

nodes.name: Complete Example (Python)
nodes.summary: Full working Python FastMCP server example: FastMCP instantiation, Pydantic input models, multiple @mcp.tool() handlers with async/await, shared utility functions, pagination with cursor-based offsets, structured error handling with McpError, and stdio/HTTP transport invocation.

nodes.name: Advanced FastMCP Features
nodes.summary: Advanced Python FastMCP patterns: Context parameter injection for logging/progress reporting, resource registration with @mcp.resource() decorator, structured output types using Pydantic models as return types, lifespan management via asynccontextmanager for startup/shutdown hooks, and transport configuration (stdio vs. Streamable HTTP).

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Primary PDF processing skill guide for AI agents. Covers quick-start patterns, Python libraries (pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab), CLI tools (pdftotext/qpdf/pdftk), common task recipes (OCR, watermark, image extraction, password protection), and a quick-reference table. Entry point that delegates to forms.md and reference.md for advanced topics.

nodes.name: Quick Start
nodes.summary: Entry-level usage section providing the minimal installation commands and first working code examples for reading and processing PDFs.

nodes.name: Python Libraries
nodes.summary: Covers three Python PDF libraries: pypdf for basic merge/split/metadata/rotation, pdfplumber for text-layout and table extraction, and reportlab for programmatic PDF creation with multi-page support.

nodes.name: Command-Line Tools
nodes.summary: Documents pdftotext (poppler-utils), qpdf, and pdftk command-line tools with usage examples for text extraction, PDF manipulation, and encryption.

nodes.name: Common Tasks

nodes.name: Quick Reference
nodes.summary: Condensed reference table summarising which tool or library to use for each common PDF task, enabling rapid decision-making during agent execution.

nodes.name: forms.md

Covers fillable vs non-fillable field detection, a four-step visual-analysis-to-annotation workflow: visual inspection, fields.json + validation image creation, bounding-box validation (automated intersection check + manual image review), and final annotation writing.

nodes.name: Fillable fields
nodes.summary: Describes how to detect and interact with AcroForm fillable fields in PDFs, including reading widget annotations and writing values.

nodes.name: Step 1: Visual Analysis (REQUIRED)
nodes.summary: Mandatory first step: render PDF pages to images and visually inspect all form field locations before any programmatic annotation. Ensures coordinates are accurate.

nodes.name: Step 2: Create fields.json and validation images (REQUIRED)
nodes.summary: Defines the fields.json schema for describing form field bounding boxes and generates per-field validation images to confirm box placement.

nodes.name: Step 3: Validate Bounding Boxes (REQUIRED)
nodes.summary: Two-stage bounding-box validation: automated intersection check to detect overlapping fields, and manual image inspection to confirm visual correctness.

nodes.name: reference.md
nodes.summary: Advanced PDF processing reference (612 lines, 46 sections). Covers pypdfium2 (rendering + text extraction), JavaScript libraries (pdf-lib for creation/merge/split, pdfjs-dist for rendering/text/forms), advanced CLI ops (poppler-utils with bounding boxes, qpdf encryption and repair), advanced Python techniques (pdfplumber precise coordinates, reportlab professional reports), complex workflows (figure extraction, batch processing, cropping), performance optimisation, and troubleshooting (encrypted/corrupted PDFs, text encoding issues).

nodes.name: pypdfium2 Library
nodes.summary: Apache/BSD-licensed Python library for high-quality PDF-to-image rendering and precise text extraction using PDFium engine.

nodes.name: JavaScript Libraries
nodes.summary: Covers pdf-lib (MIT) for loading, modifying, creating, merging, and splitting PDFs in JavaScript/Node.js, and pdfjs-dist (Apache) for rendering, coordinate-aware text extraction, annotations, and form reading in browser or Node contexts.

nodes.name: Advanced Command-Line Operations
nodes.summary: Advanced poppler-utils usage (text with bounding boxes, high-DPI image conversion, embedded image extraction) and qpdf advanced features (complex page manipulation, PDF optimisation and repair, advanced AES encryption).

nodes.name: Advanced Python Techniques
nodes.summary: Advanced pdfplumber usage for pixel-precise text coordinates and custom table extraction settings, plus reportlab for professional multi-column reports with styled tables and paragraph flows.

nodes.name: Complex Workflows
nodes.summary: End-to-end workflow recipes: figure/image extraction using pdfimages or pypdfium2+PIL, batch PDF processing with structured error handling and output tracking, and advanced page cropping using MediaBox manipulation.

nodes.name: Performance Optimization Tips
nodes.summary: Five categories of performance guidance: lazy page loading for large PDFs, targeted page-range extraction, streaming image extraction, cached form filling, and memory management via explicit context manager usage.

nodes.name: Troubleshooting Common Issues
nodes.summary: Diagnostic patterns for encrypted PDFs (detection and decryption), corrupted PDFs (qpdf repair), and text extraction encoding issues (CIDFont fallback, character mapping fixes).

nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML main schema — largest DML schema (231 complex types, 3081 lines). Defines core drawing primitives: colors, fills, effects, geometry, text body, shape properties, transforms, themes, and style matrices shared by all OOXML parts.

nodes.name: CT_ColorScheme
nodes.summary: Theme color scheme: 12 named color slots (dk1/lt1/dk2/lt2/accent1-6/hlink/folHlink) plus extension list.

nodes.name: CT_ShapeStyle
nodes.summary: Shape style references: line ref, fill ref, effect ref, and font ref — each pointing into the theme style matrix by index.

nodes.name: CT_Transform2D
nodes.summary: 2D transform: off (position), ext (size), optional rotation angle and flip flags — used by all positioned drawing objects.

nodes.name: dml-picture.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML picture schema (23 lines). Defines CT_Picture element combining non-visual properties, picture fill (blip), and shape properties for embedded images.

nodes.name: dml-spreadsheetDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML spreadsheet drawing schema (185 lines). Defines two-cell/one-cell/absolute anchor types for positioning drawing objects within Excel worksheets using row/column offsets.

nodes.name: dml-wordprocessingDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML wordprocessing drawing schema (287 lines). Defines inline and anchor floating drawing container types for embedding DML objects in Word documents with text-wrap, positioning, and distance attributes.

nodes.name: pml.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 PresentationML schema (149 complex types, 1676 lines). Defines CT_Presentation, CT_Slide, CT_SlideLayout, CT_SlideMaster, animation (CT_TimeNodeList), transitions, notes, comments, and handout types for PowerPoint presentations.

nodes.name: CT_Presentation
nodes.summary: Root presentation element: slide size, slide master/layout/notes master references, embedded fonts, customer data, and presentation-level properties.

nodes.name: CT_Slide
nodes.summary: Individual slide type: common slide data (shape tree, guides), color map override, transition, timing/animation, and show/hide flag.

nodes.name: CT_SlideLayout
nodes.summary: Slide layout template: inherits from slide master, defines placeholder positions/types and layout-specific shape tree, color overrides, and matching attribute.

nodes.name: shared-additionalCharacteristics.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500 shared schema for additional document characteristics (28 lines). Defines CT_AdditionalCharacteristics and CT_Characteristic for annotating application-specific document properties.

nodes.name: shared-bibliography.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500 shared bibliography schema (144 lines). Defines CT_Sources and CT_Source types for embedded citation/bibliography metadata including author, title, year, and source-type enumeration.

nodes.name: shared-commonSimpleTypes.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500 shared common simple types schema (174 lines). Base namespace (officeDocument/2006/sharedTypes) for calendar types, date types, GUID, percentage, hex color, and other atomic value types reused across all OOXML schemas.

nodes.name: shared-customXmlDataProperties.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500 shared custom XML data properties schema (25 lines). Defines CT_CustomXmlDataStorage and CT_DatastoreSchemaRefs for binding custom XML part data to schema validation.

nodes.name: shared-customXmlSchemaProperties.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500 shared custom XML schema properties (18 lines). Defines CT_SchemaLibrary and CT_Schema for maintaining the schema reference library embedded in a document.

nodes.name: shared-documentPropertiesCustom.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500 shared custom document properties schema (59 lines). Defines CT_CustomProperties and CT_Property (typed via docPropsVTypes variant) for application-defined key-value metadata.

nodes.name: shared-documentPropertiesExtended.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500 shared extended document properties schema (56 lines). Defines CT_Properties for app-level metadata: author, last-modified, revision, document stats, headings vector, and linked-document info.

nodes.name: shared-documentPropertiesVariantTypes.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500 shared document property variant types schema (195 lines, docPropsVTypes namespace). Defines CT_Variant and typed scalar elements (i1/i2/i4/i8/r4/r8/decimal/bstr/bool/date/blob etc.) used in custom property values.

nodes.name: shared-math.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500 Office Math Markup Language (OMML) schema (72 complex types, 582 lines). Defines CT_OMath, CT_OMathPara, fraction/radical/integral/nary/limit/matrix and run elements for embedding mathematical equations in OOXML documents.

nodes.name: shared-relationshipReference.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500 shared relationship reference schema (25 lines). Defines the ST_RelationshipId simple type and CT_RelId — the base type for r:id relationship references used by virtually every OOXML part.

nodes.name: sml.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 SpreadsheetML schema — largest schema (367 complex types, 4439 lines). Defines CT_Workbook, CT_Sheet, CT_Worksheet, CT_Row, CT_Cell, CT_Formula, pivot tables, named ranges, conditional formatting, data validation, and chart sheets for Excel workbooks.

nodes.name: CT_Workbook
nodes.summary: Root workbook type: sheet references, named ranges, defined names, external references, calculation properties, and pivot cache definitions.

nodes.name: CT_Sheet
nodes.summary: Sheet reference element: name, sheetId, visibility state, and r:id relationship pointing to the worksheet part.

nodes.name: CT_Cell
nodes.summary: Spreadsheet cell: formula (CT_CellFormula), value (v), inline string (is), cell type enum (b/d/e/inlineStr/n/s/str), style index (s), and array/shared formula metadata.

nodes.name: vml-main.xsd
nodes.summary: Core VML (Vector Markup Language) schema for OOXML Transitional (ISO-IEC 29500-4). Defines the primary VML namespace (urn:schemas-microsoft-com:vml) with attribute groups for shape styling (AG_CoreAttributes, AG_ShapeAttributes, AG_OfficeCoreAttributes), complex types for all VML drawing primitives (shapes, groups, ovals, rectangles, curves, lines, images), and fill/stroke/shadow/textbox sub-element types. Imports and coordinates with all other VML sub-schemas.

nodes.name: AG_CoreAttributes
nodes.summary: Core VML shape attribute group: id, style, href, target, class, title, alt, coordsize, coordorigin, wrapcoords, print.

nodes.name: AG_OfficeCoreAttributes
nodes.summary: Office-extended VML core attributes: spid, oned, regroupid, doubleclicknotify, button, userhidden, bullet, hr, hrstd, hrnoshade, hrpct, hralign, allowincell, allowoverlap, userdrawn, border colors, dgmlayout, dgmnodekind, dgmlayoutmru, insetmode. References o: namespace attributes from vml-officeDrawing.

nodes.name: AG_StrokeAttributes
nodes.summary: Stroke rendering attribute group: on, weight, color, opacity, linestyle, miterlimit, joinstyle, endcap, dashstyle, filltype, src, imageaspect, imagesize. Used by CT_Stroke complex type.

nodes.name: AG_ImageAttributes
nodes.summary: Image rendering attribute group: src, cropleft, croptop, cropright, cropbottom, gain, blacklevel, gamma, grayscale, bilevel.

nodes.name: vml-officeDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Office VML extension schema for the office:office namespace. Declares 40+ global attributes used by VML shapes (bwmode, spt, connectortype, spid, etc.), top-level elements (shapedefaults, shapelayout, signatureline, ink, diagram, equationxml), and complex types for office-specific drawing features including shape layout management, signature lines, ink annotations, and diagram wiring.

nodes.name: CT_ShapeDefaults
nodes.summary: Complex type for default shape settings containing optional fill, stroke, textbox, shadow, skew, extrusion, callout, lock, colorMru, colorMenu sub-elements. Maps to shapedefaults element.

nodes.name: CT_SignatureLine
nodes.summary: Complex type for digital signature line elements: issignatureline, id, provid, signinginstructionsset, allowcomments, showsigndate, suggestedsigner fields, signinginstructions, addlxml, sigprovurl.

nodes.name: CT_ShapeLayout
nodes.summary: Complex type managing shape ID allocation and regroup tables: contains idmap (CT_IdMap), regrouptable (CT_RegroupTable), rules (CT_Rules) sub-elements.

nodes.name: vml-presentationDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: Minimal PowerPoint-specific VML extension schema (office:powerpoint namespace). Declares two elements: iscomment (CT_Empty) and textdata (CT_Rel with id attribute). Used to embed presentation comment markers and text data references in VML drawings.

nodes.name: CT_Rel
nodes.summary: Relationship reference type with a single string id attribute. Used by textdata element to reference external text content via relationship ID.

nodes.name: vml-spreadsheetDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: Excel-specific VML extension schema (office:excel namespace). Defines CT_ClientData with 70+ optional sub-elements covering all Excel embedded object types (buttons, checkboxes, dropdowns, listboxes, scrollbars, spinners, etc.) and ST_ObjectType enumeration with 19 values. The ClientData element links VML shapes to Excel worksheet objects.

nodes.name: CT_ClientData
nodes.summary: Excel client data complex type. Required ObjectType attribute (ST_ObjectType enum). Choice of 70+ child elements covering anchor, size, lock, print, visibility, DDE, formula references (FmlaRange, FmlaLink, FmlaGroup), UI properties (TextHAlign, TextVAlign, MultiLine, VScroll), scroll bar config (Min/Max/Inc/Page/Val), and embedded object properties.

nodes.name: ST_ObjectType
nodes.summary: Enumeration of 19 Excel VML object types: Button, Checkbox, Dialog, Drop, Edit, GBox, Label, LineA, List, Movie, Note, Pict, Radio, RectA, Scroll, Spin, Shape, Group, Rect.

nodes.name: vml-wordprocessingDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: Word-specific VML extension schema (office:word namespace). Declares border elements (bordertop, borderleft, borderright, borderbottom), wrap element (CT_Wrap with type/side/anchorx/anchory), and anchorlock element. Defines border type enumerations (28 values), wrap type, wrap side, and anchor positioning enumerations for Word document layout.

nodes.name: CT_Border
nodes.summary: Word border complex type with type (ST_BorderType), width (positiveInteger), and shadow (ST_BorderShadow) attributes. Used by bordertop/left/right/bottom elements.

nodes.name: ST_BorderType
nodes.summary: Border style enumeration with 28 values: none, single, thick, double, hairline, dot, dash, dotDash, dashDotDot, triple, thinThick variants, wave, doubleWave, 3D emboss/engrave, HTML inset/outset styles.

nodes.name: CT_Wrap
nodes.summary: Text wrap complex type for VML shapes in Word: type (ST_WrapType: topAndBottom/square/none/tight/through), side (ST_WrapSide: both/left/right/largest), anchorx (ST_HorizontalAnchor), anchory (ST_VerticalAnchor).

nodes.name: wml.xsd
nodes.summary: Word Markup Language (WML) full schema for OOXML Transitional (3646 lines). Defines the complete wordprocessingml/2006/main namespace including all document structure types (CT_Document, CT_Body, CT_Paragraph, CT_Run), character and paragraph formatting, tables (CT_Table, CT_TableRow, CT_TableCell), styles (CT_Styles), numbering, headers/footers, fields, comments, revisions, document settings, and caption/caption-auto types. Root elements: document, glossaryDocument, styles.

nodes.name: CT_Document
nodes.summary: Root Word document complex type extending CT_DocumentBase. Contains optional body (CT_Body) and conformance attribute (ST_ConformanceClass). Maps to the top-level document element. Supports mc:Ignorable for markup compatibility.

nodes.name: CT_GlossaryDocument
nodes.summary: Glossary document complex type extending CT_DocumentBase with optional docParts (CT_DocParts). Used for building blocks and template content storage. Maps to glossaryDocument root element.

nodes.name: CT_Styles
nodes.summary: Word styles container type. Maps to the styles root element. Holds all named paragraph, character, table, and numbering style definitions referenced throughout the document.

nodes.name: CT_Captions
nodes.summary: Caption configuration container with one or more CT_Caption elements and optional CT_AutoCaptions. CT_Caption has name, pos (ST_CaptionPos: above/below/left/right), chapNum, heading, noLabel, numFmt, sep attributes.

nodes.name: xml.xsd
nodes.summary: W3C XML namespace schema (http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace). Defines the canonical xml:lang (language attribute), xml:space (whitespace handling: default/preserve), xml:base (URI base for relative references), and the specialAttrs attributeGroup combining all three. Imported by wml.xsd and other OOXML schemas for XML-standard attribute support.

nodes.name: specialAttrs
nodes.summary: W3C XML attributeGroup combining xml:base, xml:lang, and xml:space for use by schemas that need standard XML attribute support on their element types.

nodes.name: opc-contentTypes.xsd
nodes.summary: ECMA OPC (Open Packaging Convention) XML Schema defining content-type mappings. Declares Default (extension→MIME) and Override (part-name→MIME) elements, plus strict regex patterns for MIME type and file extension validation. Used to validate [Content_Types].xml in OOXML packages (pptx/docx/xlsx).

nodes.name: CT_Types
nodes.summary: Root container complex type holding unbounded Default and Override content-type entries.

nodes.name: CT_Default
nodes.summary: Maps a file Extension (ST_Extension) to a MIME ContentType (ST_ContentType) as a default rule.

nodes.name: CT_Override
nodes.summary: Overrides the content type for a specific package part by PartName URI.

nodes.name: ST_ContentType
nodes.summary: Simple type enforcing RFC 2045 MIME media-type syntax via a Unicode-safe regex pattern (type/subtype; params).

nodes.name: ST_Extension
nodes.summary: Simple type restricting file extension strings to URI-safe characters (pchar subset).

nodes.name: opc-coreProperties.xsd
nodes.summary: ECMA OPC schema for document core metadata. Imports Dublin Core (dc) and Dublin Core Terms (dcterms) namespaces; defines CT_CoreProperties with optional fields for creator, description, keywords, revision, timestamps, etc. Validates docProps/core.xml in OOXML packages.

nodes.name: CT_CoreProperties
nodes.summary: Root complex type (19 lines) aggregating all optional document metadata: category, contentStatus, created (dcterms), creator/description/identifier/language/subject/title (dc), keywords, lastModifiedBy, lastPrinted, modified, revision, version.

nodes.name: CT_Keywords
nodes.summary: Mixed-content container for multilingual keyword values; carries optional xml:lang attribute.

nodes.name: CT_Keyword
nodes.summary: Single keyword string extended with optional xml:lang for per-keyword language tagging.

nodes.name: opc-digSig.xsd
nodes.summary: ECMA OPC schema for digital signature support. Defines timestamp (CT_SignatureTime), relationship reference (CT_RelationshipReference / CT_RelationshipsGroupReference), and ISO 8601 datetime patterns (ST_Format, ST_Value) used to sign and validate OPC package parts.

nodes.name: CT_SignatureTime
nodes.summary: Timestamp structure for digital signatures; contains Format (ST_Format) and Value (ST_Value) child elements.

nodes.name: CT_RelationshipReference
nodes.summary: References a specific relationship by SourceId attribute for inclusion in digital signature scope.

nodes.name: CT_RelationshipsGroupReference
nodes.summary: References a group of relationships by SourceType URI for bulk signature scope inclusion.

nodes.name: ST_Format
nodes.summary: Simple type enumerating valid ISO 8601 date-time format strings (YYYY through YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sTZD).

nodes.name: ST_Value
nodes.summary: Simple type with an exhaustive regex enforcing ISO 8601 datetime values including timezone (Z or ±hh:mm), covering all six precision levels defined by ST_Format.

nodes.name: opc-relationships.xsd
nodes.summary: ECMA OPC schema for package-part relationships. Defines Relationships container and individual Relationship element with Id, Type (URI), Target (URI), and optional TargetMode (Internal|External). Validates *.rels files inside OOXML packages.

nodes.name: CT_Relationships
nodes.summary: Root container holding zero or more Relationship elements in sequence.

nodes.name: CT_Relationship
nodes.summary: Single relationship definition (10 lines): required Id (xs:ID), Type (anyURI), Target (anyURI); optional TargetMode (Internal|External via ST_TargetMode).

nodes.name: ST_TargetMode
nodes.summary: Simple type enumerating relationship target modes: External or Internal.

nodes.name: wml-2010.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2010 WordprocessingML extension schema (w14 namespace). Defines conflict-tracking types, color/drawing model types (CT_SRgbColor, CT_SchemeColor, EG_ColorTransform), paragraph identity attributes (AG_Parids, anchorId), and run-level conflict groups. Bridges ISO-IEC29500-4 WML with Word 2010-specific extensions including shadow, glow, and ligature formatting.

nodes.name: wml-2012.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2012 WordprocessingML extension schema (w15 namespace). Adds collaborative editing types: threaded comments (CT_CommentsEx, CT_CommentEx), people/presence info (CT_People, CT_Person, CT_PresenceInfo), repeating content controls (CT_SdtRepeatedSection), GUID-based document identity, and SDT appearance customization.

nodes.name: wml-2018.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2018 WordprocessingML base extension schema (w16 namespace). Provides generic extension point types CT_Extension and CT_ExtensionList with open content model (xsd:any), used as extensibility infrastructure by later Word 2018 sub-schemas.

nodes.name: wml-cex-2018.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2018 extensible comments schema (w16cex namespace). Defines CT_CommentsExtensible and CT_CommentExtensible types with durableId, dateUtc, and intelligentPlaceholder attributes. Imports from wml-2018.xsd for extension list support. Extends threaded comment model with persistent identifiers.

nodes.name: wml-cid-2016.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2016 comment IDs schema (w16cid namespace). Defines CT_CommentsIds container and CT_CommentId type associating paragraph IDs (paraId) with durable comment identifiers (durableId). Enables stable comment anchoring across document versions.

nodes.name: wml-sdtdatahash-2020.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2020 SDT data hash schema. Minimal schema defining a single storeItemChecksum attribute on the w16sdtdh namespace for structured document tag (SDT) data integrity verification.

nodes.name: wml-symex-2015.xsd
nodes.summary: Microsoft Word 2015 symbol extension schema (w16se namespace). Defines CT_SymEx with font and char attributes and a symEx element for enhanced symbol handling with explicit font/codepoint references beyond the base WML sym element.

nodes.name: CT_LongHexNumber
nodes.summary: Complex type wrapping w:ST_LongHexNumber as a required val attribute. Used for docId and paragraph identity hex values.

nodes.name: ST_OnOff
nodes.summary: Simple type restricting string to boolean values: true/false/0/1. Used for toggle attributes like noSpellErr.

nodes.name: AG_Parids
nodes.summary: Attribute group providing paraId and textId hex identifiers for paragraph-level revision tracking identity.

nodes.name: EG_RunLevelConflicts
nodes.summary: Element group for run-level conflict tracking: conflictIns and conflictDel elements of type w:CT_RunTrackChange. Supports Word co-authoring conflict resolution at the text run granularity.

nodes.name: EG_Conflicts
nodes.summary: Element group for block-level conflict tracking: choice between conflictIns and conflictDel elements of type w:CT_TrackChange. Supports co-authoring merge conflict annotation.

nodes.name: ST_SchemeColorVal
nodes.summary: Enumeration of theme color identifiers (bg1/tx1/bg2/tx2/accent1-6/hlink/folHlink/dk1/lt1/dk2/lt2/phClr) adopted from DrawingML for use in Word 2010 color definitions.

nodes.name: EG_ColorTransform
nodes.summary: Element group providing DrawingML-style color transform modifiers: tint, shade, alpha, hueMod, sat, satOff, satMod, lum, lumOff, lumMod. Enables precise color derivation from base colors.

nodes.name: ST_SdtAppearance
nodes.summary: Enumeration controlling structured document tag (SDT) visual rendering: boundingBox, tags, or hidden.

nodes.name: CT_CommentsEx
nodes.summary: Container type for extended comment metadata: unbounded sequence of CT_CommentEx items. Holds threaded comment reply state for Word 2013+ collaborative review.

nodes.name: CT_CommentEx
nodes.summary: Extended comment type with required paraId (anchor), optional paraIdParent (thread parent), and optional done (resolution flag). Enables threaded comments and comment resolution tracking.

nodes.name: CT_People
nodes.summary: Container for author identity records (CT_Person) referenced in collaborative editing sessions.

nodes.name: CT_Person
nodes.summary: Author identity type with required author string and optional presenceInfo child. Links comment authors to real-time presence providers.

nodes.name: CT_PresenceInfo
nodes.summary: Presence identity type with required providerId and userId strings. Used to associate document authors with external presence/identity services (e.g., Lync/Teams).

nodes.name: CT_SdtRepeatedSection
nodes.summary: Repeating section content control type with optional sectionTitle and doNotAllowInsertDeleteSection child elements. Controls repeatable table/list row structures in Word 2013+.

nodes.name: ST_Guid
nodes.summary: GUID simple type restricting token to standard braced hex pattern {8-4-4-4-12}. Used for w15:docId document unique identity.

nodes.name: CT_Extension
nodes.summary: Generic extension container with a uri attribute and lax open content (xsd:any). Provides forward-compatible extension points for w16 schema family.

nodes.name: CT_ExtensionList
nodes.summary: List of CT_Extension elements providing extLst extension point pattern common throughout OOXML. Consumed by wml-cex-2018.xsd.

nodes.name: CT_CommentsExtensible
nodes.summary: Root container for extensible comments part: sequence of CT_CommentExtensible items plus optional extLst. Document-level part root for w16cex:commentsExtensible.

nodes.name: CT_CommentExtensible
nodes.summary: Extensible comment type with required durableId (stable cross-revision anchor), optional dateUtc (ISO timestamp), and optional intelligentPlaceholder flag. Extends basic comment model with AI/intelligent features.

nodes.name: CT_CommentsIds
nodes.summary: Container for comment ID mapping records: unbounded CT_CommentId sequence. Root type for the w16cid:commentsIds part.

nodes.name: CT_CommentId
nodes.summary: Comment identity mapping: pairs paraId (paragraph anchor hex) with durableId (persistent comment reference hex). Ensures comment references survive replication and merge.

nodes.name: CT_SymEx
nodes.summary: Symbol extension type with font (string) and char (LongHexNumber) attributes. Provides explicit font+codepoint symbol references as a more precise alternative to the base WML sym element.

nodes.name: LICENSE.txt
nodes.summary: Apache License Version 2.0 governing the slack-gif-creator skill package.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Agent skill specification for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack. Covers Slack size/FPS constraints, core PIL-based workflow, drawing primitives, available utility modules (GIFBuilder, validators, easing, frame_composer), animation concepts, optimization strategies, and design philosophy.

nodes.name: Slack Requirements
nodes.summary: Defines Slack-specific GIF constraints: emoji GIFs 128x128, message GIFs 480x480, FPS 10-30, colors 48-128, max 3s for emoji GIFs.

nodes.name: Core Workflow
nodes.summary: Step-by-step Python workflow using GIFBuilder and PIL: instantiate builder, generate frames with ImageDraw, call builder.save() with optimization flags.

nodes.name: Drawing Graphics
nodes.summary: Guidance on working with user-uploaded images vs. drawing from scratch with PIL ImageDraw primitives (ellipse, polygon, line, rectangle). Includes design advice: thick lines, gradients, layered shapes, vibrant colors for polished output.

nodes.name: Available Utilities
nodes.summary: Documents four core modules: GIFBuilder (frame assembly + optimization), validators (validate_gif / is_slack_ready), easing (interpolate with linear/ease/bounce/elastic/back curves), and frame_composer (blank frames, gradient backgrounds, helper draw functions).

nodes.name: Animation Concepts
nodes.summary: Explains eight animation patterns: Shake/Vibrate (sin offset), Pulse/Heartbeat (sin scale), Bounce (easing), Spin/Rotate (PIL rotate), Fade In/Out (alpha channel), Slide (interpolate ease_out), Zoom (scale + crop), Explode/Particle Burst (velocity + gravity).

nodes.name: Optimization Strategies
nodes.summary: Five file-size reduction strategies: lower FPS, fewer colors (num_colors=48), smaller dimensions, remove_duplicates=True, and optimize_for_emoji=True flag in GIFBuilder.save().

nodes.name: Philosophy
nodes.summary: Skill scope definition: provides knowledge, utilities, and flexibility via PIL primitives. Explicitly excludes rigid templates, emoji font rendering, and pre-packaged graphics libraries.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python package dependencies for the slack-gif-creator skill: pillow>=10.0.0, imageio>=2.31.0, imageio-ffmpeg>=0.4.9, numpy>=1.24.0.

nodes.name: arctic-frost
nodes.summary: Theme definition for the Arctic Frost visual style — a cool, crisp winter-inspired palette (ice blue, steel blue, silver, crisp white) with DejaVu Sans typography. Best suited for healthcare, technology, clean tech, and pharmaceutical presentations.

nodes.name: botanical-garden
nodes.summary: Theme definition for the Botanical Garden visual style — a vibrant, organic palette (fern green, marigold, terracotta, cream) with DejaVu Serif headers. Targets garden, food, farm-to-table, and natural product presentations.

nodes.name: desert-rose
nodes.summary: Theme definition for the Desert Rose visual style — a soft, muted palette (dusty rose, clay, sand, deep burgundy) with FreeSans typography. Oriented toward fashion, beauty, wedding planning, and boutique business contexts.

nodes.name: forest-canopy
nodes.summary: Theme definition for the Forest Canopy visual style — an earthy palette (forest green, sage, olive, ivory) with FreeSerif headers and FreeSans body. Suited for environmental, sustainability, outdoor, and wellness content.

nodes.name: golden-hour
nodes.summary: Theme definition for the Golden Hour visual style — a warm, autumnal palette (mustard yellow, terracotta, warm beige, chocolate brown) with FreeSans typography. Targets restaurant, hospitality, lifestyle, and artisan contexts.

nodes.name: midnight-galaxy
nodes.summary: Theme definition for the Midnight Galaxy visual style — a dramatic, deep palette (deep purple, cosmic blue, lavender, silver) with FreeSans typography. Intended for entertainment, gaming, luxury, and creative agency presentations.

nodes.name: modern-minimalist
nodes.summary: Theme definition for the Modern Minimalist visual style — a clean grayscale palette (charcoal, slate gray, light gray, white) with DejaVu Sans typography. Maximally versatile for tech, architecture, design, and data visualization presentations.

nodes.name: ocean-depths
nodes.summary: Theme definition for the Ocean Depths visual style — a professional maritime palette (deep navy, teal, seafoam, cream) with DejaVu Sans typography. Suited for corporate, financial, and professional consulting presentations.

nodes.name: sunset-boulevard
nodes.summary: Theme definition for the Sunset Boulevard visual style — a warm, vibrant palette (burnt orange, coral, warm sand, deep purple) with DejaVu Serif headers. Targets creative pitches, marketing, lifestyle brands, and event promotions.

nodes.name: tech-innovation
nodes.summary: Theme definition for the Tech Innovation visual style — a bold, high-contrast palette (electric blue, neon cyan, dark gray, white) with DejaVu Sans typography. Designed for tech startups, software launches, AI/ML, and digital transformation content.

nodes.name: ACTIVATION-STATUS.md
nodes.summary: Activation status tracker for the Autonomous Improvement System. Documents current activation state (completed vs pending steps), post-activation validation checklist (service creation, timer, CLI, dry-run, database), first-24h monitoring expectations, behavioral scenarios, troubleshooting for common failures (timer, LLM, DB), success metrics, configuration options, and Phase 2 preparation roadmap.

nodes.name: DEPLOYMENT.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step deployment guide for the Autonomous Improvement System on NixOS. Covers 6 deployment steps (verify state, nixos-rebuild, verify service activation, dry-run test, monitor, enable production), troubleshooting (timer, service start, Python imports, DB connection, LLM), configuration (interval, manual trigger, daemon mode, disable), cycle phase descriptions (metric sync, trigger check, research, execution, learning), database table overview, and success indicators.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary documentation for the Autonomous Improvement System. Describes the 5-component architecture (trend_database.py for metric storage, trigger_engine.py for anomaly detection, research_phase.py for LLM-driven analysis, autonomous_loop.py for orchestration, aq-autonomous-improve CLI wrapper), database schema, NixOS deployment steps, a worked example (cache-hit-rate degradation scenario), security model, future phases roadmap, and key innovation summary.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependency manifest for the autonomous improvement subsystem. Declares psycopg2-binary>=2.9.9 (PostgreSQL adapter) and aiohttp>=3.9.0 (async HTTP client for LLM/coordinator calls).

nodes.name: context-scan.json
nodes.summary: JSON validation schema for context-scan agent output. Enforces required fields: content (str, min 20 chars via regex), sources (list), confidence (float), and constraints_respected (bool, must be true). Used to validate structured LLM output before persistence.

nodes.name: draft-plan.json
nodes.summary: JSON validation schema for draft-plan agent output. Enforces required fields: plan (dict) and constraints_respected (bool, must be true). No regex constraints. Validates planning-stage structured output.

nodes.name: final-aggregation.json
nodes.summary: JSON validation schema for final aggregation agent output. Enforces required fields: final_answer (str, min 20 chars), merged_sources (list), issues (list), and constraints_respected (bool, must be true). Terminal stage schema in multi-step pipeline.

nodes.name: web-brief.json
nodes.summary: JSON validation schema for web-brief agent output. Enforces required fields: content (str, min 20 chars), sources (list), and constraints_respected (bool, must be true). Validates web-sourced content summaries before storage.

nodes.name: eval-20260224T092035Z.json
nodes.summary: Promptfoo evaluation run (eval-sbZ-2026-02-24T09:20:39) against local llama.cpp at :8080. 9 test cases, 8 passed (score 8.5/9), 505s total latency.
