nodes.name: TOOL_DECORATORS.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for a strands-agents-inspired @tool decorator system. Covers automatic JSON Schema generation from Python type hints, hot-reloading, provider-based tool organization, and integration with the MCP bridge via get_tool_registry().

nodes.name: Quick Start
nodes.summary: Usage examples for the @tool decorator: basic function decoration with type hints, custom name/provider metadata, and ToolProvider class for grouping related tools.

nodes.name: Tool Registry
nodes.summary: Global ToolRegistry API: list_tools, get_tool, get_tool_names, unregister, reload_tool. Provides hot-reload capability and provider-filtered listing.

nodes.name: Integration with MCP Bridge
nodes.summary: Shows how get_tool_registry() is consumed inside mcp-bridge-hybrid.py to expose decorated tools as MCP tool schemas (name, description, inputSchema).

nodes.name: API Reference
nodes.summary: Formal API for @tool decorator, ToolRegistry class, ToolProvider class, and ToolDefinition dataclass with all parameters and return values documented.

nodes.name: system-prompt-evaluation.md
nodes.summary: Analysis comparing system prompt v1 (216 lines, ~2100 tokens) vs v2-optimized (84 lines, ~750 tokens, 64% token reduction) targeting Gemma 4 E4B. Covers optimization strategy, A/B test corpus of 20 prompts, performance metrics, rollback plan.

nodes.name: Optimization Strategy
nodes.summary: Five optimization steps applied to reduce prompt size: removed verbose JSON schemas and delegation protocol details, simplified tool descriptions (dropped optional params), consolidated routing rules to a single decision tree, replaced JSON response format with natural language guidelines, added a 5-step task workflow.

nodes.name: A/B Testing Plan
nodes.summary: 20-prompt test corpus across 5 categories (simple queries, tool use, delegation, planning, code tasks) with scoring metrics: tool selection accuracy, delegation decisions, response quality, response length, token usage.

nodes.name: system-prompt-v2-optimized.md
nodes.summary: Optimized system prompt for Gemma 4 E4B local orchestrator. Defines 7 MCP tools, routing rules (local vs Claude Sonnet/Opus delegation by complexity), 5-step task workflow, response guidelines, critical constraints, and conventional commit format.

nodes.name: Routing Rules
nodes.summary: Decision table: handle locally (simple queries, retrieval, status, planning); delegate to Claude Sonnet for code >50 lines or complex refactoring; delegate to Claude Opus for architecture or security audits. Cost-awareness constraint: delegation is expensive.

nodes.name: Task Workflow
nodes.summary: 5-step workflow: Understand → Search (hybrid_search/get_hints) → Decide (local vs delegate) → Execute → Validate.

nodes.name: system-prompt.md
nodes.summary: Original v1 system prompt for the Local Orchestrator on Qwen3.6-35B. Defines 5-persona dynamic role matrix (Architect/Implementer/Reviewer/SRE/SDET), 8 MCP tools, 6 domain shells, 8-step workflow canon, and bitemporal memory via aq-crystallize.

nodes.name: Dynamic Role Matrix
nodes.summary: 5-persona role adoption based on workflow phase: Architect (Orient/Research/Plan), Implementer (Execute), Reviewer (Validate), SRE/DevOps (Homeostasis), SDET (QA). References docs/architecture/role-matrix.md as SSOT.

nodes.name: Available Tools (MCP Protocol)
nodes.summary: 8 MCP tools grouped by category: Knowledge & Context (hybrid_search, get_hints, query_aidb, recall_memory), Implementation & Hardware (opencode, aq-qa, aq-report, harness_health), Planning & Workflows (workflow_plan, aqd_workflows_list).

nodes.name: Operating Principles
nodes.summary: 4 operating principles: (1) 8-step workflow canon mandatory, (2) local-first orchestration (delegate to Claude/Codex only for >100 lines), (3) declarative consistency (Nix-based, no hardcoded values), (4) bitemporal memory with aq-crystallize for promotion.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive documentation for the AIDB MCP Server (FastAPI, port 8091) covering architecture, RAG data flow, API endpoints, PostgreSQL/Qdrant database schema, environment variable configuration with sops-nix secrets, deployment on NixOS and K3s, monitoring, troubleshooting, and performance benchmarks.

nodes.name: Architecture
nodes.summary: Lists the AIDB server's Python module layout: server.py, settings_loader.py, skills_loader.py, registry_api.py, parallel_inference.py, ml_engine.py, codemachine_client.py, mindsdb_client.py, llama_cpp_tool_agent.py, llm_parallel.py, and middleware/.

nodes.name: RAG Data Flow
nodes.summary: Describes the 5-step RAG pipeline: Ingest documents → Chunk with ChunkingStrategy + embed_texts → Store embeddings in Postgres pgvector via index_embeddings → Retrieve with search_vectors → Assemble context in semantic_search and get_related_docs MCP actions.

nodes.name: Configuration
nodes.summary: Documents all environment variables for database (PostgreSQL, Redis, Qdrant), API keys, and AIDB service settings. Emphasises FILE-based secrets in production via sops-nix paths (e.g. AIDB_POSTGRES_PASSWORD_FILE=/run/secrets/postgres_password).

nodes.name: API Endpoints
nodes.summary: Catalogs AIDB HTTP endpoints: health check (/health), document management (/documents, /document_importer), vector search (/search_vectors, /semantic_search), model inference (/inference, /parallel_inference), and agent skills (/skills, /load_skill, /execute_skill).

nodes.name: Database Schema
nodes.summary: Documents PostgreSQL tables (imported_documents, document_embeddings with pgvector, agent_skills, model_registry, inference_logs) and Qdrant collections (mcp-embeddings, skill-embeddings) used by the AIDB server.

nodes.name: SECURITY-NOTES.md
nodes.summary: Security incident post-mortem (2026-03-02) and mandatory secrets management guidance for the AIDB MCP Server. Covers sops-nix production architecture, the _read_secret() Python pattern, systemd EnvironmentFile wiring, AI agent coding guidance to avoid hardcoded credentials, audit scripts, and incident response steps.

nodes.name: Production Secrets Architecture
nodes.summary: Defines the sops-nix secrets flow: Age-encrypted secrets.sops.yaml → decrypted at runtime to /run/secrets/{postgres_password,aidb_api_key,embeddings_api_key,redis_password,hybrid_api_key}. Services access them via *_FILE environment variables and _read_secret() helper.

nodes.name: AI Agent Guidance
nodes.summary: Direct guidance for AI agents reading the codebase: always use _read_secret() with *_FILE env vars; never suggest hardcoded passwords; use correct NixOS systemd EnvironmentFile pattern; example production-safe code snippet included.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependency manifest (v3.0.0, Nov 2025) for the AIDB MCP Server: FastAPI 0.122 + uvicorn, psycopg3 (async PostgreSQL), asyncpg, Redis 7 with hiredis, SQLAlchemy 2, pgvector, qdrant-client 1.16, httpx, llama-index-core 0.14 + HuggingFace sentence-transformers, OpenTelemetry stack, scikit-learn/numpy/scipy/pandas, and pytest/locust for testing.

nodes.name: start_with_discovery.sh
nodes.summary: Bash entry-point that exports AIDB_TOOL_DISCOVERY_ENABLED=true and AIDB_TOOL_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL=300, starts server.py and tool_discovery_daemon.py as background processes, then uses 'wait -n' to detect the first exit and kills both processes to ensure clean shutdown.

nodes.name: config.yaml
nodes.summary: Base configuration for the aidb-mcp server. Defines 15 top-level sections: server (host/port/workers), database (postgres pool + redis pool with Docker secrets), llm (llama.cpp endpoint, parallel processing), tools (sandbox, caching, disclosure), logging, security (API key via secrets file, rate limit), rate_limiting, continuous_learning (checkpoint interval, backpressure), rag (BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 embeddings, pgvector HNSW settings, context size limits), websearch, telemetry, embeddings (batching, timeouts), hybrid (qdrant + llama-cpp + embedding service URLs, confidence thresholds), garbage_collection (daily cron, age/value/dedup thresholds), and query_validation (size limits, rate limits, allowed_collections list of 6 named Qdrant collections).

nodes.name: config.dev.yaml
nodes.summary: Development environment override for aidb-mcp: sets logging.level=DEBUG and disables security.rate_limit to allow unrestricted local requests during development.

nodes.name: config.prod.yaml
nodes.summary: Production environment override for aidb-mcp: INFO logging, rate limiting enabled at 240 requests/minute (4x the base 60 rpm), and telemetry explicitly enabled.

nodes.name: config.staging.yaml
nodes.summary: Staging environment override for aidb-mcp: INFO logging, rate limiting enabled at 120 requests/minute — halfway between dev (disabled) and prod (240 rpm).

nodes.name: BEFORE-AFTER-COMPARISON.md
nodes.summary: Technical reference comparing sequential vs parallel implementations across 4 Phase 5.2 optimizations in the hybrid coordinator: parallelization of independent operations, backend selection caching, collection timeout guards, and smart backend deferment. Includes timeline showing ~600ms saved per query.

nodes.name: OPTIMIZATION-SUMMARY.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Phase 5.2 route search optimization summary covering 4 strategies (parallelization, backend caching, timeout guards, deferment), metrics/monitoring, expected P95 latency reduction, adaptive timeout and cache-size configuration, unit test coverage, and validation checklist.

nodes.name: QUALITY_CACHE_INTEGRATION.md
nodes.summary: Integration guide for quality response caching at three points in the hybrid coordinator: handle_query (line 2087), delegation_feedback.py, and hint delivery. Covers cache configuration, monitoring, testing plan, and next steps.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary documentation for the Hybrid Agent Coordinator MCP Server. Documents four feature pillars (context augmentation, query routing, continuous learning, knowledge management), eight MCP tools (augment_query, track_interaction, update_outcome, hybrid_search, route_query, learning_feedback, generate_training_data, search_context), five Qdrant collections, value scoring, environment variables, integration examples, fine-tuning workflow, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: README.sdk.md
nodes.summary: SDK documentation for the hybrid coordinator covering A2A compatibility surface, Python SDK usage, TypeScript/JavaScript SDK usage, full API surface reference, and release automation instructions for both npm and PyPI packages.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: npm package manifest for @nixos-ai/harness-sdk v0.1.0 — ESM TypeScript/JavaScript SDK for NixOS AI hybrid coordinator workflow APIs. Entry point harness_sdk.js with TypeScript declarations. Includes check and pack:dry scripts.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python package configuration for nixos-ai-harness-sdk v0.1.0 using setuptools. SDK for hybrid coordinator workflow/review/eval APIs. Requires Python 3.11+, depends on httpx>=0.27. Single module: harness_sdk.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python runtime dependencies for the hybrid coordinator MCP server: qdrant-client, httpx, aiohttp, mcp framework, pydantic, python-dotenv, pyyaml, structlog, prometheus-client, full OpenTelemetry stack (api/sdk/otlp/aiohttp), psycopg3, sqlalchemy, redis with hiredis, pytest and locust for testing.

nodes.name: start_with_learning.sh
nodes.summary: Bash startup script that launches server.py and optionally continuous_learning_daemon.py in background. Reads CONTINUOUS_LEARNING_ENABLED, LEARNING_PROCESSING_INTERVAL, LEARNING_DATASET_THRESHOLD from environment. Waits on either PID and kills both on exit.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive documentation for the NixOS Documentation MCP Server — a FastAPI service aggregating NixOS docs from multiple sources (nixos.org, NixOS GitHub, nixpkgs, home-manager) with Redis caching, disk cache fallback, full-text and vector search, and AI stack integration (AIDB, Ralph Wiggum). Covers API endpoints (/search, /packages, /options, /sources, /sync, /cache), architecture/data-flow, environment config, deployment (K3s, standalone), monitoring, performance benchmarks, troubleshooting, and roadmap.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependency manifest for the NixOS docs MCP server. Key groups: web framework (fastapi 0.122, uvicorn, pydantic 2.x), HTTP clients (httpx, aiohttp, requests), doc parsers (beautifulsoup4, lxml, markdown, markdownify), data stores (psycopg2-binary, redis, sqlalchemy), vector embeddings (sentence-transformers), full-text search (whoosh), disk cache (diskcache), observability (structlog, prometheus-client, opentelemetry), and test tooling (pytest, locust).

nodes.name: start_server.sh
nodes.summary: Container entry-point script for the NixOS docs MCP server. Waits up to 30s for Redis on ${REDIS_HOST}:${REDIS_PORT} (falls back to disk-cache-only mode), then spawns a background Python process that calls `clone_or_update_repo` for every entry in `DOCUMENTATION_SOURCES` from server.py to pre-sync all doc repos. Finally execs `python3 /app/server.py` to start the FastAPI service on port 8094.

nodes.name: codebase-analysis.sop.md
nodes.summary: SOP defining the standard procedure for comprehensive codebase analysis: project discovery (structure, deps, build config), code quality assessment (organization, static analysis, security scan), documentation review, testing infrastructure evaluation, and output reporting to `.agents/summary/codebase-analysis.md`. Governed by MUST/SHOULD/MAY RFC-style requirements.

nodes.name: Discovery Phase
nodes.summary: MUST steps: identify project structure and technology stack (directory layout, languages, frameworks, config files), analyze dependency management (package.json/requirements.txt/Cargo.toml, direct/transitive deps, outdated/vulnerable flags), SHOULD review build and deployment configuration (CI/CD pipelines, deployment manifests, env-specific configs).

nodes.name: Code Quality Assessment
nodes.summary: MUST evaluate code organization (module structure, naming conventions, architectural patterns). SHOULD perform static analysis (linters, anti-patterns, complexity metrics). MAY conduct security scanning (SAST, hardcoded secrets, secure coding practices).

nodes.name: Documentation Review
nodes.summary: SHOULD assess documentation completeness (README, contributing guidelines, API docs, inline comments). MAY evaluate documentation quality (outdated info, functional examples, clarity).

nodes.name: Testing Infrastructure
nodes.summary: MUST identify test coverage (locate test files and frameworks, measure coverage percentage, identify untested critical paths). SHOULD evaluate test quality (unit/integration/e2e presence, maintainability, passing status).

nodes.name: Output Requirements
nodes.summary: MUST generate analysis report saved to `.agents/summary/codebase-analysis.md` with metrics and recommendations. SHOULD create issue tracker entries with labels and priority. MAY produce visualization artifacts (dependency graphs, architecture diagrams, metrics dashboards).

nodes.name: deployment-check.sop.md
nodes.summary: SOP for deployment readiness validation covering: pre-deployment checks (tests, build artifacts, critical bugs), configuration verification (env vars, infrastructure changes, load testing), dependency checks, rollback preparation and testing, deployment execution with gradual rollout and smoke tests, post-deployment health monitoring, rollback triggers, and output report saved to `.agents/summary/deployment-{timestamp}.md`.

nodes.name: Pre-Deployment Validation
nodes.summary: MUST verify all tests pass (full suite, zero failures, coverage threshold met), validate build artifacts (clean build, checksums, version tagging), and MUST NOT deploy with known critical bugs (P0 bugs resolved, no security vulnerabilities).

nodes.name: Configuration Verification
nodes.summary: MUST validate environment configuration (env vars, target environment match, no hardcoded secrets). SHOULD review infrastructure changes (database migrations, resource scaling, networking/firewall). MAY perform load testing (performance benchmarks, auto-scaling validation).

nodes.name: Dependency Checks
nodes.summary: MUST verify dependency compatibility (availability, version compatibility, license compliance). SHOULD update dependency lock files (package-lock.json, Cargo.lock, verify no unexpected changes, commit updates).

nodes.name: Rollback Preparation
nodes.summary: MUST prepare rollback strategy (document procedure, database backup, tag previous stable version). SHOULD test rollback procedure in staging and prepare communication plan.

nodes.name: Deployment Execution
nodes.summary: MUST follow deployment runbook (ordered steps, record times, monitor health). SHOULD enable gradual rollout (deploy to subset, monitor error rates, progressively increase traffic). MUST NOT skip smoke tests (post-deployment verification of critical user flows and integration points).

nodes.name: Post-Deployment Validation
nodes.summary: MUST monitor application health (error rates, logs, service health, performance metrics). SHOULD validate user experience (critical workflows, UI/UX changes, cross-browser). MUST document deployment outcome (timestamp, version, issues, changelog update).

nodes.name: Rollback Triggers
nodes.summary: MUST rollback if error rate exceeds defined threshold (automatic rollback, immediate team notification). SHOULD rollback for significant performance degradation (latency/throughput vs baseline comparison).

nodes.name: Output Requirements
nodes.summary: MUST generate deployment report saved to `.agents/summary/deployment-{timestamp}.md` with all validation results and issue resolutions. SHOULD update deployment dashboard (status, version info, artifact links).

nodes.name: test-validation.sop.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive SOP for test suite validation covering all test levels: discovery (frameworks/coverage mapping), unit tests (coverage threshold ≥80%, isolation), integration tests (mocks, data management, contract testing), E2E and visual regression, performance/stress testing, security scanning (dependency vulns, auth flows, no hardcoded creds), test maintenance (flaky tests), CI pipeline validation, coverage analysis, and failure handling with deployment gate. Output saved to `.agents/summary/test-validation-{timestamp}.md`.

nodes.name: Test Discovery
nodes.summary: MUST identify all test files and frameworks (unit tests, integration/e2e tests, config files). SHOULD catalog test types and coverage (frameworks like pytest/jest, map tests to modules, calculate coverage baseline).

nodes.name: Unit Test Validation
nodes.summary: MUST run all unit tests (full suite, zero failures, execution time check). MUST achieve minimum coverage threshold (line/branch coverage, meet or exceed threshold, identify uncovered critical paths). SHOULD validate test quality (isolation, no interdependencies, deterministic execution).

nodes.name: Integration Test Validation
nodes.summary: MUST run integration tests (full suite, external service mocks, database state management). SHOULD validate test data management (fixtures, cleanup, no test pollution). MAY perform contract testing (API contracts, backward compatibility, schema validation).

nodes.name: End-to-End Test Validation
nodes.summary: SHOULD run e2e test suite (critical user journeys, UI automation, cross-browser compatibility). MAY run visual regression tests (screenshot capture/comparison, unintended UI change detection, responsive design verification).

nodes.name: Performance Testing
nodes.summary: SHOULD run performance benchmarks (load tests, response times, resource utilization). MAY perform stress testing (system limits, bottleneck identification, graceful degradation verification).

nodes.name: Security Testing
nodes.summary: MUST scan for security vulnerabilities (dependency scans, common security issues, input validation). SHOULD perform authentication testing (auth/authorization flows, session management, privilege escalation checks). MUST NOT expose sensitive data in tests (no hardcoded credentials, test log secrets check, secure test data handling).

nodes.name: Test Maintenance
nodes.summary: SHOULD identify flaky tests (multi-run execution, failure pattern tracking, fix or quarantine). MAY update test documentation (patterns, conventions, guidelines, examples for new tests).

nodes.name: Continuous Integration Validation
nodes.summary: MUST verify CI pipeline runs (all jobs execute, results reported, artifacts generated). SHOULD validate pre-commit hooks (test on sample commits, formatting/linting verification, commit message validation).

nodes.name: Coverage Analysis
nodes.summary: MUST generate coverage reports (HTML and JSON, highlight uncovered lines, track coverage trends). SHOULD identify coverage gaps (list uncovered files, prioritize critical uncovered code, create improvement tasks).

nodes.name: Output Requirements
nodes.summary: MUST generate test validation report saved to `.agents/summary/test-validation-{timestamp}.md` with pass/fail counts, coverage metrics and trends. SHOULD create test improvement plan (issues, coverage improvements, priority). MAY generate test badges (coverage badge, build status badge, README update).

nodes.name: Failure Handling
nodes.summary: MUST investigate test failures immediately (analyze logs, reproduce locally, determine root cause). SHOULD NOT ignore failing tests (fix or disable, document known issues, track test debt). MUST block deployment on critical test failures (define critical suite, prevent deployment, require manual override with justification).

nodes.name: add_ralph_repos_metadata.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the ralph_implementations table and seeds it with 5 Ralph Wiggum autonomous-agent repositories; also creates the recommended_ralph_implementations view and updates package_versions/package_changelogs with cross-references.

nodes.name: ralph_implementations
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL table tracking Ralph Wiggum AI-agent GitHub repositories with fields for repo metadata, star count, JSONB key_features, status (production/experimental), and recommended flag.

nodes.name: recommended_ralph_implementations
nodes.summary: View over ralph_implementations that filters to recommended=TRUE rows and orders by status priority then stars descending, providing a curated list of production-grade Ralph implementations.

nodes.name: comprehensive_update_jan2026.sql
nodes.summary: January 2026 bulk migration: inserts missing containers into package_versions, updates known package versions, creates critical_updates_needed view, and introduces upgrade_edge_cases and dependency_conflicts tables with pre-populated data.

nodes.name: upgrade_edge_cases
nodes.summary: Table recording per-upgrade migration risks: breaking_changes flag, data_backup_required, estimated_downtime_minutes, and mitigation_steps for major version jumps across stack components.

nodes.name: dependency_conflicts
nodes.summary: Table capturing known version and API conflicts between package pairs (e.g. aider-chat vs aiohttp, fastapi vs starlette), with conflict_type enum and resolution guidance.

nodes.name: critical_updates_needed
nodes.summary: View selecting high-priority packages (jaeger, aider-chat, prometheus, asyncpg, postgres, qdrant) from packages_needing_updates with an urgency_reason CASE expression ordered by criticality.

nodes.name: package_versions_schema.sql
nodes.summary: Canonical schema definition for the package version tracking subsystem: creates package_versions, package_changelogs, and package_dependencies tables with indexes, seeds initial data for the AI stack, defines the packages_needing_updates view, and implements the get_update_summary() PL/pgSQL function.

nodes.name: package_versions
nodes.summary: Core table tracking current vs latest version of every package across the AI stack; includes a generated STORED column update_available computed from current_version != latest_version.

nodes.name: package_changelogs
nodes.summary: Table storing release notes, breaking_changes and security_fixes flags per package version; unique on (package_name, version) to allow upsert patterns.

nodes.name: package_dependencies
nodes.summary: Table mapping parent-to-dependency relationships with version_constraint and required flag; unique on (parent_package, dependency_package).

nodes.name: packages_needing_updates
nodes.summary: View over package_versions that computes action_needed (install/check/update/current) and orders results with aider-chat, fastapi, asyncpg at the top to guide automated upgrade workflows.

nodes.name: get_update_summary
nodes.summary: PL/pgSQL function returning aggregate counts of total_packages, needs_install, needs_update, and up_to_date from package_versions in a single query.

nodes.name: update_package_versions_jan2026.sql
nodes.summary: Data-only migration script that updates current_version and latest_version for 8 packages to verified January 2026 releases and inserts corresponding changelog entries for aider-chat, qdrant, postgres, llama.cpp, redis, prometheus, and grafana.

nodes.name: conditional-deployment
nodes.summary: Conditional deployment workflow that gates test execution and approval based on boolean inputs, then deploys only when approval decision is 'yes'

nodes.name: test
nodes.summary: Conditionally runs test suite on build artifacts with up to 3 retry attempts on failure

nodes.name: approval
nodes.summary: Requests claude approval based on build status, test results, and coverage before deployment proceeds

nodes.name: deploy
nodes.summary: Deploys artifacts to target environment only when approval decision evaluates to 'yes'

nodes.name: notify
nodes.summary: Sends post-deployment notification with environment, status, and deployment URL

nodes.name: resilient-deployment
nodes.summary: Deployment workflow with layered retry strategies (exponential and linear backoff), on_error rollback handlers, and claude-powered failure investigation

nodes.name: preflight-checks
nodes.summary: Pre-deployment validation of dependencies, database access, config, and resources with 3-attempt exponential backoff retry

nodes.name: deploy
nodes.summary: Service deployment with 5-attempt exponential retry and rollback error handler that halts workflow on failure

nodes.name: health-check
nodes.summary: Post-deployment health verification with 10-attempt linear backoff (3s base) and rollback trigger on persistent service-not-ready failure

nodes.name: smoke-tests
nodes.summary: Post-health smoke tests with 3-attempt retry on test_flake; routes to investigate-failure handler but continues workflow on error

nodes.name: investigate-failure
nodes.summary: Claude-powered root cause analysis of smoke test failures producing investigation report and recommended actions

nodes.name: feature-implementation
nodes.summary: Feature implementation workflow using iterative loop execution, conditional testing and code review, goto-based revision cycle, and configurable multi-agent roles (implementer/reviewer)

nodes.name: analyze
nodes.summary: Breaks down feature spec into concrete testable tasks using memory layers L0-L3 with architecture and patterns context

nodes.name: implement
nodes.summary: Iteratively implements tasks in loop until ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE condition met; max 20 iterations with fresh context per pass

nodes.name: test
nodes.summary: Conditionally writes comprehensive tests for changed files with 3-attempt retry on test_failure

nodes.name: review
nodes.summary: Conditional codex review of implementation and tests checking code quality, security, performance, and coverage

nodes.name: revise
nodes.summary: Conditionally addresses review feedback and loops back to review via goto when decision is 'needs_revision'

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Final validation of test passing, linter checks, and documentation when review approves implementation

nodes.name: iterative-refactoring
nodes.summary: Iterative code refactoring workflow that loops with memory-aware context per pass until a numeric quality threshold score is met, capped at 10 iterations

nodes.name: initial-assessment
nodes.summary: Codex quality scoring on 0-10 scale across readability, maintainability, test coverage, and documentation with issue enumeration

nodes.name: refactor
nodes.summary: Iterative refactoring loop until quality score meets threshold; one focused improvement per iteration with memory layers L0-L3

nodes.name: final-assessment
nodes.summary: Codex final quality verification comparing original vs refactored score and confirming no new issues introduced

nodes.name: parallel-analysis
nodes.summary: Parallel codebase analysis workflow running security (claude), performance (qwen), and code quality (codex) scans concurrently then aggregating into prioritized report

nodes.name: security-scan
nodes.summary: Parallel claude security audit covering authentication, input validation, SQL injection, XSS, and secret exposure

nodes.name: performance-scan
nodes.summary: Parallel qwen performance analysis of algorithmic complexity, DB query efficiency, memory usage, and concurrency issues

nodes.name: code-quality-scan
nodes.summary: Parallel codex code quality review of structure, naming conventions, error handling, test coverage, and documentation

nodes.name: aggregate
nodes.summary: Fan-in aggregation by claude of all parallel scan results into executive summary with priority-ranked actions and effort estimates

nodes.name: simple-sequential
nodes.summary: Minimal three-step sequential workflow template demonstrating analyze → execute → validate pattern with basic L0-L1 memory usage

nodes.name: analyze
nodes.summary: Decomposes task description into actionable steps with effort estimation using L0-L1 memory layers

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Codex validation of execution result against original task requirements, reporting status and any issues found

nodes.name: multi-service-deployment
nodes.summary: Multi-service deployment orchestrator using sub-workflow composition to invoke resilient-deployment per service, then runs integration tests and conditionally updates routing or triggers full rollback

nodes.name: validate-environment
nodes.summary: Validates target environment readiness by checking infrastructure, dependencies, and available capacity before service deployment

nodes.name: deploy-services
nodes.summary: Loops over service list invoking resilient-deployment.yaml sub-workflow per service until ALL_SERVICES_DEPLOYED; max 50 iterations with deployment progress tracking

nodes.name: integration-tests
nodes.summary: Cross-service integration tests covering service-to-service communication, E2E workflows, and data consistency with 3-attempt retry on test_flake

nodes.name: rollback-all
nodes.summary: Conditionally rolls back all deployed services when integration tests report 'failed' result

nodes.name: agent-routing-test
nodes.summary: Integration test workflow validating routing to each agent type (local, qwen, codex, claude) sequentially and in parallel via agent variable binding

nodes.name: route-to-local
nodes.summary: Baseline routing test to local agent verifying always-available direct dispatch

nodes.name: route-to-qwen
nodes.summary: Tests routing to qwen agent with documented fallback to local if qwen not available

nodes.name: route-to-codex
nodes.summary: Tests routing to codex agent with documented fallback to local if codex not available

nodes.name: route-to-claude
nodes.summary: Tests routing to claude agent with documented fallback to local if claude not available

nodes.name: parallel-route-implementer
nodes.summary: Parallel routing test dispatching to implementer agent variable after sequential routing baseline passes

nodes.name: memory-integration-test
nodes.summary: Integration test workflow validating each memory layer (L0-L3) individually, combined layer loading, agent-level isolation modes, and diary-only access

nodes.name: test-l0-l1
nodes.summary: Tests loading of L0 (session) and L1 (ephemeral) memory layers with architecture topic filter; max 300 tokens

nodes.name: test-l2
nodes.summary: Tests loading of L2 (persistent project) memory layer with architecture and patterns topics; max 500 tokens

nodes.name: test-l3
nodes.summary: Tests loading of L3 (global cross-project) memory layer with architecture topic; max 200 tokens

nodes.name: test-all-layers
nodes.summary: Tests simultaneous loading of all memory layers L0-L3 with agent-level isolation and architecture/patterns/best-practices topics; max 1000 tokens

nodes.name: test-isolation
nodes.summary: Tests agent-level memory isolation mode across L0-L2 layers verifying scope boundaries are respected

nodes.name: test-diary-only
nodes.summary: Tests diary-only memory mode that restricts loading to diary entries only from L0-L1; max 200 tokens

nodes.name: bug-fix
nodes.summary: Multi-agent workflow template for diagnosing, fixing, and validating bug resolution. Orchestrates qwen investigator/fixer and codex reviewer across 6 sequential steps with conditional regression test generation.

nodes.name: ci-cd-setup
nodes.summary: Multi-agent workflow for setting up CI/CD pipelines across GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, and CircleCI. Nine steps covering analysis, design, implementation of build/test/deployment stages, quality gates, and documentation.

nodes.name: code-review
nodes.summary: Automated and AI-driven code review workflow with configurable depth. Runs parallel quality, security, and optional performance analysis then synthesizes into a prioritized decision.

nodes.name: dependency-update
nodes.summary: Safe dependency update workflow with incremental application, compatibility testing, and rollback support. Handles patch/minor/major/security update types with conditional auto-merge.

nodes.name: documentation
nodes.summary: Documentation generation workflow supporting API, user guide, architecture, and readme types. Loops over sections, conditionally adds code examples, and iterates on review feedback.

nodes.name: performance-optimization
nodes.summary: Performance bottleneck identification and optimization workflow. Establishes baseline metrics, profiles hot paths, iteratively applies optimizations, measures improvements, and validates correctness with rollback support.

nodes.name: refactoring
nodes.summary: Safe refactoring workflow with test preservation and incremental execution. Analyzes current code metrics, plans steps, executes refactoring loop, validates results, and reverts if goals are unmet.

nodes.name: security-audit
nodes.summary: Comprehensive security audit and vulnerability assessment workflow. Builds threat model, runs parallel code scan, dependency scan, config audit, and access control review, then prioritizes findings and creates remediation plan.

nodes.name: testing
nodes.summary: Comprehensive testing workflow supporting unit and integration test generation, execution, and coverage verification. Includes failure fix loops and configurable coverage targets.

nodes.name: investigate
nodes.summary: Root cause analysis step using qwen investigator. Identifies root cause, creates reproduction steps, and lists affected components from bug description and error details.

nodes.name: fix
nodes.summary: Develops minimal targeted fix using qwen fixer. Requires investigate output; produces fix description, changed files, and code changes following existing patterns.

nodes.name: test
nodes.summary: Conditional regression test creation step. Runs only when add_regression_test is true; creates test reproducing original bug scenario with up to 3 retry attempts.

nodes.name: verify
nodes.summary: Runs tests and validates fix correctness, checking for side effects. Depends on both fix and test steps; retries up to 2 times on verification failure.

nodes.name: review
nodes.summary: Codex-agent quality and correctness review of the complete bug fix. Checks fix correctness, test coverage, code quality, and potential side effects; produces approve/needs_revision decision.

nodes.name: revise
nodes.summary: Conditional revision step triggered when review decision is needs_revision. Addresses review feedback and loops back to verify.

nodes.name: analyze
nodes.summary: Analyzes CI/CD requirements for given project type and platform. Identifies build steps, test requirements, deployment strategies, env variables, secrets management, and artifact storage needs.

nodes.name: design
nodes.summary: Designs nine pipeline stages with parallel execution, caching, fail-fast, and rollback strategies. Produces pipeline_design, stages list, and optimization strategies.

nodes.name: build_stage
nodes.summary: Implements build stage configuration including env setup, dependency caching, build commands, artifact generation, and optional build matrix.

nodes.name: test_stage
nodes.summary: Configures test stage with parallel test runners, coverage reporting, result artifact storage, and failure notifications.

nodes.name: deployment_stages
nodes.summary: Loop-based deployment configuration iterating over all target environments. Configures triggers, env-specific variables, deployment strategy (blue-green/rolling/canary), health checks, and rollback automation.

nodes.name: quality_gates
nodes.summary: Adds security and quality gates: dependency vulnerability scanning, SAST, code quality checks, license compliance, branch protection, and required approval rules.

nodes.name: create_config
nodes.summary: Generates the complete pipeline configuration file by aggregating build, test, deployment, and quality gate outputs into platform-specific YAML/JSON.

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Codex-agent validation of generated pipeline configuration. Checks syntax, stage dependencies, secret definitions, deployment safety, best practices, and absence of hardcoded credentials.

nodes.name: document
nodes.summary: Conditional documentation step (only when validation passes). Creates pipeline overview, stage descriptions, env variable guide, secrets setup, deployment process, troubleshooting, and rollback procedures.

nodes.name: automated_checks
nodes.summary: Runs automated code quality checks on changed files: syntax validation, linting, formatting, import organization, and complexity metrics.

nodes.name: quality_review
nodes.summary: Reviews code clarity, naming conventions, documentation, error handling, test coverage, and design pattern adherence. Uses L0/L1/L2 memory layers for pattern context.

nodes.name: security_review
nodes.summary: Codex security scan for SQL injection, XSS, auth issues, hardcoded credentials, input validation gaps, and insecure dependencies. Produces risk_level output.

nodes.name: performance_analysis
nodes.summary: Conditional performance analysis (thorough depth only). Examines algorithm complexity, DB query efficiency, memory patterns, network calls, and caching opportunities.

nodes.name: synthesize
nodes.summary: Aggregates quality, security, and performance findings into a prioritized review summary. Classifies blocking vs non-blocking issues and produces an approval decision.

nodes.name: analyze
nodes.summary: Scans project dependencies for available updates, breaking changes, security vulnerabilities, deprecations, and changelogs. Produces update_plan.

nodes.name: prioritize
nodes.summary: Orders dependency updates by priority: critical security fixes first, then security updates, major, minor, and patch. Produces an ordered update_sequence.

nodes.name: update
nodes.summary: Applies dependency updates incrementally in a loop (max 50 iterations). Updates one dependency at a time for easier rollback, tracking completed updates in state.

nodes.name: test
nodes.summary: Runs comprehensive compatibility tests after updates: unit, integration, build, type checking, linting, and runtime compatibility. Retries up to 2 times on failure.

nodes.name: fix_failures
nodes.summary: Conditional loop that fixes compatibility failures by patching code, pinning versions, or finding alternatives. Loops back to test step until all tests pass or rollback is needed.

nodes.name: security_check
nodes.summary: Post-update security validation ensuring no new CVEs were introduced, all security patches applied, license compliance maintained, and no malicious packages added.

nodes.name: review
nodes.summary: Codex review of dependency changes (conditional on auto_merge=false). Verifies updates are appropriate, documentation updated, and changelogs reviewed.

nodes.name: rollback
nodes.summary: Conditional rollback of problematic updates when fix_failures determines rollback is needed. Preserves successful updates while reverting failures.

nodes.name: analyze
nodes.summary: Analyzes target component to extract public interfaces, key features, configuration options, dependencies, and usage patterns for the requested documentation type.

nodes.name: outline
nodes.summary: Creates structured documentation outline with logical flow, necessary sections, and code example placeholders based on component analysis.

nodes.name: write
nodes.summary: Iteratively writes each documentation section in a loop (max 20 iterations). Produces clear explanations, code examples, best practices, and pitfall warnings.

nodes.name: examples
nodes.summary: Conditional step creating complete, runnable code examples for common use cases with error handling and best practices when include_examples is true.

nodes.name: review
nodes.summary: Codex review for technical accuracy, clarity, completeness, formatting, working examples, and grammar. Produces approve/needs_revision decision with feedback.

nodes.name: revise
nodes.summary: Conditional revision loop addressing review feedback and issue list. Loops back to review until documentation is approved.

nodes.name: baseline
nodes.summary: Establishes performance baseline by measuring response times, throughput, resource usage (CPU/memory/IO), DB query times, and network calls.

nodes.name: profile
nodes.summary: Conditional profiling step (when profiling_enabled=true). Identifies hot code paths, slow queries, inefficient algorithms, resource leaks, and network bottlenecks.

nodes.name: prioritize
nodes.summary: Ranks optimization opportunities by impact on performance goal, implementation effort, risk, and dependencies. Produces ordered optimization_plan.

nodes.name: optimize
nodes.summary: Iterative optimization loop (max 10) applying algorithm improvements, caching, query optimization, lazy loading, parallel processing, and resource pooling until goal is met.

nodes.name: measure
nodes.summary: Measures post-optimization performance and compares against baseline. Calculates improvement percentages and determines whether the performance goal was achieved.

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Codex-agent validation that optimizations did not break functionality. Verifies all tests pass, functionality preserved, no new bugs, edge cases handled. Retries up to 2 times.

nodes.name: rollback
nodes.summary: Conditional rollback when validation fails. Determines which optimization caused breakage, identifies safe optimizations to keep, and produces a rollback plan.

nodes.name: analyze
nodes.summary: Analyzes code to be refactored: measures complexity/coupling/cohesion, assesses test coverage, maps dependencies, and identifies refactoring opportunities.

nodes.name: plan
nodes.summary: Creates safe refactoring strategy breaking work into small testable steps that preserve functionality, maintain test coverage, and minimize risk.

nodes.name: refactor
nodes.summary: Executes refactoring steps in a loop (max 15). After each step verifies tests pass, commits incrementally. Uses fresh_context=false to maintain state. Retries up to 2 times.

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Validates refactored code: all tests pass, functionality preserved, goal achieved, code metrics improved, no new bugs. Retries up to 2 times on failure.

nodes.name: compare
nodes.summary: Compares before/after code metrics to determine if refactoring achieved its goal and whether code is more maintainable. Produces goals_met boolean.

nodes.name: revert
nodes.summary: Conditional step when goals_met=false. Analyzes what went wrong and recommends an alternative refactoring approach or accepts current code state.

nodes.name: threat_model
nodes.summary: Creates threat model identifying attack surfaces, trust boundaries, sensitive data flows, auth points, external interfaces, and threat actor scenarios.

nodes.name: code_scan
nodes.summary: Scans code for OWASP vulnerabilities: SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, auth bypasses, authorization flaws, insecure crypto, hardcoded secrets, deserialization, path traversal, command injection.

nodes.name: dependency_scan
nodes.summary: Conditional dependency vulnerability scan checking outdated packages with CVEs, vulnerable transitive dependencies, license compliance, and supply chain risks.

nodes.name: config_audit
nodes.summary: Audits security configurations: HTTPS/TLS settings, CORS policies, security headers, cookie security, session management, rate limiting, input validation, and error handling.

nodes.name: access_control
nodes.summary: Reviews access control mechanisms: authentication strength, authorization checks, least privilege, RBAC, API endpoint protection, and resource isolation.

nodes.name: prioritize
nodes.summary: Aggregates findings from code scan, dependency scan, config audit, and access control review. Ranks by CVSS score, exploitability, impact, and remediation ease.

nodes.name: remediation_plan
nodes.summary: Creates remediation plan for critical and high-risk findings with recommended fixes, implementation steps, testing requirements, timeline estimates, and interim workarounds.

nodes.name: design
nodes.summary: Analyzes code and designs comprehensive test plan with test cases, edge/boundary conditions, mock/stub requirements, and test data needs.

nodes.name: unit_tests
nodes.summary: Conditional unit test implementation (when 'unit' in test_types). Creates isolated, fast tests with appropriate mocking covering happy paths and edge cases. Retries up to 3 times.

nodes.name: integration_tests
nodes.summary: Conditional integration test implementation. Tests component interactions with realistic data, end-to-end flows, and proper setup/teardown. Retries up to 3 times.

nodes.name: run_tests
nodes.summary: Executes all created tests and collects pass/fail status, coverage metrics, performance metrics, and failure reasons. Retries up to 2 times on execution failure.

nodes.name: fix_failures
nodes.summary: Conditional loop fixing failing tests (max 10 iterations). Root-cause analysis per failing test, loops back to run_tests until all pass.

nodes.name: verify_coverage
nodes.summary: Verifies test coverage meets configured target percentage. Identifies uncovered code paths and suggests additional test cases when coverage is insufficient.

nodes.name: 3p-updates.md
nodes.summary: Agent skill example defining the 3P update format (Progress, Plans, Problems) — a weekly status template for executive and team audiences. Covers data-gathering from Slack/Drive/Email/Calendar, strict formatting rules (emoji + team + date + 3 sentences per section), and a 4-step workflow.

nodes.name: Instructions
nodes.summary: Core 3P update instructions: defines Progress/Plans/Problems sections, audience scope (team to company level), time period (weekly), granularity scaling by team size, and requirement to confirm team name before drafting.

nodes.name: Tools Available
nodes.summary: Data-gathering guidance for 3P updates: Slack (high-reaction posts), Google Drive (high-view docs from key members), Email (high-response threads), Calendar (non-recurring important meetings). Maps each source to the appropriate 3P time window (past week vs. next week).

nodes.name: company-newsletter.md
nodes.summary: Agent skill example for authoring a company-wide newsletter (1000+ person org). Specifies 20-25 bullet structure broken into thematic sections (Announcements, Progress, Leadership, Social), tool usage priorities (Slack/Email/Calendar/Docs/Press), and an emoji-prefixed section format for Slack/email delivery.

nodes.name: Tools to use
nodes.summary: Newsletter data-sourcing: Slack (high-reaction/response messages in large channels), Email (executive company-wide announcements), Calendar (large-attendee events, All-Hands, attached docs), Documents (high-attention new docs from executives), External press references.

nodes.name: Example Formats
nodes.summary: Concrete newsletter template with four emoji-headed sections: Company Announcements, Progress on Priorities (nested sub-areas), Leadership Updates, and Social Updates. Defines bullet hierarchy and emoji usage convention.

nodes.name: faq-answers.md
nodes.summary: Agent skill example for surfacing and answering company-wide FAQs. Instructs agent to mine Slack/Email/Docs for high-engagement questions across the whole employee base (not team-specific), then produce concise *Question*/*Answer* pairs grounded in official communications.

nodes.name: general-comms.md
nodes.summary: Catch-all internal communication template for formats that don't fit 3P, newsletter, or FAQ patterns. Requires agent to clarify audience, purpose, tone, and formatting before drafting, then apply general writing principles (active voice, most-important-first, relevant links).

nodes.name: evaluation.md
nodes.summary: MCP Server Evaluation Guide: comprehensive reference for creating, running, and troubleshooting evaluations (evals) for MCP servers. Covers question/answer guidelines, 5-step evaluation process, output format (tool_calls/expected_response/grading_notes), YAML eval file format, CLI execution against STDIO/SSE/HTTP transports, and troubleshooting common issues.

nodes.name: mcp_best_practices.md
nodes.summary: MCP Server Best Practices: canonical patterns for server/tool naming (kebab-case, snake_case verb_noun), response formats (JSON/markdown), cursor-based pagination, transport selection (Streamable HTTP vs stdio), security (auth, input validation, DNS rebinding), tool annotations, error handling, testing requirements, and documentation standards.

nodes.name: node_mcp_server.md
nodes.summary: Node/TypeScript MCP Server Implementation Guide: full reference for building MCP servers with the TypeScript SDK. Covers tool registration via setRequestHandler with ListToolsRequestSchema/CallToolRequestSchema, Zod schemas for input validation, cursor-based pagination, error handling, async/await patterns, package.json and tsconfig.json configuration, advanced features (resources, SSE transport, notifications), and quality checklists.

nodes.name: python_mcp_server.md
nodes.summary: Python MCP Server Implementation Guide: full reference for building MCP servers with FastMCP. Covers @mcp.tool() decorator-based registration, Pydantic v2 schemas for input validation, cursor-based pagination, error handling, async/await patterns, context parameter injection, @mcp.resource() registration, lifespan management, transport options (stdio/Streamable HTTP), and quality checklists.

nodes.name: Evaluation Requirements
nodes.summary: Mandatory requirements for MCP evaluations: minimum 10 questions across diverse tool calls, graded answers with verification steps, full tool coverage, structured output with tool_calls/expected_response/grading_notes fields, and acceptable output formats.

nodes.name: Question Guidelines
nodes.summary: Guidelines for writing quality evaluation questions: core requirements (diversity, all-tool coverage, natural language phrasing), complexity tiers, tool testing patterns (chaining, edge cases, error conditions), and stability criteria for deterministic outputs.

nodes.name: Answer Guidelines
nodes.summary: Guidelines for writing evaluation answers: verification procedures, readable output formats (markdown/JSON), stability requirements (no timestamps or random IDs in expected responses), and diversity across tool types and response shapes.

nodes.name: Evaluation Process
nodes.summary: 5-step process for creating MCP evaluations: (1) documentation inspection, (2) tool inspection via list_tools, (3) developing conceptual understanding, (4) read-only content inspection, (5) task generation and finalization.

nodes.name: Running Evaluations
nodes.summary: Execution guide for running MCP evals: environment setup, YAML eval file format, running against STDIO/SSE/Streamable HTTP transports, command-line options (--model, --output, --verbose), interpreting pass/fail output, saving reports to file, and a complete end-to-end example workflow.

nodes.name: Troubleshooting
nodes.summary: Troubleshooting guide for evaluation failures: connection errors (server not running, wrong transport), low accuracy (ambiguous questions, non-deterministic tools), and timeout issues (increase --timeout, optimize slow tools).

nodes.name: Server Naming Conventions
nodes.summary: Canonical MCP server naming: domain-based (company-service pattern), kebab-case, max 3 words, version suffix for breaking changes. Examples and anti-patterns included.

nodes.name: Tool Naming and Design
nodes.summary: Tool naming: snake_case verb_noun pattern (get_user, create_issue). Tool design principles: single responsibility, exhaustive descriptions that guide LLM usage, strict parameter validation, avoid side effects in read-only tools, use tool annotations (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint).

nodes.name: Security Best Practices
nodes.summary: Security patterns for MCP servers: authentication/authorization at tool level, input validation (sanitize, bounds-check, path traversal prevention), error handling without stack trace leakage, DNS rebinding protection (Host header validation) for HTTP transports.

nodes.name: Transport Options
nodes.summary: Transport selection guide: Streamable HTTP for remote/production deployments (session management, stateless mode), stdio for local integrations (direct process communication). Includes session management considerations and when to use each.

nodes.name: Error Handling
nodes.summary: Error handling patterns: return structured errors within tool response content (isError: true), provide actionable error messages for LLM consumption, log internally without exposing stack traces to clients, distinguish user errors from system errors.

nodes.name: Tool Implementation
nodes.summary: Core tool implementation patterns for TypeScript MCP servers: snake_case tool naming, setRequestHandler for ListToolsRequestSchema and CallToolRequestSchema, argument validation at handler entry, response construction with content array, isError flag for tool-level errors.

nodes.name: Zod Schemas for Input Validation
nodes.summary: Zod schema patterns for TypeScript MCP input validation: z.object() schema definition, .parse()/.safeParse() patterns, converting to JSON Schema via zodToJsonSchema() for tool inputSchema registration, handling optional/required fields.

nodes.name: Pagination Implementation
nodes.summary: Cursor-based pagination for TypeScript MCP servers: nextCursor/cursor parameter pattern, page size limits, opaque cursor encoding (base64/JSON), returning hasMore indicator, client-side cursor threading across calls.

nodes.name: Advanced MCP Features
nodes.summary: Advanced Node MCP features: resource registration with URI templates and MIME types, Streamable HTTP transport setup with Express, stdio transport for local integrations, server-sent notifications via server.notification() for progress/change events.

nodes.name: Complete Example
nodes.summary: Full working TypeScript MCP server implementation: imports, server initialization with name/version, tool listing handler, tool call handler with switch-case dispatch, async external API calls, error handling with try/catch, stdio transport connection.

nodes.name: Package Configuration
nodes.summary: package.json and tsconfig.json configuration for TypeScript MCP servers: required dependencies (@modelcontextprotocol/sdk, zod, zod-to-json-schema), ESM module type, build/start scripts, TypeScript strict mode, ES2022 target, NodeNext module resolution.

nodes.name: MCP Python SDK and FastMCP
nodes.summary: Introduction to the FastMCP framework for Python MCP servers: decorator-based @mcp.tool() registration, automatic JSON schema generation from Python type hints and docstrings, comparison to low-level MCP SDK, installation requirements.

nodes.name: Tool Implementation
nodes.summary: Tool implementation with FastMCP: @mcp.tool() decorator, type-annotated parameters for schema generation, docstrings as tool descriptions, Pydantic BaseModel for complex input objects, async function patterns, returning str/dict/list results.

nodes.name: Pydantic v2 Key Features
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 patterns used in Python MCP servers: BaseModel with Field(description=, ge=, le=), @model_validator for cross-field validation, @computed_field for derived properties, integration with FastMCP for automatic JSON schema generation in tool inputSchema.

nodes.name: Advanced FastMCP Features
nodes.summary: Advanced FastMCP patterns: mcp.Context parameter injection for logging/progress reporting, @mcp.resource() registration with URI templates and MIME types, structured output types with TypedDict, @asynccontextmanager lifespan management for startup/shutdown, stdio and Streamable HTTP transport configuration.

nodes.name: Complete Example
nodes.summary: Full working Python FastMCP server implementation: FastMCP() initialization, multiple @mcp.tool() decorated async functions, Pydantic model inputs, error handling with McpError, pagination pattern, and mcp.run() with stdio transport.

nodes.name: Quality Checklist
nodes.summary: Quality checklist for Python MCP server implementations: strategic design (tool coverage, naming), implementation quality (async patterns, error handling), tool configuration (descriptions, Pydantic models), advanced features applicability, and code quality (type hints, docstrings, testing).

nodes.name: LICENSE.txt
nodes.summary: License file for the archived PDF skill package (agent-onboarding-package v2.0.0). Governs usage terms for all files in the pdf skill directory.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: PDF Processing Guide skill — primary entry point. Covers pypdf (merge/split/rotate/metadata), pdfplumber (text + table extraction), reportlab (PDF creation), and CLI tools (pdftotext, qpdf, pdftk). Includes OCR for scanned PDFs, watermarking, image extraction, and password protection patterns. 27 sections, 294 lines.

nodes.name: forms.md
nodes.summary: PDF form filling guide — distinguishes fillable vs non-fillable fields, mandates visual analysis before automation, covers fields.json creation, bounding box validation (automated intersection check + manual image inspection), and annotation injection workflow. 8 sections, 205 lines.

nodes.name: reference.md
nodes.summary: Advanced PDF processing reference — pypdfium2 (render/text), pdf-lib and pdfjs-dist JavaScript libraries, advanced poppler-utils and qpdf CLI operations, advanced pdfplumber/reportlab Python techniques, batch processing with error handling, image/figure extraction, performance optimization tips, and troubleshooting for encrypted/corrupted PDFs. 46 sections, 612 lines.

nodes.name: LICENSE.txt
nodes.summary: MIT License for the archived pptx skill package (agent-onboarding-package v2.0.0). Grants permission to use, copy, modify, and distribute the software without restriction.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Primary skill definition for AI agent PowerPoint creation and editing. Covers design principles, color palettes, layout workflows for creating PPTX files from scratch, from templates, and via editing. Also documents thumbnail grids, slide-to-image conversion, and Python-pptx dependencies.

nodes.name: Reading and analyzing content
nodes.summary: Describes text extraction and raw XML access workflows for reading existing PPTX files. Covers unzip-based inspection of pptx/slides/*.xml, key file structures (presentation.xml, slideMaster, slideLayout), and typography/color extraction techniques.

nodes.name: Creating a new PowerPoint presentation without a template
nodes.summary: Design principles and full workflow for generating PPTX files from scratch using python-pptx. Covers color palette selection (primary, secondary, accent, neutral, background), visual detail options (gradients, borders, icons, backgrounds), layout tips, and step-by-step creation workflow.

nodes.name: Editing an existing PowerPoint presentation
nodes.summary: Workflow for modifying an existing PPTX: read/analyze existing styles → plan changes → modify XML or python-pptx API → validate → save.

nodes.name: Creating a new PowerPoint presentation using a template
nodes.summary: Long workflow (~225 lines) for template-based PPTX generation. Covers reading template structure, preserving layouts and masters, populating placeholders, adding slides from slide layouts, and validation checklist. Includes extensive python-pptx code examples.

nodes.name: Creating Thumbnail Grids
nodes.summary: Technique for arranging slide thumbnails in a grid layout within a PPTX slide. Uses python-pptx to add image placeholders with precise positioning math for rows and columns.

nodes.name: Converting Slides to Images
nodes.summary: Documents LibreOffice headless CLI method for converting PPTX slides to PNG/JPG images. Command: `libreoffice --headless --convert-to png --outdir ...`. Covers output naming conventions and batch conversion.

nodes.name: Dependencies
nodes.summary: Lists required Python packages: python-pptx (primary), Pillow (image handling), lxml (XML manipulation). Also documents LibreOffice as optional system dependency for slide-to-image conversion.

nodes.name: html2pptx.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive reference guide for converting HTML to PowerPoint slides using both the html2pptx library and PptxGenJS. Covers HTML slide authoring, layout dimensions, supported elements, styling rules, icons/gradients, API reference, and JavaScript-based chart/table/text generation.

nodes.name: Creating HTML Slides
nodes.summary: Rules and examples for authoring HTML that will be converted to PPTX slides. Covers layout dimensions (10in × 7.5in), supported HTML elements, critical text rules, CSS styling, DIV shape styling, icons, gradients, and a full slide example.

nodes.name: Using the html2pptx Library
nodes.summary: Python API reference for html2pptx library. Documents dependencies, basic usage pattern, full function signature with parameters, return values, validation behavior, placeholder handling, and a complete end-to-end code example.

nodes.name: Using PptxGenJS
nodes.summary: JavaScript-based PPTX generation reference using PptxGenJS. Covers critical color rules, adding images, text, shapes, charts (scatter/line/pie/multi-series), chart color configuration, and tables (basic, custom formatting, merged cells, table options).

nodes.name: ooxml.md
nodes.summary: Technical reference for Office Open XML (OOXML) as used in PowerPoint files. Covers schema compliance, presentation structure, XML snippets for slides/text/shapes/images/tables/layouts, file update procedures, slide operations (add/duplicate/reorder/delete), common errors, and validation checklists.

nodes.name: Presentation Structure
nodes.summary: Core OOXML XML snippets for slide structure, text boxes, text formatting (bold/italic/underline/color/size/font), bullet lists, shapes (rect/ellipse/triangle), images (blipFill, rId references), tables (tbl/tr/tc), and slide layouts. Each element shown with complete minimal XML example.

nodes.name: File Updates
nodes.summary: Procedures for updating PPTX files by directly modifying OOXML: unzip, edit XML files, re-zip, rename to .pptx. Covers relationship file updates (_rels/), content_types updates, and Python zipfile module usage pattern.

nodes.name: Slide Operations
nodes.summary: Reference for OOXML-level slide operations: adding new slides (create slide XML, register in presentation.xml sldIdLst, add relationship), duplicating slides (copy XML + rels), reordering (manipulate sldIdLst order), and deleting slides (remove from sldIdLst + delete file).

nodes.name: Validation Checklist for Template-Based Presentations
nodes.summary: Pre-pack validation checklist: verify all rId references exist in .rels files, check content_types for new slide parts, confirm no duplicate sldId values, validate namespace declarations. Documents common template duplication pitfalls like stale rIds and missing media entries.

nodes.name: dml-chart.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML chart schema. Defines XML types and elements for chart objects (bar, line, pie, scatter, etc.) embedded in OOXML documents. Imports dml-main and shared-commonSimpleTypes.

nodes.name: dml-chartDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML chart-drawing schema. Defines drawing-space elements (absolute/relative anchors) used within chart documents. Imports dml-main and dml-chart.

nodes.name: dml-diagram.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML SmartArt diagram schema. Defines data model, layout, style, and color types for SmartArt graphics. Imports dml-main and shared-commonSimpleTypes.

nodes.name: dml-lockedCanvas.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML locked-canvas schema. Minimal schema (11 lines) defining the lockedCanvas root element that wraps drawing content to prevent editing. Imports dml-main.

nodes.name: dml-main.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML main schema (3081 lines). Core foundation for all DrawingML namespaces. Defines shapes, themes, effects, fills, text bodies, transforms, geometry, and connector types shared across Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

nodes.name: dml-picture.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML picture schema. Minimal schema (23 lines) defining the pic:pic element for inline images and blipFill references. Imports dml-main.

nodes.name: dml-spreadsheetDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML spreadsheet-drawing schema. Defines one-cell and two-cell anchor types for positioning drawing objects in spreadsheet cells. Imports dml-main and dml-picture.

nodes.name: dml-wordprocessingDrawing.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 DrawingML word-processing drawing schema. Defines inline and anchor elements for embedding drawing objects within word-processor paragraphs and pages. Imports dml-main and dml-picture.

nodes.name: pml.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 Presentation Markup Language schema (1676 lines). Defines presentations, slide masters, slide layouts, slides, notes, transitions, and animations. Primary schema for PowerPoint (.pptx) documents.

nodes.name: shared-additionalCharacteristics.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 shared additional-characteristics schema. Small schema (28 lines) for declaring application-specific document capabilities and constraints as key-value characteristics.

nodes.name: shared-bibliography.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 shared bibliography schema. Defines bibliographic source types (book, journal article, website, patent, etc.) and citation fields for the Office Bibliography feature. Imports shared-commonSimpleTypes.

nodes.name: shared-commonSimpleTypes.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 shared common simple types schema. Defines fundamental reusable simple types (percentages, angles, language IDs, hex colors, EMU measurements) used across all OOXML schemas.

nodes.name: shared-customXmlDataProperties.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 shared custom XML data properties schema. Small schema (25 lines) for associating custom XML data stores with document content via dataBinding elements.

nodes.name: shared-customXmlSchemaProperties.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 shared custom XML schema properties. Minimal schema (18 lines) for referencing external XML schema files attached to a document for content validation.

nodes.name: shared-documentPropertiesCustom.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 shared custom document properties schema. Defines the docProps/custom.xml part for user-defined metadata properties stored as typed variant values. Imports shared-documentPropertiesVariantTypes.

nodes.name: shared-documentPropertiesExtended.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 shared extended document properties schema. Defines docProps/app.xml part with application name, version, page count, word count, slide count, and heading pairs. Imports shared-documentPropertiesVariantTypes.

nodes.name: shared-documentPropertiesVariantTypes.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 shared variant types schema. Defines the vt:variant union type and subtypes (bool, i4, r8, lpstr, lpwstr, date, vector, array, etc.) used by custom and extended document property values.

nodes.name: shared-math.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 shared math schema (582 lines). Defines Office Math Markup Language (OMML) elements: fractions, radicals, integrals, matrices, sub/superscripts, accents, and math run properties.

nodes.name: shared-relationshipReference.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 shared relationship reference schema. Small schema (25 lines) defining the r:id attribute type for referencing OPC relationships between document parts.

nodes.name: sml.xsd
nodes.summary: ISO/IEC 29500-4 Spreadsheet Markup Language schema (4439 lines). Largest schema in the set. Defines worksheets, workbooks, cells, formulas, pivot tables, conditional formatting, tables, defined names, and chart sheets for Excel (.xlsx) documents.
